title: Method and apparatus for the purification of hydrogen
abstract:
Hydrogen gas from an generator (1) is occluded in a hydrogen
recovery container (20), thereafter, in the initial stage during
which hydrogen gas is released from the hydrogen recovery container
(20), initially purged hydrogen gas containing much amount of impure
gas is introduced from the hydrogen recovery container (20) to a gas
tank (2) and saved therein, then, hydrogen gas having high purity
released from a hydrogen absorbing alloy in the hydrogen recovery
container (20) is refluxed to the generator (1), and this process
comprising absorbing and releasing of hydrogen gas in the hydrogen
recovery container (20) and saving in the gas tank (2) is repeated
once or several times, successively, the hydrogen gas containing much
amount of impure gas saved in the gas tank (2) is introduced in the
hydrogen recovery container (20) and occluded therein, thereafter,
in the initial stage during which hydrogen gas is released from the
hydrogen recovery container (20), the hydrogen gas containing much
amount of impure gas is discharged outside, then hydrogen gas having
high purity released from the hydrogen absorbing alloy in the
hydrogen recovery container (20) is refluxed to the generator (1).

title: Manufacture of alkaline storage battery and hydrogen storage alloy for alkaline storage battery
abstract: PURPOSE:To simplify the production process and improve the storage characteristic by using a hydrogen storage alloy capable of reversibly storing or releasing hydrogen for a negative electrode. CONSTITUTION:A hydrogen storage alloy made of a metal compound phase capable of reversibly storing or releasing hydrogen and a pure metal phase of an additive metal having the potential lower than the standard hydrogen generating potential in an alkaline solution or its solid solution phase is used for a negative electrode. The metal compound has an AB type hexagonal structure, where A is at least one kind selected among rare earth elements or Ca and B is at least one kind selected among the first-class transition metals. The production process of the hydrogen storage alloy capable of suppressing the self-discharge is simplified, the homogeneous hydrogen storage alloy is used for the negative electrode, thereby the storage characteristic of an alkaline storage battery is improved.

title: System and method for producing hydrogen and methane
abstract:
The present invention relates to a hydrogen and methane integrated production system and integrated production method, comprising: a first waste storage tank for storing a first organic waste capable of abnormal fermentation; A second waste storage tank for storing the second organic waste which can be fermented daily; A heat treatment tank receiving the first organic waste from the first waste storage tank and heat-treating to promote hydrolysis of the first organic waste and predominate hydrogen producing bacteria; A hydrogen fermentation tank for anaerobic fermenting the heat treated first organic waste supplied from the heat treatment tank to generate hydrogen gas; A hydrogen gas storage tank for storing hydrogen gas generated from the hydrogen fermentation tank; A pH supply device for supplying an alkaline solution to the hydrogen fermentation tank so as to promote the generation of hydrogen gas in the hydrogen fermentation tank and maintaining the pH of the first organic waste in an appropriate state; A methane fermentation tank for generating anaerobic fermentation of methane gas by anaerobic fermentation of the remaining organic wastes supplied from the hydrogen fermentation tank and the second organic wastes supplied from the second waste storage tank; It includes a methane gas storage tank for storing methane gas generated from the methane fermentation tank. 
     According to the present invention as described above, by using the organic waste, such as livestock manure and food waste, it is possible to maximize the energy yield by integrated production of hydrogen and methane.

According to the present invention as described above, by using the organic waste, such as livestock manure and food waste, it is possible to maximize the energy yield by integrated production of hydrogen and methane.

title: Hydrogen-producing cell comprising a cell of a high temperature steam electrolyzer
abstract: A hydrogen-producing cell comprising a cell of a high temperature steam electrolyzer or HTSE comprising a porous cathode ( 404 ) and a porous anode ( 402 ) on either side of a dense and gases-impervious anion conducting electrolyte ( 403 ), wherein said cell of the high temperature steam electrolyzer is directly coupled, in series, with a cell of an electrochemical pump comprising a porous anode ( 406 ) and a porous cathode ( 408 ) on either side of a dense and gases-impervious proton conducting electrolyte ( 407 ), at the cathode ( 404 ) of the cell of the high temperature steam electrolyzer and at the anode ( 406 ) of the electrochemical pump.

title: System for hydrogen storage and generation
abstract: The invention provides a solid phase hydrogen-generating system utilizing a solid chemical hydride fuel selected from the group consisting of sodium borohydride, lithium borohydride, magnesium hydride and calcium hydride, wherein the fuel is encapsulated in a plurality of removable capsules, the capsules being pumpable and having a major axis of up to 40 mm.

title: Hydrogen storage
abstract:
For economically storing relatively large quantities of 
hydrogen, a hydrogen storage material comprises (1) a major 
R₂Fe₁₇ phase wherein the R is lanthanum, cerium and, option­
ally, other rare earth(s) such as praesodymium and neodym­
ium, (2) an R′/R′Fe₂ eutectic where R′ is lanthanum-starved R, 
(3) an unalloyed lanthanum phase, and (4) an unalloyed iron 
phase. The material contains less than 55% by weight cerium 
based on the total weight of cerium, lanthanum and other rare 
earth(s). Such a material is produced by an homogenising heat 
treatment.
text: Hydrogen storage For economically storing relatively large quantities of hydrogen, a hydrogen storage material comprises (1) a major R₂Fe₁₇ phase wherein the R is lanthanum, cerium and, option­ ally, other rare earth(s) such as praesodymium and neodym­ ium, (2) an R′/R′Fe₂ eutectic where R′ is lanthanum-starved R, (3) an unalloyed lanthanum phase, and (4) an unalloyed iron phase. The material contains less than 55% by weight cerium based on the total weight of cerium, lanthanum and other rare earth(s). Such a material is produced by an homogenising heat treatment.

title: Magnesium-based hydrogen storage material
abstract: <P>PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a magnesium-based hydrogen storage material capable of storing and releasing hydrogen at a lower temperature.  <P>SOLUTION: The magnesium-based hydrogen storage material is made by mechanically crushing magnesium-based raw material consisting of a magnesium-based hydride and a nickel-containing substance for improving hydrogen storage and releasing characteristics. The magnesium-based hydrogen storage material is produced through high dispersion composite formation of particles made of the nickel-containing substance on particles of magnesium-based material being releasing base material that stores and releases hydrogen. Therein, the average particle size of the particles of the magnesium-based material in the magnesium-based hydrogen storage material is made to be ≥100 nm and ≤300 nm and the average particle size of the particles of the nickel-containing substance is made to be ≥10 nm and ≤100 nm. Further, the hydrogen releasing processing is performed at a temperature of ≥200°C and ≤350°C.  <P>COPYRIGHT: (C)2006,JPO&NCIPI

title: Air supply system used for fuel cell and capable of strengthening safety discharge of hydrogen gas
abstract: The invention discloses an air supply system used for a fuel cell and capable of strengthening safety discharge of hydrogen gas. The air supply system comprises an air supply machine, a humidifier, a main path regulating system, a branched path regulating system and a hydrogen dilution device, wherein the air supply machine conveys one part of ambient air into the fuel cell through the humidifier, the discharged air after reaction flows into the main path regulating system through the humidifier, the output of the main path regulating system is connected with the hydrogen dilution device, and the air supply machine conveys the other part of ambient air into the hydrogen dilution device through the branched path regulating system. The air supply system can simply and rapidly strengthen the safety discharge of the hydrogen gas, and is used for solving the problem that the discharge concentration of tail gas-hydrogen gas exceeds the standard and creating the condition for safety operation of the fuel cell.

title: Method for producing a preferential oxidation catalyst
abstract: Disclosed is a hydrogen generation process for use with fuel cells which includes a preferential oxidation step to reduce the concentration of carbon monoxide. The preferential oxidation step includes contacting a fuel stream comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide in the presence of an oxygen at a preferential oxidation temperature of between about 70° and about 160° C. with preferential oxidation catalyst for reducing the concentration of carbon monoxide to produce a treated fuel gas stream comprising less than about 50 ppm-vol carbon monoxide. The preferential oxidation catalyst comprises ruthenium metal dispersed on a shaped alumina carrier, wherein at least 60 percent of the ruthenium metal is present in a band extending from the surface towards the center and having a width of about 50 percent of the distance from the surface to the center of the shaped alumina carrier. Superior performance at low preferential oxidation temperatures below 130° C. was observed when the band comprised 50 percent of the alumina carrier and contained at least 60 percent of the ruthenium metal. The preferential oxidation catalyst may be employed to reduce carbon monoxide in fuel gas streams supplied to fuel cells wherein carbon monoxide will poison the active noble metal membrane and higher preferential oxidation temperatures may reduce the hydrogen content of the gas stream.

title: Preparation of titanium-manganese material for storing hydrogen
abstract:
PURPOSE: To suppress the evaporation and oxidation of Mn and improve the hydrogenating and occluding and releasing characteristics of hydrogen, by enclosing a Ti-Mn alloy in a heat-resistant inorganic material having a particle diameter of a specific value or below in heat-treating the Ti-Mn alloy.  
     CONSTITUTION: A Ti-Mn alloy 2 is introduced into a container 1 and enclosed with a heat-resistant inorganic material 3 having an average particle diameter of 4,000μm or less. The container 1 is then deaerated and heated at a high temperature while passing an inert gas containing hydrogen gas or the inert gas through the container 1.

title: Control method of air fuel ratio of hydrogen engine
abstract: PURPOSE: A control method of air fuel ratio of a hydrogen engine is provided to control the injection moment of hydrogen fuel at the excess section by controlling the injection time of the hydrogen fuel with considering the pressure difference considering the temperature of the hydrogen fuel at the excess section. CONSTITUTION: A control method of air fuel ratio of a hydrogen engine includes several steps of: measuring the temperature and the pressure of fuel and obtaining the difference between the mapped pressure of fuel according to an intake air amount and an engine RPM and the measured pressure(S20,S30); calculating an allowable error of the pressure difference(S40); outputting a fuel compensation control signal considering the mapping pressure and the pressure compensation map value of fuel when the pressure difference is over a limit value of the allowable error(S50,S60,S90); and outputting a compensation control signal of the injection time considering the fuel injection time and compensation map value of the fuel pressure when the pressure difference is within the range of the calculated allowable error(S70,S80,S90).

title: Vehicle
abstract: A hydrogen station includes gas storage equipment for storing hydrogen, a dispenser for charging hydrogen gas supplied from the gas storage equipment into a hydrogen tank of a vehicle, and a blower. When charging hydrogen, the blower blows air towards a radiator of the vehicle parked at a predetermined vehicle parking area of the hydrogen station. The current flow of the blower is adjusted in accordance with the heat load on the radiator.

When charging hydrogen, the blower blows air towards a radiator of the vehicle parked at a predetermined vehicle parking area of the hydrogen station. The current flow of the blower is adjusted in accordance with the heat load on the radiator.

title: Hydrogen generating fuel container
abstract:
The present invention relates to a hydrogen generating fuel storage tank. It has an upper chamber and a lower chamber partitioned up and down by a movable partition, the lower chamber has a fuel supply port for introducing external hydrogen generating fuel into the lower chamber, and the introduced hydrogen generating fuel to the outside of the lower chamber. A tank body having a fuel discharge port for discharging, the upper chamber including a waste fuel inlet for introducing external waste fuel into the upper chamber, and a waste fuel outlet for discharging the introduced waste fuel from the upper chamber to the outside; It characterized in that it comprises a level detector for sensing the amount of hydrogen-producing fuel contained in the lower chamber of the tank body and outputs the information. 
     Hydrogen fuel storage tank of the present invention made as described above, the partition is installed in the storage tank according to the amount of hydrogen-producing fuel which is a hydrogen-containing compound that generates hydrogen gas through a chemical reaction and the partition wall outside the storage tank By installing a level detector that detects and outputs the position of the fuel cell, it is possible to always check the amount of hydrogen-generated fuel in the storage tank, and by forming a discharge volume of a predetermined volume to accommodate the hydrogen-generated fuel at the bottom of the storage tank. Even if there is no supply of hydrogen-generated fuel in the state where the partition is located at the lowest point, the fuel is configured to be able to supply some fuel, so that fuel supply is not interrupted during continuous operation.

title: Special-shaped heat insulation supporting structure of horizontal liquid hydrogen container
abstract: The invention discloses a special-shaped heat insulation supporting structure of a horizontal liquid hydrogen container, which comprises: the inner end of the inner pipe is inserted into the inner end of the outer pipe, the inner end of the inner pipe and the inner end of the outer pipe are connected and fixed, the outer end of the inner pipe is used for being fixed with the end socket of the inner cylinder or the outer cylinder, and the outer end of the outer pipe is used for being fixed with the end socket of the cylinder which is not fixed with the inner pipe in the inner cylinder and the outer cylinder. The special-shaped heat insulation supporting structure is simple and convenient to manufacture, and can be inserted into the outer pipe through the inner pipe in the limited space between the inner cylinder and the outer cylinder and extend the heat conduction path by prolonging the connection length between the inner end of the inner pipe and the inner end of the outer pipe, so that the introduction amount of external heat in the inner cylinder can be greatly reduced, and the heat insulation efficiency of the liquid hydrogen vacuum heat insulation gas cylinder can be improved by more than 50%.

title: Nano magnesium-based composite hydrogen storage material and preparation method thereof
abstract: The invention relates to a nano magnesium-based composite hydrogen storage material and a preparation method thereof. The main component of the material is magnesium, and the material also comprises multiple catalysts of mixed rare earth, carbonyl nickel powder and graphite so as to improve the low-temperature hydrogen absorption performance of the material. The material has a nano-crystal structure, the grain size is 20-50 nanometers, and the material has excellent low-temperature hydrogen absorption dynamic performance. In the preparation method, firstly, pure magnesium and a certain amount of mixed rare earth are subjected to vacuum smelting by adopting a vacuum induction smelting method to prepare a brittle magnesium-rare earth alloy ingot with magnesium in-situ doped with rare earth elements; and then mixing the obtained alloy with carbonyl nickel powder, graphite powder and an inert organic grinding aid, and further preparing the high-capacity magnesium-based composite hydrogen storage material by a mechanical ball milling method. The preparation method of the material overcomes the wall adhesion phenomenon in the mechanical ball milling process of the magnesium-based hydrogen storage alloy, improves the recovery rate of the material, and obtains the high-capacity magnesium-based composite hydrogen storage material with excellent low-temperature hydrogen absorption performance.

title: Hydrogen storage alloy electrode and alkaline secondary cell using it
abstract: PURPOSE:To improve capability to consume oxygen gas generated at a positive electrode when a cell is made so as to suppress rise-up of the internal pressure of the cell by applying a water repelling process of high water repellent performance to a hydrogen storage alloy used as a negative electrode of an alkaline secondary cell. CONSTITUTION:The surface of a hydrogen storage alloy electrode is treated to be water repellent with isocyanate ester or the hydrogen storage alloy electrode is manufactured by the use of a hydrogen storage alloy whose particle surface is treated with isocyanate ester to be water repellent.

title: Hydrogen gas utilizing method
abstract: A hydrogen gas utilization method using an aromatic compound as a hydrogen storage / transport medium to provide a hydrogen gas utilization method that efficiently uses hydrogen by increasing the efficiency of hydrogenation and dehydrogenation reactions.   In one hydrogenation reactor, a hydrogen-containing gas and an aromatic compound are reacted in the presence of a catalyst, the hydrogen is reacted and stored in an aromatic compound to obtain a hydrogenated aromatic compound, and then the hydrogenated aromatic compound. title: Hydrogen fuel cell journey-increasing electric vehicle and display device thereof
abstract: The invention provides a hydrogen fuel cell journey-increasing electric vehicle, which comprises a vehicle controller, a power battery management system, a hydrogen fuel cell energy controller and a display device. The vehicle controller is used for obtaining status information and fault information of the vehicle; the power battery management system is used for obtaining status information and fault information of a power battery; the hydrogen fuel cell energy controller is used for obtaining status information and fault information of a hydrogen fuel supply device and a hydrogen fuel cell; the display device comprises a combination instrument and a display screen; the combination instrument and the display screen are used for respectively displaying the status information and fault information of the vehicle, the status information and fault information of the power battery and the status information and fault information of the hydrogen fuel supply device and the hydrogen fuel cell. By applying the hydrogen fuel cell journey-increasing electric vehicle, multiple key vehicle condition information and fault information can be displayed reasonably. The invention also provides the display device of the hydrogen fuel cell journey-increasing electric vehicle.

title: Alloy compositions for use as electrode materials and for hydrogen production
abstract: This invention provides novel metal alloys, methods for making these alloys, and methods of using these alloys in numerous applications. The alloys of the present invention comprise the following components: (A) one or more of the transition metal elements; at least one of either (B) aluminum or (C) one or more of the group 1A alkali metal elements; and (D) one or more elements and/or compounds having high mobility values for electrons. Thus, components A, D, and at least one of components B or C comprise the present invention. These alloys are useful as electrode materials in devices such as batteries, capacitors, fuel cells and similar devices, and useful in the direct production of hydrogen gas.

title: Hydrogen storage element and gaseous hydrogen detecting element
abstract:
PURPOSE: To prevent the release of a hydrogen occluding thin film due to the repeated occlusion and discharge of hydrogen by depositing a ductile metallic strip on at least a part of the hydrogen storage alloy thin film deposited on the surface of a substrate to cover the substrate and the thin film.  
     CONSTITUTION: The thin film 3 of the hydrogen storage alloy such as a lanthanum-nickel alloy is formed on the surface of the insulating substrate having a flat surface such as a glass substrate 1 by sputtering, vapor deposition, etc. A thin strip 4 consisting of a good-conductivity metal having ductility such as copper, gold, silver, and aluminum is deposited on both ends, central part, etc., of the thin film 3 in the longitudinal direction to cover the surface of the thin film 3 and the surface of the glass substrate 1 by sputtering, etc., and a hydrogen storage element is obtained. The electric conductivity of the element is increased when hydrogen is occluded, and decreased when hydrogen is discharged. As a result, the element can also be utilized as a gaseous hydrogen detecting element. 
     COPYRIGHT: (C)1989,JPO&Japio

title: Fuel cell module and fuel cell system
abstract: This fuel cell module (20) is provided with fuel cells (110), a dehydrogenation reaction unit (132) which performs a dehydrogenation reaction of a hydrogenated product of an aromatic compound, a hydrogen separation unit (134) which separates the hydrogen generated by the dehydrogenation reaction unit (132) from the dehydrogenated product of the hydrogenated product, a hydrogen supply unit (140) which supplies the hydrogen separated in the hydrogen separation unit (134) to the fuel cells (110), and a unit frame (100) that houses the fuel cells (110), the dehydrogenation reaction unit (132), the hydrogen separation unit (134), and the hydrogen supply unit (140). The dehydrogenation reaction unit (132) is heated by the heat generated in the fuel cells (110) and/or the heat generated by combustion of off-gas from the fuel cells (110).

title: Square type sealed alkaline battery using hydrogen storage alloy negative electrode
abstract: PURPOSE:To provide a square type sealed alkaline battery by which deformation of the battery can be prevented and which has excellent filtrate proof perfor mance by preventing rise in battery internal pressure when the battery is left as it is at a high temperature or when it is charged rapidly. CONSTITUTION:In a square type sealed alkaline battery formed by using a hydrogen storage alloy as a negative electrode, the hydrogen storage alloy has CaCu5 type structure and a hydrogen balanced pressure value when the PCT curve H/M=0.75 is equal to or less than 5kg/cm<2> at 45 deg.C as well as equal to or less than 5.0kg/cm<2> at 85 deg.C and a theoretical capacity of a negative electrode to a theoretical capacity of a positive electrode is equal to or more than 1.65 times. Thereby, when the battery is left as it is at a high temperature or even when it is charged rapidly, rise in battery internal pressure can be prevented, so that filtrate proof performance can be also improved.

title: Vehicle-mounted hydrogen supply system frame for hydrogen fuel logistics vehicle
abstract: The utility model belongs to the technical field of the fuel cell car technique and specifically relates to a hydrogen fuel logistics vehicle carries hydrogen supply system frame. The hydrogen supply system comprises a frame main body, wherein the lower part of the frame main body is a power assembly installation space, the upper part of the frame main body is a hydrogen supply system installation space, and the hydrogen supply system installation space is integrally distributed in an L shape. The utility model has light weight, can greatly reduce the total weight of the vehicle-mounted hydrogen supply system, and meanwhile, the installation space of the hydrogen supply system is integrally distributed in an L shape, thus reducing the space required by the vehicle-mounted hydrogen supply system and effectively reducing the gravity center of the vehicle-mounted hydrogen supply system; in addition, simultaneously, the top of frame main part reserves on-vehicle hydrogen storage bottle installation position, if the continuation of journey of the longer mileage of whole car demand, installs the 4 th on-vehicle hydrogen storage bottle additional at the on-vehicle hydrogen storage bottle installation position at top, reaches four bottles and organizes hydrogen system hydrogen supply ability to effectively increase hydrogen fuel storage capacity, improve the continuation of journey mileage.

title: Hydrogen generator for a fuel cell
abstract: A hydrogen generator includes a housing, a pellet strip with a plurality of pellets disposed on a flexible carrier, the pellets including a hydrogen containing material that will release hydrogen gas when heated. A feed system feeds the pellet strip to sequentially position one or more pellets in proximity to a heater that heats the pellets to release hydrogen gas. The pellet strip can be folded or wound on a reel, stored in a compartment in the hydrogen generator or in a user-replaceable container. The hydrogen generator can be part of a fuel cell system that includes the hydrogen generator and a fuel cell battery.

title: Hydrogen generator, Carbon dioxide and sulfate captor
abstract: This invention provides a means to produce on site Hydrogen to power fuel cells or Hydrogen engines storing the energy in Calcium metal. It continues by using the Calcium hydroxide byproduct of Calcium generated Hydrogen reaction to capture Carbon dioxide from exhaust situations, both moving and stationary as trucks and power plants. This reaction goes to completion with the sedimentation of Calcium carbonate, which may be useful to the cement industry as a component of their products, or it could be used to capture sulfates in stack gas. Either Calcium carbonate or Calcium sulfate may be electrolyzed to recover metallic Calcium, which is not readily available on the market at this time. Solar power, water wheels, resistive gym apparatus, wind power generation of electrical energy as well as unused power from power plants can electrolyze water into Hydrogen and Oxygen. The Hydrogen is passed through and over Calcium carbonate freeing the Calcium as pure metal and releasing as gases water and Carbon dioxide, which, if fed into a greenhouse, will make plants grow rapidly and during daylight, release heavily Oxygenated air from the greenhouse environment. Oxygen can be sold to health, scuba and industrial businesses. These reactions are commercially feasible making Hydrogen propulsion as with a Hydrogen piston engine and fuel cell driving for generating power safer because large amounts of Hydrogen do not have to be carried or on hand during use or down times. Replaceable vessels for both Hydrogen generation and Carbon dioxide capture systems provide viable options for both transportation and stationary Hydrogen consumption applications.

title: Fuel cell system using carbon-containing fuel as raw material and fuel, fuel cell vehicle and installation type fuel cell facility
abstract: A PEFC power generation system using hydrogen obtained from a steam reforming process as a fuel is constructed.   As a fuel cell vehicle, in a reactor E, hydrogen is produced using a potassium carbonate aqueous solution as a catalyst at the operating temperature of PEFC2 by a steam reforming process using TXLPG which is a liquefied petroleum gas as a raw material. By reacting the carbon dioxide generated by the reforming process with the aqueous potassium carbonate solution, the aqueous potassium carbonate solution is converted into an aqueous potassium hydrogen carbonate solution, and hydrogen gas not containing carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide is discharged from the reactor E, and the hydrogen carbonate The aqueous potassium solution is regenerated into a potassium carbonate aqueous solution by the solution regenerator H, and the regenerated potassium carbonate aqueous solution is equipped with a fuel reformer 1 that can be used for the next steam reforming process in the reactor E. Power is generated with PEFC2 as fuel.   [Selection] Figure 1

title: Portable hydrogen generator
abstract: A hydrogen generator employs substantially adiabatic hydrolysis and thermal decomposition of chemical hydrides to provide a controllable generation of hydrogen from a small, lightweight container. The hydrogen generator includes a thermally isolated container for containing a chemical hydride, a preheater to heat the chemical hydride to a predetermined temperature before the chemical hydride is hydrolyzed, a water supply controlled to maintain substantially adiabatic and controlled generation of hydrogen from said chemical hydride, and a buffer to supply an initial flow of hydrogen during generator start-up, absorb excess hydrogen during generator shut-down, and to smooth the hydrogen flow due to changing loads.

title: Electrode for hydrogen generation, method for producing the same, and method of electrolysis therewith
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide: an electrode for hydrogen generation, which can be used in industries such as electrolysis of water or alkali metal chloride aqueous solutions, has sufficiently low hydrogen overvoltage, is unaffected by poisoning due to iron ions, further does not cause a rise in hydrogen overvoltage or shedding of supported material during operation or during startup or shutdown, and has excellent durability; a production method for the electrode for hydrogen generation; and an electrolysis method using an electrode for hydrogen generation as the cathode, thereby reducing the amount of power consumed in the industries such as electrolysis of water or alkali metal chloride aqueous solutions.SOLUTION: An electrode for hydrogen generation comprises a catalyst layer having principal components therein supported on an electrically conductive base material, wherein the principal components are platinum, nickel, and ruthenium. In a production method for the electrode for hydrogen generation, a catalyst layer precursor is formed on an electrically conductive base substrate, and then a reduction treatment is carried out, thereby forming the catalyst layer. Using the electrode for hydrogen generation as the cathode, in an electrolysis tank in which the anode is arranged to the other side of a barrier membrane therefrom, water or an alkali metal chloride aqueous solution is subjected to electrolysis, and hydrogen gas and an alkali metal hydroxide aqueous solution are generated from on the cathode, while oxygen gas or chlorine gas is generated from on the anode.

title: System and Method for Supplying a Lighter-Than-Air Vehicle with Hydrogen Gas
abstract: A system for supplying hydrogen gas to a lighter-than-air (LTA) vehicle includes a manifold having multiple vessels. Each vessel has a first chamber that is separated from a second chamber by a barrier. A trigger assembly integrated with the barrier allows a liquid to be combined with a reactant and a catalyst in the second chamber to form a chemical reaction to generate hydrogen gas. A pressure relief valve located on each vessel opens to allow the hydrogen gas to exit when a predetermined pressure is reached, and the hydrogen gas is supplied to the LTA vehicle connected to the manifold.

Each vessel has a first chamber that is separated from a second chamber by a barrier. A trigger assembly integrated with the barrier allows a liquid to be combined with a reactant and a catalyst in the second chamber to form a chemical reaction to generate hydrogen gas. A pressure relief valve located on each vessel opens to allow the hydrogen gas to exit when a predetermined pressure is reached, and the hydrogen gas is supplied to the LTA vehicle connected to the manifold.

title: Magnesium base composite hydrogen storage material and preparation method
abstract: The invention discloses a magnesium-based composite hydrogen storage material comprising the following compositions by weight percent: 1.0-10.0% of Al, 1.0-5.0% of Ni, and the balance of Mg. A preparation method thereof comprising the following steps: at first, smelting block-shaped metal Mg and Al by a medium frequency induction to prepare Mg17Al12 alloy which is crushed to 300 meshes; hydrogenising Mg powder at 340 DEG C under a hydrogen atmosphere of 4.0MPa to prepare MgH2; then blending Mg17Al12, MgH2, Ni powder in accordance with contents by weight percent for ball-grinding for 60 to 100hours under an atmosphere of argon. The magnesium-based composite hydrogen storage material can reversely absorb and release hydrogen at low temperature, and has high hydrogen storage capacity, excellent activation performance and superior hydrogen absorbing and releasing kinetics property. The magnesium-based composite hydrogen storage material can be used for manufacturing hydrogen source to facilitate purification and storage and transportation of hydrogen, and can also used for hydrogen storage material for fuel cells.

title: Hydrogen refuelling station comprising a fill tank arranged with a cooling system
abstract: The present invention discloses a hydrogen refuelling station (1) comprising:a source (2) of hydrogen gas arranged to provide hydrogen gas at a source pressure; a first pressure storage tank arranged to store hydrogen gas at a first pressure; one or more compressors (5, 9, 13) adapted to compress and convey the hydrogen gas from the source (2) of hydrogen gas at source pressure to the first pressure storage tank;a dispensing system (19) adapted to dispense the hydrogen gas to a vehicle fuel tank (23);a fill tank (17) having an internal volume smaller than an internal volume of the first pressure storage tank, adapted to be arranged in fluid communication with the first pressure storage tank, and adapted to provide the dispensing system (19) with hydrogen gas; anda control unit (21) adapted to receive information regarding a fuel requirement of a vehicle fuel tank (23), and adapted to control the transfer of a quantity of hydrogen gas between the first pressure storage tank and the fill tank (17) based upon the information received regarding a fuel requirement of a vehicle fuel tank (23).

title: Method and system for producing carbon dioxide, purified hydrogen and electricity from a reformed process gas feed
abstract: Method and system for producing CO2, purified hydrogen and electricity from a reformed process gas feed using a solid oxide fuel cell. The method having the steps of: introducing the reformed process gas into the solid oxide fuel cell; converting hydrogen and CO of the reformed process gas in combination with oxygen into an anode off-gas including steam, CO 2  and unconverted process gas; introducing the anode off-gas into a high temperature water gas shift reactor; in the high temperature water-gas shift reactor, converting CO and steam into CO 2  and hydrogen, introducing the gas exiting the high temperature water-gas shift reactor into a low temperature water-gas shift membrane reactor, in the low temperature water-gas shift membrane reactor, converting CO and steam into CO 2  and hydrogen, whereby the low temperature water-gas shift membrane reactor comprises a hydrogen pump producing purified hydrogen on a permeate side, while removing hydrogen from a feed side.

title: A kind of interior experimental provision for generating hydrogen longitudinal direction concentration gradient of enclosure
abstract: The present invention relates to the experimental provisions that hydrogen longitudinal direction concentration gradient is generated in a kind of enclosure.Including enclosure, air accumulator, valve actuating mechanism and data collecting mechanism；Spray head is laid at the top of enclosure, one side is from top to bottom laid with gas concentration sensor, and lower part is equipped with gas vent；Valve actuating mechanism includes gas-distribution pipe, vacuum pump, hydrogen gas tank and air tank；Data gather computer structure includes data module and computer.When experiment, enclosure lower exhaust port and top showerhead are opened, hydrogen is uniformly sprayed into, original air discharge, makes to remain an atmospheric pressure in cabin in cabin；Gas vent is closed, hydrogen diffuses to form longitudinal density of hydrogen gradient in cabin；Gas concentration sensor detects the real-time oxygen concentration of different location, and since air pressure remains unchanged in cabin, increased density of hydrogen is reduced oxygen concentration.The present invention compensates for the blank of enclosure fuel gas longitudinal direction concentration gradient research in the research of gas fuel burning blast characteristics.

title: Nickel-hydrogen storage alloy battery
abstract: A nickel-hydrogen storage alloy battery is disclosed. This nickel-hydrogen storage alloy battery is roughly divided into a case, a plurality of plate-shaped negative electrode plates placed in the case, and a plurality of plate-shaped positive electrode plates insulated from the negative electrode plate by an insulator. The negative electrode plate consists of a current collector and only an active material attached to the current collector, thereby increasing the capacity of the nickel-hydrogen storage alloy battery per unit volume and reducing the cost. In addition, the negative electrode plate is formed to be a negative electrode plate as a plate-shaped hydrogen storage alloy plate applied to the current collector itself, thereby improving the workability according to the production of the negative electrode plate has the effect of cost reduction.

title: Hydrogen storage alloy electrode
abstract: PURPOSE:To restrain elusion of an alloy composition to an alkali electrolyte by using an alloy, where Zn is added into a hydrogen storage alloy, the phase of which mainly includes a C15 type Laves phase so as to prepare a hydrogen storage alloy electrode. CONSTITUTION:A hydrogen storage alloy, where an alloy phase is mainly composed of a C15 (MgCU2) type Laves phase, is prepared by heating and dissolving metals such as Zr, Mn, V, Co, and Ni as raw materials inside an arc melting furnace in the atmosphere of argon, so as to provide an alloy composition of ZrMn0.6V0.2Co0.1Ni1.2. A hydrogen storage alloy electrode is manufactured by using the hydrogen storage alloy or hydride added with Zn as a base. Consequently, it is possible to restrain elusion of the alloy composition to an alkali electrolyte, and to efficiently store and discharge hydrogen from the beginning by using a Zn-Mn-V-Co-Ni alloy as the hydrogen storage alloy, i.e., the base.

title: Hydrogen recovery from hydrogen sulfide
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a method for recovering hydrogen from H2 S and separating sulfur therefrom. SOLUTION: This method for recovering hydrogen is constituted by separating and removing various accompanying components by continuously cooling a raw material H2 S gas becoming almost 100% H2 S, further cooling the gas until temperature thereof attains at <=-50 deg.C, and passing the H2 S gas through membranes constituted by a transition metal such as Ni, Fe, etc., or also filled with a granular sulfur therein for a separation by an adsorption and a separation by a reaction to separate the H2 S gas into hydrogen gas and a sulfur fraction.

SOLUTION: This method for recovering hydrogen is constituted by separating and removing various accompanying components by continuously cooling a raw material H2 S gas becoming almost 100% H2 S, further cooling the gas until temperature thereof attains at <=-50 deg.C, and passing the H2 S gas through membranes constituted by a transition metal such as Ni, Fe, etc., or also filled with a granular sulfur therein for a separation by an adsorption and a separation by a reaction to separate the H2 S gas into hydrogen gas and a sulfur fraction.

title: Me-RGO (Reduced Graphene Oxide)/LiBH4 hydrogen storage material with high hydrogen storage capacity and preparation methods of Me-RGO/LiBH4 hydrogen storage material
abstract: The invention provides a Me-RGO (Reduced Graphene Oxide) modified LiBH4 hydrogen storage material with high hydrogen storage capacity and preparation methods of the Me-RGO modified LiBH4 hydrogen storage material. A uniformly-dispersed Me-RGO catalyst with a high special surface is compounded on the surface of the hydrogen storage material LiBH4 at the inert gas shielding atmosphere by respectively using a solid-phase high-speed ball-milling method, a melt infiltration method and a liquid-phase dispersion method to prepare the hydrogen storage material with high hydrogen storage capacity. The preparation methods of the Me-RGO catalyst with high dispensability include an aqueous phase complex reduction method, a vapour phase reduction method, an ion exchange method, a sol-gel method, a hydrothermal method, a vacuum sputtering method and a gas evaporation method. Even if the content of the used Me-RGO catalyst is as low as 3wt.%, the hydrogen discharging temperature of LiBH4 can also be greatly reduced, and meanwhile, the low-temperature hydrogen discharging capacity and the circulated hydrogen absorption and discharging properties of LiBH4 can be improved.

title: Hydrogen gas producing apparatus and hydrogen gas producing method
abstract: The present invention is applied to human livestock excreta and organic waste to produce hydrogen gas, and at the same time reduce pollutants from the organic waste and the like, and a hydrogen gas production method It is about.   In the hydrogen gas production apparatus of the present invention, water is put in a tank in a high pressure state. The high-pressure water is heated to high temperature with a heat exchanger, and then mixed with stirring organic wastes and the like. Moreover, the said stirring container makes the mixture of the said organic waste etc. and water into a high pressure state with a high pressure pump. The mixture of organic waste or the like and water that has reached a high pressure is passed through a static mixer at a high pressure and a high temperature to be in a supercritical state. The mixture of organic waste or the like and water in a supercritical state is then separated into hydrogen gas and impurities when passing through the hydrocyclone.   [Selection] Figure 1

title: Hydrogen production system
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a hydrogen production system capable of suppressing deterioration in a catalyst during temperature rising.SOLUTION: Provided is a hydrogen production system 1 comprising: a dehydrogenation reaction part 10 where an organic halide R is contacted with a catalyst 12, and dehydrogenation for the organic halide R is caused to generate a hydrogen-containing fluid HT; a pressure rising part 52 for increasing the pressure of the hydrogen-containing fluid HM made to flow out from the hydrogen reaction part 10; a buffer tank 54 provided in the flow direction of the hydrogen-containing fluid Hf and also at the downstream side of the pressure rising part 52; and a recycle gas line 61 directly or indirectly introducing the hydrogen-containing fluid Hr in the buffer tank 54 into the dehydrogenation reaction part 10. In this way, before the dehydrogenation reaction of the organic hydride R is performed, the feed of the hydrogen Hr into the dehydrogenation reaction part 10 from the buffer tank 54 stored with the hydrogen Hr generated in the former is made possible, and deterioration in the catalyst 12 caused by hydrogen sufficiency can be suppressed.SELECTED DRAWING: Figure 1

abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To optimally inject hydrogen into an intake pipe or a cylinder in a hydrogenated internal combustion engine using hydrogen gas together with gasoline as a combustion fuel.   A hydrogenated internal combustion engine 10 that uses hydrogen gas together with hydrocarbon fuel as combustion fuel, a hydrogen fuel port injection valve 28 that injects hydrogen gas into an intake port 18 or a cylinder, and a hydrogen fuel port injection valve And a regulator 44 that lowers the pressure of the hydrogen gas applied to the hydrogen fuel port injection valve 28 as the amount of hydrogen injected from the fuel injector 28 decreases. title: Fuel Storage System for Hydrogen Propulsion Conrainer Ship
abstract: The present invention relates to a fuel storage system of a hydrogen-propelled container carrier capable of minimizing structural changes of an existing container ship by modularizing a storage container for storing liquefied hydrogen and a container shipping on a container ship and rearranging it in a design space. The fuel storage system of a hydrogen-propelled container carrier according to the present invention includes a hydrogen storage container in which liquefied hydrogen is stored, an outer container having a size and shape corresponding to that of a container conveyed by the container carrier, and receiving the hydrogen storage container, the A hydrogen storage module including a fuel supply unit installed in a space between the hydrogen storage container and the outer container to vaporize and depressurize liquefied hydrogen discharged from the hydrogen storage container; A cell guide unit including a plurality of cell guides that are vertically installed in a space of a ship to support both corner portions of the front end and the rear end of the hydrogen storage module so as to move up and down; A battery stack installation unit disposed outside the cell guide unit to receive hydrogen gas supplied from the fuel supply unit of the hydrogen storage module to generate electricity for propulsion of the container carrier; And a hydrogen supply unit for supplying hydrogen from the hydrogen storage module to the fuel cell stack through the partition wall of the cell guide unit.

title: Fuel cell system
abstract: A fuel cell system comprises a hydrogen storage system for storing and releasing hydrogen, a fuel cell in fluid communication with the hydrogen storage system for receiving released hydrogen from the hydrogen storage system and for electrochemically reacting the hydrogen with an oxidant to produce electricity and an anode exhaust. A catalytic combustor is in fluid communication with the fuel cell for receiving the anode exhaust and for catalytically reacting the anode exhaust to produce an offgas having an elevated temperature that is greater than the temperature of the anode exhaust. The heat from the offgas is used to release the hydrogen from the hydrogen storage system.

title: Process and installation for producing metal powder associated or not with hydrogen production
abstract: The invention relates to a process for obtaining metallic powder, especially low-carb iron, used in the steel industry, whether or not associated with hydrogen, and to a plant for the application of this process. The process according to the invention consists in that the iron ore preheated at 450 ° C is partially deoxidized, using a reducing agent a gaseous mixture formed mainly of CO and H2, after which part of the partially reduced ore is recirculated and reoxidized with steam oxygen, obtaining pure hydrogen, followed by reintroduction into the main stream of the ore, deoxidizing stream to the full, the deoxidized powder cools, separates from the sterile event and is briquetted or seamed. According to the invention, the plant consists essentially of an ore silo (1), a deoxidizing reactor (4), a steam re-oxidation reactor (14), final deoxidation reactors (20) and some heat exchangers (7, 8, 25 and 35).

title: Magnesium-based hydrogen storage material with added rare earth element and preparation method thereof
abstract: The invention discloses a magnesium-based hydrogen storage material with added rare earth elements and a preparation method thereof; the magnesium-based hydrogen storage material comprises the following components of magnesium metal and rare earth elements; the mass percent of the rare earth elements is 0.1%-20%, and the mass percent of the magnesium metal is 80%-99.9%; the rare earth elements comprise one or more than one of La, Ce, Pr, Nd, Sm and Er; the magnesium-based hydrogen storage material of the invention has good hydrogen storage dynamic performance; compared with pure magnesium nano-powder prepared under same conditions, the magnesium-based hydrogen storage material has a lower hydrogen storage-release operation temperature, a closer hydrogen absorption-desorption plateau pressure, an obviously higher speed, and a maintained high hydrogen storage capacity, and has a hydrogen absorption amount of up to 7.26% at about 300 DEG C.

title: Exhaust gas processing device for fuel cell
abstract: Hydrogen gas supplied to and then purged from a fuel cell is temporarily retained in a reservoir provided in an exhaust fuel diluter, and diluted before release into atmosphere. Cathode exhaust gas discharged from a cathode of the fuel cell is mixed to dilute the hydrogen gas, and air continues to be supplied to the cathode of the fuel cell for a specific period of time even after generation of electric power in the fuel cell is stopped, so that the hydrogen gas retained in the reservoir is continuously diluted with the cathode exhaust gas and released. Air for ventilation of the fuel cell may be used instead or in addition, to dilute and release the hydrogen gas retained in the reservoir after the generation of electric power in the fuel cell is stopped.

title: Alkaline storage battery and manufacture of hydrogen storage alloy electrode for alkaline storage battery
abstract: PURPOSE:To improve the cycle characteristic by using a hydrogen storage alloy as a negative electrode, said hydrogen storage alloy consisting of an intermetallic compound phase capable of occluding and releasing hydrogen reversibly and a pure metal phase or solid solution phase of a particular additive metal. CONSTITUTION:A hydrogen storage alloy, consisting of an intermetallic compound phase capable of occluding and releasing hydrogen reversibly and a pure metal phase or solid solution phase of at least one additive metal selected from among Cd, Sn, Bi, Pb which can be oxidized and reduced in an alkaline aqueous solution, is used as a negative electrode. In this case, the additive metal is oxidized or reduced in the alkaline aqueous solution and achieves bridging effect to bind the hydrogen storage alloy powder together and suppresses the falling-off of the alloy powder from the electrode supporter. The casted alloy has a eutectic structure and is a good electric conductor having a layer structure whose layers are arranged with a spacing of several microns. Thus, by using such a homogeneous hydrogen storage alloy as the negative electrode, the cycle characteristic of an alkaline storage battery can be improved.

Although Co is hardly or not at all contained in the hydrogen absorbing alloy, degradation due to pulverization does not occur, and a large-capacity electrode can be produced.

title: Hydrogen storage materials and methods including hydrides and hydroxides
abstract: In one aspect, the invention provides a hydrogen storage composition having a hydrogenated state and a dehydrogenated state. In the hydrogenated state, such composition comprises a hydride and a hydroxide. In a dehydrogenated state, the composition comprises an oxide. The present invention also provides methods of producing hydrogen, including for mobile fuel cell device applications.

title: Thermoelectric hydrogen poly-generation system for improving flexibility of thermoelectric unit
abstract: The invention relates to a heat, power and hydrogen poly-generation system for improving the flexibility of a thermoelectric unit, which comprises a water electrolysis hydrogen production subsystem, a steam turbine generator subsystem and a steam extraction heat supply subsystem which are connected with each other. The invention is suitable for cogeneration units and even straight condensing units, and can obviously improve the flexibility on the premise of less investment, safety guarantee, convenient and quick operation adjustment, large peak regulation range and small influence on the operation heat economy of the units. The invention organically combines the water electrolysis hydrogen production technology, the steam turbine technology and the steam extraction heat supply technology, improves the flexibility of the unit and simultaneously considers the heat economy.

title: Fuel cell system with electrochemical anode exhaust recycling
abstract: A method of operating a fuel cell system includes providing a fuel inlet stream into a fuel cell stack, operating the fuel cell stack to generate electricity and a hydrogen containing fuel exhaust stream, separating at least a portion of hydrogen contained in the fuel exhaust stream using a high temperature, low hydration ion exchange membrane cell stack, and providing the hydrogen separated from the fuel exhaust stream into the fuel inlet stream.

title: Hydrogen storage container
abstract: An object of the present invention is to prevent hydrogen embrittlement and peeling occurring between an outer layer material and an inner layer material due to retention of hydrogen between the inner layer material and the outer layer material.   An outer layer material (2) that stores a gas containing hydrogen therein and withstands an internal pressure from the gas, and an inner layer that is provided so as to cover an inner peripheral surface of the outer layer material (2) and is formed of a hydrogen-resistant material In the hydrogen storage container 1 provided with the material 3, a flow path to be guided to the outside is provided between the inner material 3 and the outer material 2. Further, the flow path was formed in a spiral shape. Further, a fluid supply means for supplying the fluid to the flow path is provided so that the fluid for removing the retained hydrogen flows to the outside.   [Selection diagram] Fig. 1

title: Hydrogen production from an integrated electrolysis cell and hydrocarbon gasification reactor
abstract: An integrated process for hydrogen gas production includes: a. operating a water electrolysis cell with an external source of electricity to produce oxygen and hydrogen; b. optionally operating an air separation unit to produce additional oxygen for the process; c. introducing a hydrocarbon feedstock into a membrane wall gasification reactor with an ash-forming material and steam, and oxygen from the electrolysis cell and, optionally, oxygen from the air separation unit to produce hot raw synthesis gas; d. passing the hot raw synthesis gas from the gasification reactor to a steam-generating heat exchanger to produce steam and a cooled raw synthesis gas; e. introducing the steam generated in the heat exchanger into a turbine to produce electricity to operate the electrolysis cell; and f. recovering the hydrogen gas from the water electrolysis cell and, optionally, subjecting the synthesis gas to a water-gas shift reaction to increase the hydrogen content and recovering the hydrogen.

title: System of keeping and supply of hydrogen
abstract:
FIELD: machine building, keeping liquids and gases. 
     SUBSTANCE: invention refers to hydrogen energy industry, i.e. to accumulating, keeping and releasing of hydrogen for utilisation in transport and stationary power installations. The technical result is achieved by manufacture of a package of micro-capillaries in the system of keeping and supply of hydrogen in form of a cylinder bottle-shaped figure. The package is sealed from the cylinder part end of a bigger radius and is opened at neck part located in the case of a valve point with the installed drive of a needle valve; neck-type part of the package is secured in an input channel of the valve point case wherein the needle valve is located; the needle valve is controlled with a mechanism built-in into the body of the valve point. Diametre of the neck part of micro-capillaries package from the side of their open ends is 0.05-0.10 of diametre of the cylinder part of the package, while an aspect number of micro-capillaries in neck and cylinder parts is within ranges of 0.01-0.10. 
     EFFECT: increased specific capacity of cartridge for gaseous hydrogen, reduced time of hydrogen releasing and charging cartridge with hydrogen at any ambient temperature; control over process of hydrogen releasing, increased reliability of operation of system of release and charging cartridge with hydrogen, reduced power costs for charge and release of hydrogen and elimination of hydrogen leaks from cartridges. 
     2 cl, 3 dwg

title: Co-producing hydrogen and power by biomass gasification
abstract: A method for co-producing hydrogen and electrical power comprises utilizing an intermittent renewable energy source to generate energy for producing hydrogen and oxygen and subsequently transferring at least a portion of the energy to a production system to produce the hydrogen and the oxygen. The current technique further comprises channeling at least a portion of the hydrogen to a hydrogen-delivery system configured to deliver the hydrogen from the hydrogen-delivery system to at least one of a power generation system or a hydrogen-storage system and channeling at least a portion of the oxygen to an oxygen delivery system configured to deliver the oxygen from the oxygen delivery system to a biomass gasification system that produces a synthesis gas by partial oxidation of a biomass feedstock. Further, this technique includes channeling at least a portion of the synthesis gas to the power generation system to produce electrical power therefrom.

title: Fuel cell system with liquid cooling device
abstract:
A fuel cell system includes a fuel cell stack, an air supply system including a
blower for driving the air to the fuel cell stack and an air humidifier for
humidifying the air supplied to the fuel cell stack, a hydrogen supply system
including a hydrogen storage and a pressure regulating device, and a hydrogen
recirculator for receiving excessive hydrogen from the fuel cell stack and forcing
the hydrogen back into the fuel cell stack in order to induce a hydrogen flow
inside the fuel cell stack, a coolant circulation system supplying low temperature
coolant to the fuel cell stack for absorbing heat from the fuel cell stack and
including a coolant reservoir in which the coolant is stored, a pump driving the
circulation of the coolant, a radiator for removing heat from the high temperature
coolant and converting the high temperature coolant into the low temperature
coolant. The coolant reservoir includes a ventilation device for removing air
bubbles from the coolant. The coolant circulation system includes a heat
exchanger for transferring heat from the high temperature coolant to the hydrogen
storage. A control circuit electrically controls the flow and pressure regulating
device, the blower, the pump and the fan.
text: Fuel cell system with liquid cooling device A fuel cell system includes a fuel cell stack, an air supply system including a blower for driving the air to the fuel cell stack and an air humidifier for humidifying the air supplied to the fuel cell stack, a hydrogen supply system including a hydrogen storage and a pressure regulating device, and a hydrogen recirculator for receiving excessive hydrogen from the fuel cell stack and forcing the hydrogen back into the fuel cell stack in order to induce a hydrogen flow inside the fuel cell stack, a coolant circulation system supplying low temperature coolant to the fuel cell stack for absorbing heat from the fuel cell stack and including a coolant reservoir in which the coolant is stored, a pump driving the circulation of the coolant, a radiator for removing heat from the high temperature coolant and converting the high temperature coolant into the low temperature coolant. The coolant reservoir includes a ventilation device for removing air bubbles from the coolant. The coolant circulation system includes a heat exchanger for transferring heat from the high temperature coolant to the hydrogen storage. A control circuit electrically controls the flow and pressure regulating device, the blower, the pump and the fan.

title: Hydrogen filling device and equipment for hydrogen storage alloy container, and method of filling container with hydrogen
abstract: The present invention provides a hydrogen filling facility that enables efficient cooling of a container when filling a hydrogen storage alloy container with hydrogen.   SOLUTION: A pipe 14 for supplying hydrogen to a hydrogen storage alloy container 1 and a passage 11 through which a cooling medium for cooling the hydrogen storage alloy container 1 is passed, and the hydrogen storage alloy container 1 is arranged in this passage 11. And a cooling unit formed so as to surround the space. The hydrogen filling facility is configured. Further, the hydrogen storage alloy container 1 is filled with hydrogen so as to cool the hydrogen storage alloy container 1 by interposing a heat conducting material such as a metal ball 12 having good heat conduction between the passage 11 through which the cooling medium passes and the hydrogen storage alloy container 1. Configure equipment.   [Selection] Figure 1

title: A hydrogen cooled hydride storage unit
abstract: A hydrogen gas cooled hydrogen storage element which includes a hydrogen storage alloy material in which hydrogen flow channels are provided. The flow channels provide pathways through the hydrogen storage material to allow for high speed hydrogen gas flow. A portion of the high speed hydrogen flow is stored within the storage material which releases its heat of hydride formation. The remainder of the hydrogen flows through the hydrogen storage material at a sufficient mass flow rate to remove the heat of hydride formation.

title: System and method for hydrogen generation using atomic reactor
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a system by which the sufficient quantity of heat to oper ate a water decomposing system at 850-950 deg.C is supplied. SOLUTION: This hydrogen generation system (10) is constituted of a liquid metal atomic reactor (12) having a non-radioactive secondary heat loop (14), a steam generator (16) connected to the secondary heat loop (14), a high temperature water decomposing apparatus (18) and a topping heater (20). The temperature of feed water (30) fed to the water decomposing apparatus is increased to 450-550 deg.C by the heat generated in the atomic reactor and further is increased to >=850 deg.C from 450-550 deg.C by the topping heater 20 to efficiently operate the water decomposing apparatus (18) for generating hydrogen and oxygen.

abstract: <P>PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To enhance the reforming capability of a hydrogen generator, to suppress catalytic deterioration and to enhance the durability of the cylinder in a reformer.  <P>SOLUTION: A hydrogen generating apparatus comprises a combustor 1, a first cylinder 2 where a combustion gas from the combustor is discharged and a second cylinder 3 located at the outside of the first cylinder. title: Hydrogen generator with reactant dilution scheme
abstract: A hydrogen generator ( 201 ) is provided which comprises (a) a chamber ( 205 ) having a hydrogen-containing material ( 213 ) disposed therein; (b) a first fluidic pathway ( 241 ) containing said chamber, said first fluidic pathway including an upstream portion which is upstream of said chamber, and a downstream portion which is downstream from said chamber; (c) a second fluidic pathway ( 243 ) which does not include said chamber, and which is adjoined to said first fluidic pathway downstream from said chamber; and (d) a reservoir ( 203 ) containing a liquid medium in which said hydrogen-containing material is soluble, said reservoir being in fluidic communication with at least one of said first and second fluidic pathways.

title: Hydrogen-generating device, and power-generating system for generating electricity by using hydrogen generated in the hydrogen-generating device as fuel
abstract: <P>PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a device for generating hydrogen in a system using an internal combustion engine, and to further provide a system for generating electricity with the use of the generated hydrogen to prevent a secondary battery from decreasing the discharging capacity.  <P>SOLUTION: A hydrogen-generating device 7 comprises a proton-conductive solid electrolyte 75 having a pair of electrodes 76 and 77, and a voltage application means 2 and 17 for applying direct voltage to a pair of the electrodes. The power-generating system comprises the steps of: contacting one electrode 76 out of a pair of the electrodes in the hydrogen-generating device 7 with a gas exhausted from an internal combustion engine 1, in a state of having applied direct voltage to a pair of the electrodes to electrolyze moisture contained in the exhaust gas; thereby generating hydrogen from the other electrode 77 of a pair of the electrodes, which does not contact with the exhaust gas; storing hydrogen generated from the hydrogen-generating device 7 in a hydrogen storage means 9; and when the internal combustion engine 1 stops working, supplying the hydrogen stored in the hydrogen storage means 9 to a fuel cell 12 to make the fuel cell 12 generate electric energy and supply the electric energy to the secondary battery 2.  <P>COPYRIGHT: (C)2006,JPO&NCIPI

title: Gas release system
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a gas discharge system capable of suppressing generation of liquid air around a discharge tower for discharging vaporized gas generated in a tank. A gas discharge system includes a tank for storing liquid hydrogen, a discharge tower extending upward, and an inner pipe having a discharge port opening to the atmosphere at least at an upper end thereof. A discharge column having a double-tube structure consisting of an outer tube that covers the inner tube with a gap between them, a release line that guides the vaporized gas of liquid hydrogen vaporized in the tank into the inner tube, the inner tube and the outer tube. A gas supply device for supplying a medium gas, which is hydrogen gas or helium gas having a temperature higher than the boiling point of oxygen, to the gap between the tubes. title: Hydrogen storage composition and method
abstract: A hydrogen storage composition based on a metal hydride dispersed in an aerogel prepared by a sol-gel process. The starting material for the aerogel is an organometallic compound, including the alkoxysilanes, organometals of the form M(OR)x and MOxRy, where R is an alkyl group of the form C n H 2n+1 , M is an oxide-forming metal, n, x, and y are integers, and y is two less than the valence of M. A sol is prepared by combining the starting material, alcohol, water, and an acid. The sol is conditioned to the proper viscosity and a hydride in the form of a fine powder is added. The mixture is polymerized and dried under supercritical conditions. The final product is a composition having a hydride uniformly dispersed throughout an inert, stable and highly porous matrix. It is capable of absorbing up to 30 moles of hydrogen per kilogram at room temperature and pressure, rapidly and reversibly. Hydrogen absorbed by the composition can be readily be recovered by heat or evacuation.

title: Nickel hydrogen storage battery
abstract: (57) [Abstract] [Purpose] To provide a nickel-hydrogen storage battery having a long life and excellent high rate discharge characteristics. A positive electrode containing nickel oxide or nickel hydroxide, a negative electrode containing a hydrogen storage alloy, a separator inserted between the positive electrode and the negative electrode, and an electrolytic solution composed of an alkaline aqueous solution are provided. Nickel-metal hydride storage battery containing sulfide in the negative electrode. The progress of corrosion of the negative electrode alloy can be stopped and the life characteristics can be significantly improved.

title: Cogeneration system using for fuel cell
abstract: Provided is a fuel cell complex generation system which minimizes the load for improving generation efficiency and is environmental friendly by using the waste heat, warm water, etc. A fuel cell complex generation system stores the hydrogen gas generated at the electrolysis device of water in a hydrogen storage tank(30) to supply it to the fuel electrode(22) of a fuel cell generation system, transmits the electricity generated by electrochemical reaction through an inverter, sends the oxygen generated at the electrolysis device of water to the air electrode(23) through an oxygen storage tank(35), and sends the water generate by reaction to the electrolysis device of water, wherein the waste heat generated at the fuel cell generation system(20) and the electrolysis device of water are sent to a heat engine(50) to generate additional power by a compressor and a steam turbine, the remaining warm water is sent to a boiler and the recovered cool water is used as the cooling water of the fuel cell generation system and the electrolysis device of water. The amount of gas from the hydrogen storage tank and the oxygen storage tank to the fuel cell generation system is controlled by a control valve(43,44) to prevent the generation on unreacted gas.

title: System and method for supplying hydrogen and hydrogen fuel cell system.
abstract: A system and a method for supplying hydrogen and a hydrogen fuel cell system are provided. The system for supplying hydrogen includes a hydrogen infusing unit and a hydrogen supply unit (100) which comprises a hydrogen storage unit (1), a hydrogen conveying unit (2) and a charging apparatus (3) connected with the hydrogen storage unit, wherein the charging apparatus (3) comprises a charging opening (31) matched with the hydrogen infusing unit which comprises an infusing apparatus matched with the charging opening (31). The external infusing apparatus uses the charging opening (31) to infuse hydrogen to the hydrogen storage container contained in the hydrogen storage unit, which avoids replacement of the hydrogen storage container (13) during the entire infusing process, and enables the intensively supplementing hydrogen period in a convenient and fast manner.

title: Fuel cell system
abstract:
A fuel cell system according to the present invention includes a reformer for reforming a fuel containing hydrogen to generate hydrogen gas, and at least one electricity generator for generating electrical energy through an electrochemical reaction between the hydrogen gas and oxygen; A fuel supply source for supplying the fuel to the reformer, and an oxygen supply source for supplying oxygen to the electricity generator, wherein the fuel supply source substantially connects a fuel tank for storing the fuel, the fuel tank, and the reformer. And a pumping unit installed on the supply path and capable of driving in both directions.

title: Fabrication method of hydrogen storage tank
abstract: The present invention relates to a method of manufacturing a hydrogen storage tank, and when the storage material is injected in the vertical direction of the tank and the rate at which the storage material is filled in the vertical direction decreases, the tank is vibrated in the horizontal direction at a resonance frequency, By resonant frequency, the mobility can be increased to be distributed in a short time in the horizontal direction, thereby reducing the time required to fill the storage material in the hydrogen tank, and reduce the consumption of energy used to operate the vibrator.

title: fuel cartridge
abstract: The invention relates to a new fuel cartridge for supplying hydrogen gas in the base with a reagent system. thus, a fuel cartridge for a fuel cell device comprises a reactor compartment for storing a first reagent, a water compartment for storing water. has a mixing compartment (106) containing a second water soluble reagent, and a fluid communication means (114) between the mixing compartment (106) and the reactor compartment (102) adapted to pass the second dissolved reagent into water into the reactor compartment (102), wherein the second dissolved reagent may react with the first reagent to generate a gas. Suitably, the fuel cartridge comprises an interface connectable to a water control mechanism disposed outside the cartridge, the water control mechanism configured to control a flow of water between the water compartment and the mixing compartment such that water flows. mix with and dissolve the second reagent in the mixing compartment.

title: Organic-transition metal composite as hydrogen storage material and method for producing the same
abstract: The present invention provides an organic-transition metal complex capable of safely and reversibly storing a large amount of hydrogen as a hydrogen storage material, and a method for producing the same.   To achieve this, a hydrogen storage material according to the present invention relates to a complex formed by combining an organic material containing a hydroxy group (-OH) and a compound containing a transition metal, and per molecule. The present invention relates to a substance that can store hydrogen more efficiently by combining one or more transition metals. Examples of the organic substance containing the hydroxy group (—OH) include alkyl derivatives containing ethylene glycol, trimethylene glycol, glycerol and the like, and aryl derivatives containing hydroxy groups such as hydroquinone and phloroglucinol. Examples of the transition metal include titanium (Ti), vanadium (V), scandium (Sc), and the like capable of Kubas binding with hydrogen.   [Selection] Figure 1

title: Hydrogen compressor
abstract: Apparatus and process for producing hydrogen gas at a desired pressure comprising feeding a hydrogen gas at a first temperature and first pressure from a hydrogen source to heat transfer means comprising cooling means and heating means; cooling the hydrogen gas with the cooling means to provide cooled hydrogen gas; feeding the cooled hydrogen gas to a metal hydride generator containing the metal; forming the metal hydride within the generator; heating the formed metal hydride to a temperature Tp and desired pressure; and releasing the pressurized hydrogen gas at the desired pressure from the generator and producing regenerated metal. Preferably, the apparatus comprises a plurality of generators linked to the heat transfer means to allow for continuous usage of both cooled hydrogen gas and generated heat from the heat transfer means to collectively provide a continuous pressurized hydrogen gas product supply.

title: Power storage device
abstract: To provide a novel power storage device.SOLUTION: A power storage device 100 comprises at least a first closed chamber 110, a second closed chamber 120, a power storage element 130 and a gas passage 140. The power storage element 130 is accommodated in the first closed chamber 110. The power storage element 130 contains at least a cathode active material, an anode active material and an alkaline electrolyte. The anode active material contains a hydrogen-storing alloy and hydrogen gas. The first closed chamber 110 and the second closed chamber 120 are configured to be immune to a pressure of the hydrogen gas. The gas passage 140 connects the first closed chamber 110 with the second closed chamber 120. Further, the power storage device 100 is configured to control movement of the hydrogen gas between the first closed chamber 110 and the second closed chamber 120.SELECTED DRAWING: Figure 1

title: Hydrogen production apparatus
abstract: <P>PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a hydrogen production apparatus with a simple structure and a small size.  <P>SOLUTION: In this hydrogen production apparatus wherein steam is supplied to a reaction chamber containing hydrocarbon- or carbon-containing reactive particles to cause a reaction at a high temperature to produce hydrogen, the reaction chamber comprises a compression chamber 2 having a part 2A convergent from one end toward the other end. At one end of the compression chamber, a plurality of nozzles 4 are installed for intermittently jetting high-pressure steam toward the other end of the compression chamber; at the other end, a gas take-out port 6 and an exhaust vent 7, both opened at a specified time, are installed; and near the other end, a reactive particles supply port 10 is installed for supplying the reactive particles into the compression chamber at a specified time. Hydrogen gas is generated by converging a plurality of impact waves caused by high-pressure steam jetted into the compression chamber and subjecting the mixture of the reactive particles and steam to impact compression by the impact waves in the compression chamber to thereby heat the mixture to a high temperature, thus reacting the reactive particles in the mixture with steam.  <P>COPYRIGHT: (C)2004,JPO

At one end of the compression chamber, a plurality of nozzles 4 are installed for intermittently jetting high-pressure steam toward the other end of the compression chamber; at the other end, a gas take-out port 6 and an exhaust vent 7, both opened at a specified time, are installed; and near the other end, a reactive particles supply port 10 is installed for supplying the reactive particles into the compression chamber at a specified time. title: Hydrogen gas storage tank with supporting filter tube(s)
abstract: A conformable hydrogen gas storage tank includes a shell defining a tank interior, a hydrogen storage material contained in the tank interior, and one or more filter tubes disposed within the tank interior. Each of the filter tubes includes an internal flow passage for directing a bulk flow hydrogen gas and is constructed such that hydrogen gas is allowed to diffuse between the internal flow passage and the tank interior outside of the filter tube. Additionally, the one or more filter tubes are constructed to support the tank shell against outwardly-directed forces resulting from pressure within the tank interior.
