title: System comprising a pivotable armrest and a pendulum element
abstract: A system includes a pivotable armrest ( 1 ) and a pendulum element ( 18 ). The armrest ( 1 ) is pivotably hinged on a holding device ( 10 ) around a primary axis ( 11 ). The armrest ( 1 ) is connected rotatably fixed to a connection element ( 15 ). The holding device ( 10 ) includes the pendulum element ( 18 ). The pendulum element ( 18 ) is pivotable around a secondary axis ( 12 ). The connection element ( 15 ) and the pendulum element ( 18 ) are configured such that the pendulum element ( 18 ) is pivoted into a first position when a force is exerted on the system. The pendulum element ( 18 ) pivoted in the first position interlockingly interacts with the connection element ( 15 ) in such a way that a pivoting of the connection element along a second direction of rotation ( 32 ) is countered. The first direction of rotation ( 31 ) runs counter to the second direction of rotation ( 32 ).

title: Systems and methods for using technology to enable consumers' purchasing power in the marketplace
abstract: Systems and methods can enable individuals to become highly informed consumers with regard to their purchases, including directly at the point of purchase, and can allow them to fully assert their powers of consumer choice on each product or service purchase they consider. Various embodiments also enable consumers to provide individual companies and entire industries direct and clear feedback on why consumers purchased certain products or services and avoided others. Additionally, various embodiments may enable consumers with similar purchase decision criteria to consolidate and summarize their feedback without ever having to organize, meet each other, or interact in any way. Companies can be presented with organized and structured data from regarding consumers' purchasing decisions. In some embodiments, disclosed systems and methods can serve as a marketplace for like-minded consumers and companies to find each other with a reduced effort, thereby substantially empowering consumers and lowering the companies' customer acquisition costs.

title: Decontaminating aerosol
abstract: Methods and compositions for decontaminating exposed surfaces or spaces which may be contaminated with chemical and biological warfare agents and the like are disclosed. The contaminants can be chemical warfare agents, industrial pollutants, pesticides, toxins and the like. For decontamination, a contaminated surface or space is contacted with an aerosol comprising a decontaminating agent, and subsequently or simultaneously, the decontaminated surface or space is contacted with an aerosol comprising a decontaminating agent antagonist. In particular, self-inactivating aerosols are disclosed.

title: Target detection device
abstract: (57) [Summary] [PROBLEMS] In a device for detecting a ship using satellite images, there is a problem that geographical coordinates of latitude and longitude cannot be obtained at the same time. In addition, there is a problem that the identification of the detected ship requires collation by hand. SOLUTION: The pixel coordinates of a satellite image are encoded into geographical coordinate values, and a latitude / longitude value is calculated from the pixel coordinate values of a detected ship.

title: Model training method and device
abstract: A model training method and device, the method comprising: reading partial sample data from a full sample set, and forming a sample subset (101); mapping model parameters relevant to the partial sample data from a first feature component of the full sample set to a second feature component of the sample subset (102); training a model according to sample data having said partial second feature component (103). After mapping, it is possible to decrease the size of the copy of model parameters on a sample computer, greatly decrease the amount of training data, minimize computer memory footprint, and use the sample computer memory to place vectors and load samples - and thereby, with as little efficiency loss as possible, to implement machine learning and large-scale model training with relatively low resource overhead. Mapping has no effect on the computing performance of the model training process and is transparent to the training algorithm, so the original training algorithm can be used directly without modification.

title: Cleaner for air in automobile
abstract: The utility model relates to a cleaner for air in an automobile, composed of an air cleaner and harmful gas remover, wherein, the air cleaner is composed of a casing and a dust removal box. The dust removal box is arranged in the hollow frame of the inner part of the casing, and foam plastic and gauze net are arranged in the dust removal box. The harmful gas remover is composed of a casing and a gas filter, activated carbon granules are arranged in the gas filter, and the filter is arranged in the hollow frame of the inner part of the casing. The air cleaner and the harmful gas remover are arranged in the ducting system of an air-conditioner in the automobile. The utility model has the advantages of simple structure, easy application, and good dust removal effect.

title: Scooter having a spring cushioning device
abstract: A scooter includes one or more wheels attached to the rear portion of a board, and a shaft rotatably secured to the front portion of the board for supporting one or more front wheels. A cushioning device is disposed between the board and the shaft for preventing the board from striking onto the shaft and for allowing the riders to more comfortably ride the scooter. One or more poles are secured between the board and the shaft, and one or more springs are engaged between the board and the shaft for cushioning the board.

title: Full-automatic mah-jong machine delivery mechanism
abstract: The utility model relates to a full-automatic mah-jong transmitter, comprising four transmission shafts linked by relative transmitters, wherein each transmission shaft is connected with a card feeder. And the utility model comprises left and right transmission shafts on a casing at the inner side of a card lifting plate, wherein the shaft is mounted with a gear wheel, a card synchronous band is between two transmission wheels, two ends of the synchronous band are respectively relative to an adsorption magnet 1 and a support base on the casing, the transmission shaft is mounted with a synchronous band wheel meshed with the inner surface tooth of the synchronous band. The utility model uses the synchronous band to feed card, with stable operation and low noise, while the positioning ribs and the adsorption magnet on the outer surface of the synchronous band can automatically lay cards and transmit card to a card pusher in the transmission on the synchronous band, to confirm accuracy.

title: Reversible thermal recording material
abstract: PURPOSE:To contrive boardening of a transparentizing temperature range, by using a specified mixture with a specified weight ratio as an organic low molecular weight substance, and incorporating a high boiling point solvent with a specified boiling point in a thermal recording layer. CONSTITUTION:A thermal recording layer is provided which comprises a resin matrix and an organic low molecular weight substance dispersed therein as main constituents and the transparency of which is reversibly varied depending on temperature. The organic low molecular weight substance is a mixture of a 16C or higher fatty acid with at least one selected from the group consisting of a 10-15C higher fatty acid, a 12C or higher alcohol, a compound of formula I (each of R1 and R2 is a 10C or higher substd. or unsubstd. alkyl or aralkyl or the like, and X is -O-, -NH-, -S- or -S-S-), a compound of formula II (R11 is a 10C or higher alkyl, and R12 is an alkyl) and formula III (R20 is hydrogen or -COR21) in a weight ratio of 95:5-20:80. A high boiling point solvent having a boiling point of at least 200 deg.C is further contained in the thermal recording layer.

title: Phase comparator using flip-flop circuits
abstract: A bistable flip-flop is set and reset respectively by two signals whose phases are to be compared and provides pulses of width determined by the phase difference. In order to prevent erroneous indication of phase synchronism when one signal frequency is an integral multiple of the other, a frequency comparator blocks the flip-flop when the frequencies are different and itself provides the said pulses, instead of the flip-flop.

title: Tillage implement with scraper/deflector
abstract: An agricultural tillage implement including a carriage frame assembly and pull hitch towed by a tractor and secondary frames supported by the carriage frame assembly. The secondary frames mount gangs of disk blades for tilling the soil in a travel direction. Bearing hanger assemblies support the gangs of disk blades below the secondary frames and deflector/scraper elements are mounted on the bearing hanger assemblies for scraping soil and residue from the disk blades and deflecting it away from the travel direction.

title: Real time telephone auction service system and method
abstract:
1. TECHNICAL FIELD OF THE INVENTION
   
   
    The present invention relates to a real-time telephone auction service system and method thereof.
   
   
    2. The technical problem to be solved by the invention
   
   
    The present invention provides a real-time telephone auction service system, a method and a program for realizing the above method for providing an auction service in real time through a wired / wireless telephone alone or in combination with an existing TV home shopping company or an internet auction site. Its purpose is to provide a computer-readable recording medium.
   
   
    3. Summary of Solution to Invention
   
   
    The present invention provides a real-time telephone auction service system, comprising: service linkage means for real-time auction service based on wired / wireless telephones; A service application means including a telephone auction service logic, connecting an auction participant with a call through the service interlocking means based on the auction participant phone number list, and providing a VXML Uniform Resource Locator (URL) when an auction participant call occurs; And requesting the VXML page of the corresponding URL from the service application means to interpret the VXML page and broadcast the auction contents, bid collection, and the highest price to the telephone auction participants through the voice recognition, speech synthesis, and DTMF collection function according to the interpreted contents. And service providing means for determining the final successful bidder.
   
   
    4. Important uses of the invention
   
   
    The present invention is used for real-time telephone auction service.
   
     
   
    Real time, telephone auction, wire / wireless telephone, VXML page, payment

title: Floating plant raft and planting device
abstract: The present invention relates to a floating planting raft and a planting device thereof. This planting raft includes a polygonal floating frame whose inside is hollow. The outer periphery has several lugs to join the adjacent planting raft. More than one planting pot is disposed in the area surrounded by the floating frames. The floating platform with different shape and area can be assembled by using the design of joining the adjacent planting raft. The floating planting raft can further cooperate with an anchor-balance device to make the floating platform stay stably at the installation position and keep the same draft. It avoids the influence resulting from the change of water level so as to efficiently satisfy the requirement of constructed wetland or landscaping.

title: Production of rigid urethane foam
abstract:
PURPOSE: To produce a rigid urethane foam having a low thermal conductivity and excellent heat-insulating properties, by mixing a specified polyether-polyol, isocyanate, blowing agent, foam stabilizer and catalyst.  
     CONSTITUTION: A compound containing at least two active hydrogen atoms (e.g., glycerol) is polymerized with propylene oxide and ethylene oxide to obtain (A) a polyether-polyol consisting of a poly(oxypropylene/oxyethylene)-polyol, oxyethylene content of the polyether chain 5W50mol%. Then, this component A is mixed with (B) an isocyanate, viscosity ≤600cP, so that the virtual mixed viscosity of components A and B is 2,000W3,000cP (20°C) and, next, mixed with (C) a blowing agent, a foam stabilizer and a catalyst, and the mixture is expanded.  
     EFFECT: It is possible to obtain a rigid urethane foam, average cell diameter ≤0.25mm, thermal conductivity ≤0.016kcal/mh°C.

title: Composition for penetration-driving oil recovery agent and penetration-driving oil recovery agent
abstract: The invention provides a composition for a penetration-driving oil recovery agent. The composition comprises the following components in percentage by weight: 0.5-45 percent of a main agent, 0.5-50 percent of an auxiliary agent and 5-99 percent of an inorganic alkali metal salt, wherein the main agent is polyacrylate, and comprises one or a combination of potassium polyacrylate, polyacrylate sodium, sodium polymethacrylate and ammonium polyacrylate; and the auxiliary agent is one or a combination of polyaspartic acid, polyaspartate and polyoxyethylene sorbitol anhydride mono-fatty acid ester. The invention further provides a penetration-driving oil recovery agent containing the composition for the penetration-driving oil recovery agent and a preparation method thereof and an application thereof in an oil recovery process. title: Quinazolinyl chalcone derivatives, and preparation method and application thereof
abstract: The invention discloses a preparation method and bioactivity of anti-plant-virus compounds-quinazolinyl chalcone derivatives. The quinazolinyl chalcone derivatives are disclosed as the following general formula. Various substituted hypnones, p-hydroxybenzaldehyde, various substituted anthranilic acids, formamide, thionyl chloride, nitromethane, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and potassium carbonate used as raw materials are subjected to four steps to synthesize the quinazolinyl chalcone derivatives. The invention also discloses high inhibiting actions of the compounds q, u, w and the like on protecting inactivating and treating cucumber mosaic virus (CMV), tobacco mosaic virus (TMV), southern rice black-streaked dwarf virus (SRBSDV) and rice stripe virus (RSV). The compounds have higher anti-plant-virus activity and can be used for preparing anti-plant-virus pesticides.

title: Magnetic-acoustic surface wave magnetic field sensor and preparation method thereof
abstract: The invention relates to a magnetic-acoustic surface wave magnetic field sensor and a preparation method thereof, and belongs to the technical field of electronic functional materials and devices. The sensor comprises a piezoelectric thin film, a magnetostrictive thin film and a substrate, wherein the magnetostrictive thin film is positioned between the piezoelectric thin film and the substrate; the thickness of the magnetostrictive thin film is 2-5 times the thickness of the piezoelectric thin film; and an interdigital transducer is arranged on the surface of the piezoelectric thin film, and the interdigital transducer and the piezoelectric thin film constitute an acoustic surface wave device. A layer of SiO2 is covered on the surface of the piezoelectric thin film and the interdigital transducer. When the sensor is in resonant state, if an external magnetic field changes, the Young's modulus of a magnetostrictive layer changes, the propagation speed of acoustic surface waves in a piezoelectric layer can be further affected and then the changes of the external magnetic field can be known by detecting the changes in resonance center frequency. The SiO2 covering layer can compensate for a frequency temperature coefficient of the piezoelectric thin film and realize the frequency temperature coefficient which is close to zero. The sensor has the characteristics of miniaturization, easiness in integration, high sensitivity, good consistency, stability and reliability.

title: Hydrogen water humidifier
abstract:
[Problem]
To provide a hydrogen water humidifier that is able to generate hydrogen water easily, and atomize the generated hydrogen water.
[Solution]
Water supplied from a water tank unit 3 to the interior of a reservoir T1 through an antibacterial cartridge 31 is electrolytically decomposed by a hydrogen water generation unit 94, immediately generating hydrogen water, the generated hydrogen water is supplied to a reservoir unit T2 and atomized by an atomization unit 95, and the atomized hydrogen water passes through a flow path 32 and a handle 34 and is sprayed from a discharge opening 41 of a discharge opening mist cover 4.

title: Performance enhanced control of DLN gas turbines
abstract: A control system and methodology for a gas turbine having multiple fuel valves for multiple combustion chambers involves tuning the fuel valves to associated combustion chambers to optimize nitrous oxide emissions; dynamic pressure, and variation in fuel/air ratio. Further, fine tuning of the fuel valves is then used to maintain dynamic pressure oscillations, nitrous oxide emissions or variation in fuel/air ratio within predetermined limits.

title: Body construction of small ship
abstract:
PURPOSE: To mount a meter panel to an easy-to-see place and perform an easy work of steering a ship, by forming higher bulwarks around an engine room cover at the stern of a small ship made of Fiber Reinforced Plastics (FRP) simultaneously applying a double construction.  
     CONSTITUTION: In a small ship made of Fiber Reinforced Plastics (FRP) where an engine room 5 is formed in its stern part, bulwarks 9 around an engine room cover 7 are formed higher than bulwarks 8 in other parts simultaneously applied a double construction in such a manner as to form an internal space between a deck 4 and the bulwarks. To the inboard side of said stern bulwarks 9 an accelerator handle 18 and meter panel 17 are mounted, and a person 19 sits on the cover 7 and is allowed to operate a steering rod by his left hand and a handle 18 by his right hand while observing the meter panel 17.

title: Workpiece centering fixture capable of being pressed tightly with own oil cylinder
abstract: The utility model relates to a workpiece centering fixture capable of being pressed tightly with an own oil cylinder. The workpiece centering fixture comprises a fixture body, a thrust ball bearing, a thrust bearing washer, a deep groove ball bearing, a clamping cone seat, a front clamping cone disk and a rear clamping cone disk, wherein a piston is arranged in the fixture body; the front end of the piston is provided with a guide bushing seat and a compressed spring; a guide bushing is arranged at the front end of the guide bushing seat; through the clamping motion produced by the rear end of the fixture, a workpiece is clamped through the front clamping cone disk and the rear clamping cone disk; the piston is used for feeding oil through a hydraulic oil port P, and discharging oil through a T oil port; through the oil passage in the fixture body, the piston moves backwards to drive the guide bushing seat and the guide bushing to move backwards, thereby tightly closing to the end surface of the workpiece, and realizing the guiding function of a drill hole. As the front clamping cone disk and the rear clamping cone disk are adopted, and the own oil cylinder can be pressed tightly and can be used for ejecting the centering fixture structure of the workpiece, the centering requirement of the workpiece is ensured, and the workpiece can be separated with the front clamping cone disk, and moreover, the workpiece centering fixture has the advantages of simple structure, convenient in operation and the like.

title: Scanned electron image observing apparatus
abstract:
PURPOSE: To enable both electron signal amount to be adjusted without using exclusive high voltage power source by arranging a detector for detecting the scattered electron and transmitted electron from a sample and arranging a projection lense between the sample and a transmitted electron detector and connecting said lens with a variable DC source.  
     CONSTITUTION: Upper sample 4, a secondary or radiation electron detector 10 is arranged, and directly under a slit 6, a detector 11 for transmitted electron is arranged, and both detectors 10 and 11 are connected in common with a DC voltage source 12, and the gain is controlled in common by a gain controller 13. Under the sample 4, a projection lens 5 is arranged, and the electron which passed through the sample  if focused to form image in the vicinity of above the slit 6. The projection lens  5 is connected with a current controller 8 through a current amplifier 7, and the  position of image formation due to the transmited electron is varied, and thus the amount of electrons which pass through the slit 6 can be controlled. Thus, the exclusive use of high voltage power source for the transmitted electron edetector is not required, and the signal amounts of both electrons can be adjusted only by varying the excitation of the projection lens. 
     COPYRIGHT: (C)1980,JPO&Japio

title: Ethernet communication method and system and SDN exchanger
abstract: The invention discloses an Ethernet communication method and system and an SDN exchanger. The method comprises the steps that the SDN exchanger reports the MAC address learned locally to an integrated controller, and the integrated controller issues forwarding tables of forwarding ports corresponding to target MAC addresses to all SDN exchangers under a management domain based on whole network topology automatic finding and route computing based on strategies; and the SDN exchangers achieve Ethernet frame forwarding according to the MAC address forwarding tables learned locally and the MAC address forwarding tables issued by the integrated controller. The integrated controller issues the MAC address forwarding tables to the SCN exchangers in the management domain, the boundedness that a traditional Ethernet exchanger learns whole network MAC addresses through a data forwarding plane is avoided, and the problems that broadcast storm and spanning tree protocols exist in the traditional Ethernet, so that a large amount of redundancy chain blocking is caused, network convergence speed is slow, and traffic engineering cannot be achieved are solved.

title: Coolant Distributor for Heat Exchanger and Method for manufacturing the same
abstract:
The present invention relates to a refrigerant distributor for a heat exchanger, to minimize the generation of vortex inside the distributor to minimize the generation of noise and to ensure that the refrigerant is uniformly supplied through each refrigerant pipe.
   
   
    To this end, the present invention, the hollow body through which the refrigerant flows; An inlet formed at one end of the body to introduce a refrigerant; A plurality of through holes formed on opposite sides of the inlet; A plurality of refrigerant pipes inserted into the body through the through holes to discharge the refrigerant; It provides a refrigerant distributor for a heat exchanger comprising a rod-shaped equalization member installed in the longitudinal direction of the body in the central portion of the inside of the body.

abstract: A disposable package having a plurality of flexible-wall synthetic-resin receptacles, e.g., milk or other bulk-material receptacles of the bag type (nonselfsupporting or collapsible), consists of a heat-sealed skin drawn snugly around the receptacle. title: Anti-tip wheelchair
abstract: A manually-powered wheelchair that includes one or more features that enhance the overall maneuverability and stability of the wheelchair, such as centrally mounted drive-wheels and an anti-tip system. Other embodiments may include at least one of an adjustable anti-tip system that may be selectively located to several locations on the frame; a positive stop device attached to either the mounting bracket, frame, or the anti-tip system for limiting movement of the anti-tip system and the frame relative to each other; a resistance device attached to the frame and the anti-tip system for increasing resistance between the frame and the anti-tip system when the user leans backwards in the wheelchair; a wheel lock system engaging at least one of the drive wheels below the wheel's axis of rotation; and a tilt prevention device for selectively preventing any rotation of the wheelchair frame and anti-tip system relative to each other.

title: Polyether-ether-ketone insulating electrical penetration assembly wire for nuclear power station
abstract: The invention discloses a polyether-ether-ketone insulating electrical penetration assembly wire for a nuclear power station. The polyether-ether-ketone insulating electrical penetration assembly wire comprises a conductor, a polyether-ether-ketone insulating layer is arranged on the outside of the conductor in an extruding mode, an arc filling block is attached to the outside of the polyether-ether-ketone insulating layer, the polyether-ether-ketone insulating layer is wrapped with an outer arc wire, the arc filling block is provided with a plurality of drainage holes, drainage lines are arranged in the drainage holes, an aramid yarn layer is arranged on the outer side of the arc filing block and the outer side of the outer arc wire in a knitted mode, an outer conductor is arranged on the outside of the aramid yarn layer, and an outer polyether-ether-ketone sheath is arranged on the outside of the outer conductor in an extruding mode. The polyether-ether-ketone insulating electrical penetration assembly wire is simple in structure, has excellent mechanical properties and electrical properties, and has excellent fireproof performance, good creep resistance and self-extinguishment by the adoption of modified polyether-ether-ketone materials, extremely little smog and poisonous gas are released even under a fire situation, and the modified polyether-ether-ketone materials can bear 109 rads and are suitable for the nuclear power field and other irradiation resistant fields.

title: Health-care curative dress integrated with multi-position electrothermal compress blocks and medicinal blocks
abstract: The utility model relates to a health-care curative garment integrated with multi-position electric heating compress blocks and medicinal blocks, which is a novel health-care product. The health-care curative garment is formed by independent electric heating compress blocks and medicinal blocks in different shapes for a head region, an anterior region of neck, a back humeral region, an anterior region of chest, a lumbosacral region, an abdominal region, etc., wherein, the electric heating compress block is manufactured by electric heating hot materials arranged in a plus material bag and the medicinal block is manufactured by traditional Chinese medicine in the plus material bag; the medicinal block is inlaid in the electric heating compress block; through a control switch, each electric heating compress block can carry out electric heating compress action on each active region of the machine body simultaneously, respectively, orderly or in a way of combination, and the medicinal compress can be simultaneously carried out in the processes of the electric heating compress to obtain the goal of health care and therapy.

title: Electromagnetically controlled drive system and method
abstract: An electromagnetically controlled drive system for accessing a data storage medium comprises an optical signal generator and a reflector element adapted to receive an optical signal from the optical signal generator. The reflector element is also adapted to direct the optical signal toward the data storage medium. The system also comprises an electromagnetic element adapted to generate an electromagnetic field proximate to the reflector element. The reflector element is adapted to respond to the electromagnetic field to move the optical signal relative to the data storage medium in response to a change in the electromagnetic field.

title: Method for monitoring resource
abstract: The present invention discloses resource monitoring method, configuring call limit threshold. Said method includes A, after existing bearing establishment successful or bearing dismantling successful service, calculating current IP channel total occupying resource; B, judging current IP channel total occupying resource whether exceeding call limit threshold, if exceed then determining IP channel as congestion state, otherwise determining current IP channel non-congestion state. Said invented method realizes media gateway monitoring oneself bearing resource, to make network service provider capable of in time obtaining bearing resource congestion status, and then to make adjusting according to resource congestion status. Said invention makes admission controlling to congestion generating IP channel, ensuring quality of service.

title: Hybrid vehicle, control method for hybrid vehicle, and controller for hybrid vehicle
abstract: A hybrid vehicle includes an internal combustion engine, a rotating electric machine, an electric storage device, a power supply device, and a controller. The controller executes switching control to switch from a first electric power supply to a second electric power supply by starting the internal combustion engine. The first electric power supply is the supply of electric power from the electric storage device to the electric device. The second electric power supply is the supply of electric power from the rotating electric machine to the electric device. The controller controls the power supply device and the internal combustion engine such that the internal combustion engine is started with the first electric power supply being continued during the switching control.

title: New acetals containing epoxy groups
abstract: The invention comprises acetals containing at least two epoxide groups and of the formula: <FORM:0951399/C2/1> in which R1 to R9 each represent a hydrogen atom or a monovalent substituent such as a halogen atom, an alkoxy group or an aliphatic, cycloaliphatic, araliphatic or aromatic hydrocarbon residue, and R1 and R5 together may also represent an alkylene radical such as a methylene group; #Z represents a trivalent aliphatic, cycloaliphatic or araliphatic hydrocarbon residue with at least 3 carbon atoms, none of which has more than a single free valence, and no carbon having such a free valence carries a hydroxyl group; n is 1 or 2; and, when n is 1, G is a radical of formula -#O-#U1 or -#O-C#O-#U1, where #U1 is an organic radical containing an epoxide group and, when n is 2, G is a radical of the formula -#O-, -#O-C#O-#O-, -#O-C#O-C#O-#O, or -#OC#O-#U2-#O-C#O-, where #U2 is an aliphatic, cycloaliphatic, araliphatic, or aromatic hydrocarbon radical.  The epoxy compounds may be obtained by epoxidation of the compounds having a formula: <FORM:0951399/C2/2> in which when n is 1, G1 is -#O#U1 1 or -#OC#O#U1 1 where #U1 1 is an organic radical containing an epoxidisable group, such as a double bond or halohydrin grouping.  The double bonds may be epoxidised with a peracid such as peracetic, perbenzoic, peradipic or mono-perphthalic acid, or by reaction with H#OCI followed by treatment with strong alkali.  The unsaturated starting materials (IV) may be made by acetalising a D 3-tetra-hydrobenzaldehyde with a polyalcohol (H#O)2#Z-#C, where #C may be #O#H, Cl or Br., in the presence of an acid catalyst, or with an epoxide #O = #Z-#C.  The product is an acetal of formula: <FORM:0951399/C2/3> This acetal may be converted to (IV) by etherification, esterification or re-esterification with a compound containing one or more reactive halogen atoms or groups containing active hydrogen atoms.  Suitable types of compounds are epihalohydrins, dihalohydrins, unsaturated alcohols, glycerol diesters of unsaturated fatty acids, unsaturated carboxylic acids and functional derivatives, unsaturated halides, unsaturated acetals, carbonic acid, phosgene, and polycarboxylic acids, their acid halides or anhydrides. The acetals (IV) may also be made by acetalising the D 3-tetrahydrobenzaldehyde with a polyol of the formula (#H#O)2 #Z-G-#Z(#O#H)2, e.g., diglycerol, or with a dialcohol (#H#O)2 #Z-G1 such as an adduct of glycerol with dicyclopentadiene, or glycerol-monoallyl ether with cyclo-pentadiene. The preferred compounds of the invention are those of the formula: <FORM:0951399/C2/4> where R and R1 each represents hydrogen or C1-4 alkyl; Y2 is an aliphatic, cycloaliphatic, araliphatic or aromatic hydrocarbon residue, and m and n are each 1 or 2, and those of the formula: <FORM:0951399/C2/5> in which R and R1 each represents hydrogen or C1-4 alkyl. Specification 932,960 is referred to.ALSO:Curable mixtures comprise at least one acetal of the formula: <FORM:0951399/C3/1> wherein R1 to R9 are hydrogen or monovalent substituents such as halogen, alkoxy or aliphatic cycloaliphatic, araliphatic or aromatic hydrocarbon residues, and R1 and R5 together may also represent an alkylene radical such as a methylene group; Z is a trivalent aliphatic, cycloaliphatic or araliphatic hydrocarbon residue with at least 3 carbon atoms, none of which has more than a single free valence, and no carbon having such a free valence carries a hydroxyl group; n is 1 or 2, and, when n is 1, G is a radical of formula -O-Y1- or -O-CO-Y1, where Y1 is an organic radical containing an epoxide group and, when n is 2, G is a radical of the formula -O-, -O-CO-O, -O-CO-CO-O-, or -OCO -Y2-OCO-, where Y2 is an aliphatic, cycloaliphatic, araliphatic or aromatic radical, together with a curing agent for epoxy resins. The curing agent is preferably an anhydride of a dicarboxylic, or polycarboxylic acid.  Other suitable curing agents are amines and amides, dicyandiamides, formaldehyde resins with aniline, urea and melamine; polymers of aminostyrenes, polyamides, isocyanates, isothiocyanates, polyhydric phenols, phenol-aldehyde resins, reaction products of aluminium alcoholates and phenolates with compounds of tautomeric reaction, Friedel-Crafts catalysts, metal fluoborates and phosphoric acid.  An accelerator such as a tertiary amine or a strong Lewis base and advantageously also a polyhydroxy compound, may be added.  The curable mixtures may also contain a proportion of the above acetals whose epoxide groups have been wholly or partially hydrolysed to hydroxyl groups, and/or also other cross-linking polyhydroxy compounds, such as hexanetriol. Other epoxides, such as mono-glycidyl or polyglycidyl ethers of mono- or polyalcohols, or of mono- or polyphenols, polyglycidyl esters of polycarboxylic acids, or amino-polyepoxides.  Fillers, plasticizers and colouring matters may be added.  Specification 932,960 is referred to.

title: Improvements in valves or stop cocks
abstract: 625,110. Valves. TOMPKINS & CO., Ltd., J., and TOMPKINS, A. E. May 21, 1946, No. 15279. [Class 135] In a valve of the kind comprising a body 1 with coaxial inlet and outlet orifices 2, 3 and a pistonlike valve head 13 cooperating with a valve seat 4 and guided in a pillar or extension 5 of the valve body which supports a screw-threaded valve operating stem 9, leakage around the stem is prevented or minimized by a concentric flange or sleeve 15 on the valve head telescopically engaging a deep annular recess 16 in the extension 5 to form a labyrinth or circuitous passage for the fluid. In the fully-open position a machined surface 17 on the valve head seals against the base 18 of the pillar 5. The valve head 13 is permanently secured to the stem 9 by a screwthreaded plug 22 which clamps a shoulder 20 against a flange 19 in the valve head. Lubricant is introduced into the chamber 16 by a radial hole 5a which may be closed by a grub-screw.

title: Namespace mapping structual adjustment in non-volatile memory devices
abstract: A computer storage device having a host interface, a controller, non-volatile storage media, and firmware. The firmware instructs the controller to: allocate a named portion of the non-volatile storage device; generate, according to a first block size, first block-wise mapping data; translate, using the first block-wise mapping data, logical addresses defined in the named portion to logical addresses defined for the entire non-volatile storage media, which can then be further translated to physical addresses in a same way for all named portions; determine a second block size; generate, according to the second block size, second block-wise mapping data; translate, using the second block-wise mapping data, the logical addresses defined in the name portion to the logical addresses defined for the entire non-volatile storage media.

title: Overload detector for ac filter
abstract:
PURPOSE: To judge an overload state by providing a filter passing frequency components to be absorbed in an AC filter, means for sampling the output signal therefrom and means for determining the effective value of sampled data, and then comparing thus determined effective value with a predetermined value.  
     CONSTITUTION: A current transformer CT is connected with the earth wire of an AC filter circuit 7 and the overload state of the AC filter is detected based on an output signal from the current transformer CT. A filter 2 passes such frequency components of the output current from an auxiliary CT as to be absorbed by the AC filter 7. A sample & hold circuit 3 samples the output signals from the filter 2 at a predetermined time interval. A processing circuit 5 determines the effective value and when it exceeds a preset value, a judgment is made that an overload state has occurred and a detection signal is outputted. According to the constitution, heating of the AC filter is determined through operation and steep temperature rise is detected quickly, resulting in protection of the AC filter.

title: Electronic money handling device and electronic money recording medium used therein
abstract: (57) [Summary] [Problem] To easily set a personal limit on an electronic money card, improve operability at the time of electronic money replenishment operation, and prevent overpayment of electronic money due to incorrect input. And an electronic money card used for the same. SOLUTION: An electronic money card insertion slot 122, means 117 to 118 for setting a personal limit (a personal limit storage unit 135) in the electronic money card 130 inserted into the insertion slot 122,   It has replenishment instruction means 119 for instructing replenishment of electronic money. Based on the replenishment instruction by the replenishment instruction means, replenish the electronic money from the account to the electronic money card 130 until the personal limit is reached, replenish the amount exceeding the individual limit, and further add the individual to the electronic money card 130. When electronic money exceeding the limit is stored (remaining amount storage unit 136), the excess amount is automatically deposited in an account.

title: Wiper link rod
abstract: (57) [Problem] To provide a link rod of a wiper capable of suppressing noise generated in a vehicle cabin due to a vibration at the time of operation generated from a drive source being transmitted to a vehicle body side. A link rod transmits a driving force by connecting a wiper motor and a pivot lever of a pivot shaft. A weight 38 is integrally fixed to the link rod 10, and has a configuration in which the natural frequency is shifted to a lower frequency band. Accordingly, the vibration transmission characteristic is changed by moving the vibration damping effect region, in particular can be further significantly reduced by 1000H  Z  vibration damping effect region the vibration transmissibility of the frequency range in the vicinity generated from the wiper motor. In addition, since the natural frequency is shifted to a lower frequency band, the sound becomes difficult for a person to perceive, and as a result, the noise of the entire system can be reduced.

title: Novel vapor collecting and discharging device
abstract: The utility model discloses a novel vapor collecting and discharging device which comprises a roof of an aluminum oxide producing workshop. A main discharging pipe is arranged in the middle of the roof and comprises a roof internal discharging pipe and a roof external discharging pipe, a cone-shaped top and a pull rod capable of being connected with the roof and stabilizing the main discharging pipe are arranged on the roof external discharging pipe, an auxiliary discharging pipe and a flange capable of being connected with the roof are arranged on the roof internal discharging pipe, and a rib plate capable of being connected with the roof is arranged at the bottom of the auxiliary discharging pipe. The novel vapor collecting and discharging device not only has the advantages of being simple in structure and convenient to use, but also has the advantages of being ingenious in design, safe, reliable, convenient to maintain and capable of effectively controlling vapor discharging content, reducing pollution and the like.

title: Method for removing nitrogen present in ammonia
abstract: A method for removing nitrogen present in ammonia comprises the following steps: ammonia is provided, for example by filtration of ammonia- containing gas or by bringing that gas into contact with water and removing the ammonia dissolved therein by heating. The ammonia is heated to a temperature at which the ammonia decomposes; the mixture of nitrogen and hydrogen thus obtained is admixed with a limited amount of oxygen, as a result of which the mixture obtained burns to form water vapour and nitrogen. According to the invention, use is made of the known fact that ammonia gas, if heated to a suitable temperature, can be decomposed into a mixture of three parts hydrogen and one part nitrogen.

title: The copper-deposited silicon powders, their preparation method, and lithium secondary batteries containing the above materials as the anode
abstract:
The present invention relates to a silicon powder in which copper is deposited, a method for preparing the same, and a lithium ion secondary battery using the same as a negative electrode active material. 
     The present invention not only solves the problem of deterioration of the electrode due to the low conductivity and volume expansion of the conventional silicon powder by uniformly electroless deposition of copper on the silicon surface in powder state, and uses lithium as a negative electrode active material. Ion secondary batteries can provide excellent negative electrode characteristics, long term cycle stability and high capacity storage.

title: Laminate for balloon
abstract: PURPOSE:To obtain a transparent laminate for balloon having excellent barrier properties to helium gas and hydrogen gas by a structure wherein thin film transparent layer made of silicon oxide having a specified ratio of silicon atom to oxygen atom is provided on one side of plastic base material. CONSTITUTION:As the transparent plastic base material concerned, from the view point of required gas barrier properties, adherence property of vapor deposited layer, toughness, specific gravity and the like, polyester film, nylon film, EVOH film or the like is especially preferable. The thin film transparent layer is provided on the plastic base material with or without undercoat layer. The silicon oxide in the thin film transparent layer has the preferable ratio of silicon atom to oxygen atom of 1:0.3-2. especially 1:0.7-1.9. For the formation of the thin film transparent layer of the silicon oxide, vacuum deposition method, physical deposition method such as sputtering method, ion-plating method and the like, chemical deposition or the like is preferably used.

title: Method of forming a phase-changeable layer and method of manufacturing a semiconductor memory device using the same
abstract: A phase-changeable layer and a method of forming the same are disclosed. In the method, a first hydrogen gas is introduced into a reaction chamber into which a substrate is loaded at a first flow rate to form first plasma. A primary cyclic CVD process is carried out using precursors in the reaction chamber to form a lower phase-changeable layer having a first grain size on the substrate. A second hydrogen gas is introduced into the reaction chamber at a second flow rate less than the first flow rate to form second plasma. A secondary cyclic CVD process is carried out using the precursors in the reaction chamber to form an upper phase-changeable layer having a second grain size smaller than the first grain size on the substrate, thereby forming a phase-changeable layer. Thus, the phase-changeable layer may have strong adhesion strength with respect to a lower layer and good electrical characteristics.

title: Si alloy powder for negative electrode of lithium-ion secondary cell, and method for manufacturing same
abstract: Provided is a Si alloy powder for the negative electrode active material of a lithium-ion secondary cell having a high discharge capacity and an excellent cycle lifespan. A method for manufacturing the powder is also provided. This Si alloy powder has a eutectic structure comprising a Si phase and a CrSi2 phase, and has an average thickness of 4 [mu]m or less in the direction of the narrower width in each of the two phases, that is, the Si phase and the CrSi2 phase. The Si alloy powder is manufactured by causing a molten starting material capable of forming the composition of the Si alloy powder to solidify by rapid cooling at a cooling speed of 100 C/s of greater.

title: Sludge fuel, its production, treatment of sludge and closed-circuit treatment
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide renewable and clean sludge fuel made of sludge having high moisture content, difficult to spontaneously burn and requiring much labor in its treatment, to provide a method for producing the same fuel and to provide a method for treatment sludge. SOLUTION: Sludge is cyclically treated in such a way that a treating raw material A consisting of dehydrated sludge cake prepared by the dehydration of sludge such as sewage, shop sludge, or the like, aluminum powder, ammonium nitrate and a small quantity of surfactant is kneaded by an extruder 1 to form sludge fuel B, the fuel B spontaneously burns when ignited to generate aluminum oxide and hydrogen gas and the hydrogen gas is burnt in a fuel cell 3 to be drawn out as electricity, which is used for repurifying aluminum oxide into aluminum for the sludge fuel B.

title: Method of preparing a nanoporous nickel phosphate molecular sieve
abstract: The present invention relates to a process for preparing nanoporous VSB-5 molecular sieves, and more particularly, to a process for preparing nanoporous VSB-5 molecular sieves useful as a hydrogen storage material, a catalyst, a catalyst supporter and an adsorbent, performed in such a manner that low-cost inorganic base or monoamine instead of a template essential in the conventional process is, as a pH modifier, incorporated into raw materials comprised of nickel and phosphorous compounds and crystallized to give VSB-5 molecular sieves in an economical and efficient manner.

title: Method for effectively utilizing surplus power of power generation facility depending on time zone
abstract: The present invention relates to a method for effectively using surplus power according to a time zone of a power plant, and preventing adverse effects of waste on the environment by circulating use of by-products in the system. When there is surplus power in a power generation facility, an electrolytic activation liquid is produced from an aqueous solution, and when electric power is required, the electrolytic activation liquid is used to produce hydrogen gas to generate electric power. The apparatus includes an electrolytic activation apparatus for aqueous solution (apparatus A), a retention apparatus for electrolytically activated aqueous solution (apparatus B), a hydrogen gas generation apparatus (apparatus C) by electrolysis, a hydrogen gas retention apparatus (apparatus D), and hydrogen gas. Consists of power conversion equipment (device E). In order to purify the aqueous solution to be circulated, powder or molding containing Hakushu soil is added. As an auxiliary means for preparing the electrolytic activation liquid, beads that generate terahertz region waves are added to the liquid. [Selection] Figure 1

title: Method for producing flame by burner i.e. gas burner, to produce hot water or heat in toilet, involves supplying flammable fluid to reaction chamber edged by material layer, and supplying quantity of water to heated material layer
abstract: This method of producing flame by a burner (2) comprises the following steps: a) supplying a flammable fluid into a reaction chamber (100) bordered by a layer of material (80) formed by a network of wires or by a porous element, b) initiating the combustion of the flammable fluid, c) maintaining the combustion until the layer of material (80) reaches a temperature greater than or equal to 700 ° C, and d) supplying a quantity of liquid water in the heated material layer (80).

title: High heat conductive insulting coil and rotating electric machine
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a high heat conductive insulating coil having a high heat conductivity and a high voltage resistance, and a rotating electric machine using the same. SOLUTION: Mica grains, filling grains having a heat conductivity of at least 5 W/mK or higher, and a reinforcing material (glass clothes) on which the mica grains and filling grains are placed are laminated. A resin is filled into the gaps between the mica grains, the filling grains and the reinforcing material, and the lamination is pressurized to form both a mica layer 1 consisting of the mica grains and the resin, and a layer 6 consisting of the filling grains, the reinforcing materials, and the resin, so that a high heat conductive mica insulating layer (mica insulating tape) 12 is formed. The insulating layer 12 is wound around a coil conductor 5 for an insulating treatment, wherein the heat conductivity of the resin is 0.6 W/mK or higher.  As a result, the insulating layer of the coil is provided with both high heat conductivity and high voltage resistance in the direction of the thickness of the layer, which makes possible to provide a small size, high-power rotating electric machine or a high power rotating electric machine employing an indirect cooling method to cool the machine by circulating air or hydrogen gas on the outside of the coil insulating layer.

title: Atomic layer deposition of tungsten materials
abstract: Embodiments of the invention provide a method for depositing tungsten-containing materials. In one embodiment, a method includes forming a tungsten nucleation layer over an underlayer disposed on the substrate while sequentially providing a tungsten precursor and a reducing gas into a process chamber during an atomic layer deposition (ALD) process and depositing a tungsten bulk layer over the tungsten nucleation layer, wherein the reducing gas contains hydrogen gas and a hydride compound (e.g., diborane) and has a hydrogen/hydride flow rate ratio of about 500:1 or greater. In some examples, the method includes flowing the hydrogen gas into the process chamber at a flow rate within a range from about 1 slm to about 20 slm and flowing a mixture of the hydride compound and a carrier gas into the process chamber at a flow rate within a range from about 50 sccm to about 500 sccm.

title: Improvements in electrolysis systems and the availability of over-unity energy
abstract: A looped energy system for the generation of excess energy available to do work is disclosed. The system comprises an electrolysis cell unit (150) receiving a supply of water to liberate separated hydrogen gas (154) and oxygen (156) by electrolysis driven by a DC voltage (152) applied across respective anodes and cathodes of the cell unit (150). A hydrogen gas receiver (158) receives and stores hydrogen gas liberated by the cell unit (150), and an oxygen gas receiver (160) receives and stores oxygen gas liberated by the cell unit (150). A gas expansion device (162) expands the stored gases to recover expansion work, and a gas combustion device (168) mixes and combusts the expanded hydrogen gas and oxygen gas to recover combusted work. A proportion of the sum of the expansion work and the combustion work sustains electrolysis of the cell unit to retain operational gas pressure in the gas receivers (158, 160) such that the energy system is self-sustaining, and there is excess energy available from the sum of energies.

title: Electrochemical cell
abstract: A sodium electrochemical cell that supports a current density at the negative electrode of at least 500 μA/cm 2 , the electrochemical cell comprising (i) a negative electrode and (ii) a sodium-ion ionic liquid electrolyte having a sodium-ion concentration that is no less than 75% of its saturation limit in the electrolyte, wherein the negative electrode has a solid- electrolyte interphase (SEI) layer formed as a result of the electrochemical cell having undergone a polarisation cycle.

title: Use of a salt mixture as an additive in a lithium-gel battery
abstract: The invention relates to the simultaneous use of a first salt comprising a nitrate anion (NO   3   -) and a second salt comprising an anion other than nitrate, wherein at least one of the first and second salts is a lithium salt, as ionic conductivity promoters in a rechargeable lithium-metal-gel battery. The invention also relates to a lithium-gel battery comprising a mixture of said first salt and said second salt, to a non-aqueous gel electrolyte comprising such a mixture, and to a positive electrode for a lithium battery comprising said mixture.

title: Additional acceleration method of ram-jet and warhead
abstract: PURPOSE:To arrange a bullet core to be launched at a very fast speed by melting or evaporating a servo in a bullet with a high temperature combustible gas in a muzzle when launching the bullet and guiding the high temperature combustible gas near the bullet core and causing the core to be exploded and accelerating its speed. CONSTITUTION:In a process which accelerates a warhead with a high temperature combustible gas 10 which comprises a high pressure hydrogen gas, when a servo 31 is being melted with the heat of this high temperature combustible gas 10, there is generated a clearance between the outer surface of the servo 31 and a muzzle 11a. The high temperature combustible gas is guided near a peripheral surface 6a of a bullet core by way of the clearance and comes in contact with an air 33 on the side of a front 6c. At this very moment, the high temperature combustible gas 10 and oxygen in the air 33 cause explosion, thereby generating heat so that the servo 31 may be evaporated perfectly. In addition to that, the high temperature vapor produced by the reaction between the hydrogen and the oxygen causes reduction reaction with the servo 31 where its water content is high-pressurized. This high pressure force accelerates the accelerating bullet core 6 so that it may be launched at a very fast speed. The front 6c of the bullet core 6 and its tail 6b are cone-shaped.

title: Preparation of malonic acid dialkyl ester
abstract:
PURPOSE: To improve the rate of reaction and the space time yield in the preparation of a malonic acid dialkyl ester by the reaction of a monohalogenoacetic acid  ester with CO and a lower aliphatic alcohol, by adding H 2  gas to the reaction gas,  and recycling the gas.  
     CONSTITUTION: A malonic acid dialkyl ester is prepared by reacting a monohalogenoacetic acid alkyl ester with CO and a lower aliphatic alcohol in the presence of a cobalt carbonyl catalyst and a basic substance (e.g. an alkali metal carbonate). In the above process, the reaction is carried out by adding 10W70% of hydrogen gas to the reaction gas, and purging a part of the gas after reaction while recycling the remaining gas to the inlet of the reactor. Since the process is simple and uses water gas available at a relatively low cost, as a raw material, it is extremely economical.

title: System and method for preparing solvent oil
abstract: The invention discloses a system and a method for preparing solvent oil under low energy consumption, and belongs to the field of hydrogenation reactions. The system comprises a hydrogen storage tank, a material storage tank, a hydrogenation reactor, a first heat exchanger, a second heat exchanger, a heating furnace, a heat high-pressure separate tank, an air cooler, a hydrogenation compressor and a feeding pump; the hydrogenation reactor comprises a hydrogenation reactor inlet, a hydrodesulfurization catalyst bed layer, a hydrogen distributor arranged at the lower portion of the hydrodesulfurization catalyst bed layer, a hydrogenation and unsaturated hydrocarbon removal catalyst bed layer arranged below the hydrogen distributor, and a hydrogenation reactor outlet; the second heat exchanger is arranged at the lower portion of the hydrodesulfurization catalyst bed layer and the upper portion of the hydrogen distributor; the material storage tank is connected with the inlet of the feeding pump, and the hydrogen storage tank is connected with the outlet of the feeding pump and the hydrogen distributor; the feeding pump, the first heat exchanger, the second heat exchanger, the heating furnace and the inlet of the hydrogenation reactor are sequentially connected; and the outlet of the hydrogenation reactor, the first heat exchanger, the heat high-pressure separate tank, the air cooler, the hydrogen compressor and the feeding pump are sequentially connected.

title: Amorphous thin film solar cell
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To improve the open terminal voltage and a curve factor and to hence improve the conversion efficiency of a solar cell by setting the supply amount of atomic hydrogen to a first thick substantially intrinsic amorphous silicon thin film to a specific range value. SOLUTION: Hydrogen is supplied, pressure is controlled, and a film growth is similarly regulated to 150Torr. RF is discharged. The RF output density at this time is 0.64W/cm<2> . It is exposed with hydrogen plasma for a predetermined time, again discharge is stopped, the flow of hydrogen gas is stopped, and evacuated in vacuum until it reaches 10<-6> Torr or less similarly to that at the time of finishing the film growing step. The series of the steps are repeated, this operation is repeated until a predetermined film thickness is obtained. The film characteristics are largely varied according to the supply amount of atomic hydrogen. The supply amount of the atomic hydrogen for effecting the reduction of defect density is 1×10<15> pieces/cm<2> to 5×10<15> pieces/cm<2> . Thus, the quality of the amorphous silicon thin film can be enhanced.

title: ALVEOLAR SOLID COMPOSITE MATERIAL COMPRISING METAL NANOPARTICLES, PROCESS FOR PREPARATION AND USES FOR THE REVERSIBLE STORAGE OF HYDROGEN
abstract: The present invention relates to a macroporous monolithic composite material, in particular to a carbon monolith with a hierarchical porous structure comprising metallic nanoparticles, and to its preparation process, to a hydrogen storage method implementing it. , to a process for producing gaseous hydrogen using such a composite material, said process being reversible.

title: Natural mineral alkali liquid and the way to produce hydrogen gas by aluminum oxide
abstract:
The problem of the present invention is to solve the problems of the prior art, and as a food additive, a citric acid crystal powder is added as a solvent that dissolves calcium powder and powder of shells of incinerated clams containing antibacterial and sterilizing effects. It is mixed with deep seawater high mineral ED water (ED water) containing more than 80 kinds of high mineral ingredients in a raw material as a basic raw material, and it is catalyzed by aluminum (alumina) using various multifunctional inorganic strong alkali high mineral water solution. It is a method for producing an aqueous alkali (PH12.5 or more) aqueous solution for hydrogen gas generation. 
     In other words, in view of the conventional problem, citric acid crystal fine powder (citric acid) is added to natural natural multifunctional porous crushed plastic clam powder (calcium: food additive) which is completely inorganic. As a fusion welding, it is a mixed, slit, and gelled solvent, and ED water, which is a high-mineralized, deep sea water, is synthesized by mixing effective functions, harmless to human body above PH12.5, and excellent in natural environment. It is possible to dispose as a harmless aqueous solution. An object of the present invention is to provide a strong alkaline aqueous solution for generating hydrogen gas using aluminum (alumina) as a catalyst.

title: Method and apparatus for producing sodium borohydride
abstract: Provided are a method and device for producing sodium borohydride, having excellent energy efficiency and production efficiency. Sodium metaborate and powdered aluminum are reacted in a hydrogen atmosphere while being rolled and ground by a grinding medium, and sodium borohydride is obtained, using a production device (20) provided with a cylindrical reaction container (21), a cylindrical reaction part (22) in which sodium metaborate as a first starting material (1) and powdered aluminum are accommodated together with a grinding medium (2), and a hydrogen introduction part (23) for introducing hydrogen gas directly to the reaction part (22) or to the reaction part (22) via the reaction container (21).

title: Catalytic effects of oxygen carrier based chemical-looping reforming of ch4 with co2
abstract: The invention relates to CeO 2  and La 2 O 3  for catalyzing Fe 2 O 3 —Al 2 O 3  based chemical-looping reforming of CH 4  with CO 2  (CL-DRM). The reaction performance of all the composite oxygen carriers was evaluated in a fixed-bed reactor at atmospheric pressure condition. The influencing factors, including temperature and time-on-stream (TOS) were investigated. The characteristics of the oxygen carriers were checked with Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) analysis and X-ray diffraction (XRD). The reducibility of the composite materials was elucidated with temperature-programmed reduction by CH 4  (CH 4 -TPR). Preliminary experimental observations suggest that the simultaneous presence of CeO 2  and La 2 O 3  can not only enhance the reactivity of Fe 2 O 3 —Al 2 O 3  toward CH 4  oxidation and its oxygen releasing rate for fast reaction kinetics, but also improve the reactivity of its reduced form toward CO 2  splitting.
text: Catalytic effects of oxygen carrier based chemical-looping reforming of ch4 with co2 The invention relates to CeO 2 and La 2 O 3 for catalyzing Fe 2 O 3 —Al 2 O 3 based chemical-looping reforming of CH 4 with CO 2 (CL-DRM). The reaction performance of all the composite oxygen carriers was evaluated in a fixed-bed reactor at atmospheric pressure condition. The influencing factors, including temperature and time-on-stream (TOS) were investigated. The characteristics of the oxygen carriers were checked with Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) analysis and X-ray diffraction (XRD). The reducibility of the composite materials was elucidated with temperature-programmed reduction by CH 4 (CH 4 -TPR). Preliminary experimental observations suggest that the simultaneous presence of CeO 2 and La 2 O 3 can not only enhance the reactivity of Fe 2 O 3 —Al 2 O 3 toward CH 4 oxidation and its oxygen releasing rate for fast reaction kinetics, but also improve the reactivity of its reduced form toward CO 2 splitting.

title: Modification method and application of metal organic framework material
abstract: The application discloses a method for modifying a metal organic framework material, which comprises the following steps: carrying out in-situ modification on metal nodes of the metal organic framework material; wherein the modified substance subjected to in-situ modification contains at least one of carbonyl, amino and nitrogen. According to the method, carbonyl and nitrogen (amino) group in-situ modification is carried out on metal nodes, so that the hydrophobic and anti-water vapor interference capabilities of the porous organic framework adsorbent material are improved, and the method is suitable for trapping and adsorption separation of weakly adsorbed mixed components at a certain humidity.

title: Functional material and its production process and use
abstract: The invention relates to a novel composite of silica modified by a plurality of organic functional groups, the same composite contains a plurality of sulfydryl, sulfide, thiourea and other functional groups, the functional groups exist in different oligomers, configurations and stereochemical arrays, and the affinity and the selectivity to target metal and specific targets are obviously enhanced. In combination with different structural configurations further improve the ability to bind various forms of targets in actual process streams, product streams, and waste streams, multiple binding mechanisms achieve high levels of purification and selective removal of targets. The use of the compound comprises: product purification, removal of unwanted organic and inorganic compounds (including metals and metal complexes) from product streams, process streams and waste streams, selective removal of target components from a mixture in which multiple components coexist, use as chromatographic media for purification and separation of metals, metal complexes, organic and biological compounds, solid phase extraction, solid phase synthesis, metal ion extraction and biomolecule immobilization.

title: A kind of protective device and method of Uranium enrichment plant's material container disassembling operations
abstract: The present invention relates to uranium enriching plants for feeding technical field, specifically discloses the protective device and method of a kind of Uranium enrichment plant's material container disassembling operations, can carry out evacuation purging to container connecting tube when material container dismounting in for feeding workshop.Using protective device of the present invention, in entire disassembly process, the pernicious gas remained in container connecting tube will not be spilt into workshop, but by the device condensation of congealing of the device, and purification is further trapped through adsorption tower and filter, considerably reduce the radioactive pollution of workshop.

title: gas treatment system and offshore plant having the same
abstract: The present invention relates to a gas treatment system and an offshore structure including the same, the system being provided on an offshore structure adjacent to the coast and receiving and processing gas from the land, comprising: a pretreatment unit for removing impurities from the gas and drying the gas; An NGL processor separating the NGL from the gas from which impurities are removed; A liquefaction unit for liquefying the gas from which the NGL is separated; Condensate processing unit for processing the condensate contained in the gas; A slop processing unit for processing the slops contained in the gas; And a fuel supply unit for supplying gas to a demand destination, wherein the pretreatment unit receives gas from the land at high pressure, and the liquefaction unit includes a liquefier for cooling the gas using a refrigerant, in a heat exchange process with the refrigerant. It is characterized in that the gas is liquefied without a forced pressure change of the gas.

title: A kind of stable type lithium sulfur battery anode material and preparation method
abstract: The present invention relates to lithium-sulfur cell field, a kind of stable type lithium sulfur battery anode material and preparation method are disclosed.Including following preparation process: (1) ethanol water is added with silane coupling agent in nano silver powder and be uniformly mixed, the nano metal particles of coupling processing are made after separation is dry；(2) by nano metal particles and acrylamide, N, N- methylene-bisacrylamide carries out ground and mixed, and mixed-powder is made；(3) mixed-powder is added to the ethylene glycol solution of neopelex, is passed through the elemental sulfur of distillation under protection of argon gas, stable type lithium sulfur battery anode material is made in filtering drying after heating reaction.Positive electrode produced by the present invention can inhibit the sulphur of anode to shuttle to cathode, to improve the cycle performance and service life of battery, and using nano metal particles as conductive grid, the electric property of carrier mobility with higher, lithium-sulfur cell obtained is excellent, and application prospect is good.

title: LNG regas
abstract: Liquefied hydrocarbon gas is carried in a tanker ( 12 ) to an offshore location where the liquefied gas is to be transferred to an offloading facility ( 20 ) such as a barge ( 21 ). At the offshore location the liquefied gas is to be heated to change it into a gaseous state and to heat the cold gas to produce warmed gas of at least about 0° C., with the warmed gas transferred to a storage facility ( 32, 33 ). Heating is accomplished without using only sea water and the burning of gas, by using air blowers ( 36, 38, 52 ) that blow large amounts of environmental air past containment structures ( 14, 28, 32, 42, 44 ) such as tanks, pipes and heat exchangers thermally coupled to the tanks and pipes, in which the liquefied or cold gas lies.

title: Hydrogen pressure measuring device
abstract: A hydrogen pressure measuring device capable of measuring hydrogen partial pressure in a measurement target gas with high accuracy is provided. A hydrogen pressure measuring device includes a hydrogen pressure sensor and a calculation unit. In the hydrogen pressure sensor 10, a hydrogen partial pressure chamber 15 is formed by a diaphragm 11, a support 12, and a hydrogen permeable membrane 13. When the hydrogen pressure sensor 10 is disposed in a space where the measurement target gas exists, hydrogen gas in the measurement target gas flows into the hydrogen partial pressure chamber 15 via the hydrogen permeable membrane 13. The internal pressure of the hydrogen partial pressure chamber 15 at this time is equal to the partial pressure of the hydrogen gas in the measurement target gas. The diaphragm 11 is deformed by a differential pressure between the internal pressure of the hydrogen partial pressure chamber 15 and the pressure of the measurement target gas. The differential pressure is detected by a piezoresistor 14 whose resistance value changes due to deformation of the diaphragm 11. The calculation unit calculates the partial pressure of hydrogen gas based on the differential pressure detected based on the resistance value of the piezoresistor 14 and the pressure of the measurement target gas. [Selection] Figure 4

title: Treatment process of generated gas for preparing butadiene through oxidative dehydrogenation of butylene
abstract: The invention relates to a process for treating generated gas generated in preparation of butadiene by oxidative dehydrogenation of butylene, which comprises the following steps: the method comprises the steps of recovering heat of generated gas from a butylene oxidation dehydrogenation reactor, then feeding the generated gas into a quench tower for treatment, sequentially treating the generated gas in a quench tower in a quench section, an alkali washing section and a water washing section, wherein only gas phase circulates between two adjacent sections of the quench tower, liquid phase forcibly circulates in each section, and finally, the generated gas washed by three sections of the quench tower enters subsequent units such as compression and the like. Compared with the prior art, the invention solves the problems of large washing water amount, high energy consumption and large waste water amount in the deacidification process of the generated gas after the dehydrogenation reaction of the existing butadiene preparation device by butylene oxidative dehydrogenation, and simultaneously, the injection of alkali liquor neutralizes the generated gas and the organic acid in the waste water to make the waste water non-corrosive, thereby saving the operation and investment cost of the subsequent treatment of the waste water and having obvious economic benefit.

title: Vacuum electric induction furnace for light alloys
abstract: The utility model discloses a vacuum electric induction furnace for light alloys. The vacuum electric induction furnace comprises a furnace body and a vacuum system which are connected sequentially, wherein the furnace body is provided with a smelting system and a stirring system; the stirring system comprises an electromagnetic stirring device; a furnace cover is arranged at the upper end of the furnace body; the stirring system further comprises a mechanical stirring device arranged on the furnace cover, the mechanical stirring device extends into the furnace body and is complementary with the electromagnetic stirring device to finish uniform stirring; a temperature measuring system is arranged in the furnace body or on the inner wall of the furnace body; and a heat preserving furnace is configured in the furnace body. The vacuum electric induction furnace is widely applied to smelting and casting of precision light alloys, rare earth light alloys, active metal, copper, copper alloys, hydrogen storage materials and the like in the vacuum or protective atmosphere for scientific research and production departments. The equipment has the advantages of high smelting efficiency, little smoke, uniform metal components, simplicity in operation process, high production efficiency and the like.

title: Controlled partial sulphiding method for nickel catalyst
abstract: In a glass reactor which incorporates a turbine stirrer, 400 ml of sunflower oil and 20g of nickel catalyst are mixed, involving 82 per cent nickel on a carrier of silicone dioxide-aluminium trioxide. The mixt. is heated to 180 deg. C., after which hydrogenation is commenced. Hydrogen gas is added at the rate of 25 ml/s (1 atm., 25 deg. C). Using a gas controlled feed coil of known volume (5.43 ml), different quantities of hydrogen sulphide are fed to a gas mixer, in which the main hydrogen gas flow is allowed to carry with it the hydrogen sulphide into the hydrogenation reactor. A flame photo-multiplicator reactor is connected to the reactor outlet, and permits a rapid and simple determn. of the amount of hydrogen sulphide absorbed by the catalyst. In all cases, the hydrogenation time is 70 minutes.

title: Valve train tappet rising abrasion-resistive and method of it
abstract: PURPOSE: To provide a tappet for engines having excellent friction decreasing characteristics, wherein the tappet obtains friction decreasing effect and improves fuel efficiency of engines accordingly compared with existing tappets by applying a DLC coating process using an evaporation material coated with silicon and consisted of carbon, hydrogen and silicon, and to provide a manufacturing method the tappet. CONSTITUTION: In a method for manufacturing a tappet for engines by ion beam deposition method and sputtering deposition method, the method for manufacturing a tappet for engines having excellent friction decreasing characteristics comprises the step of forming a coating film having a certain thickness on the tappet using an evaporation material coated with silicon and consisted of carbon, hydrogen and silicon, wherein the ion beam deposition method and the sputtering deposition method may be performed at the same time to improve adhesive force of silicon layer, wherein the deposition process by the ion beam deposition method and the sputtering deposition method comprises the process of synthesizing and depositing a silicon added coating film by simultaneously supplying an argon gas and sputtering a silicon target by a sputter gun after synthesizing a coating film by supplying carbon and hydrogen gas with an ion gun, wherein the method further comprises the step of performing drying cleaning on the tappet by supplying an argon gas into a chamber with an ion gun before coating the tappet during the ion beam deposition, and wherein the method further comprises the step of rotating the tappet that is a substrate at a certain rotary speed in the chamber during the ion beam deposition to uniformly deposit the tappet.

title: Gas production apparatus and gas production method
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a gas production apparatus that can further reduce parts to be provided with a protection member for preventing the contact with an electrolyte solution to reduce manufacturing costs.SOLUTION: The gas production apparatus includes: a photoelectric conversion part 2 which has a light receiving surface and a back surface thereof; a first electrode 8 for electrolysis and a second electrode 7 for electrolysis, which are provided side by side on the back surface; and a sealing part 9 which is provided on the peripheral portion of the first or second electrode for electrolysis. In the gas production apparatus, the first and second electrodes for electrolysis are provided so as to electrolyze an electrolyte solution, when the first and second electrodes for electrolysis come into contact with the electrolyte solution, by utilizing the electromotive force that is generated when the photoelectric conversion part 2 receives light and to respectively produce a first gas and a second gas; and the sealing part 9 has corrosion resistance against the electrolyte solution and is provided so as to prevent the electrolyte solution from flowing into the space between the first or second electrode for electrolysis and the photoelectric conversion part 2.

title: Organosilicon-based electrolytes for long-life lithium primary batteries
abstract: Organosilicon electrolytes exhibit several important properties for use in lithium carbon monofluoride batteries, including high conductivity/low viscosity and thermal/electrochemical stability. Conjugation of an anion binding agent to the siloxane backbone of an organosilicon electrolyte creates a bi-functional electrolyte. The bi-functionality of the electrolyte is due to the ability of the conjugated polyethylene oxide moieties of the siloxane backbone to solvate lithium and thus control the ionic conductivity within the electrolyte, and the anion binding agent to bind the fluoride anion and thus facilitate lithium fluoride dissolution and preserve the porous structure of the carbon monofluoride cathode. The ability to control both the electrolyte conductivity and the electrode morphology/properties simultaneously can improve lithium electrolyte operation.
