title: Electrochemical cells having one or more multilayer electrodes
abstract: Electrochemical cells of the present disclosure may include one or more multilayered electrodes. Each multilayered electrode may be configured such that active materials of the layer closest to the current collector have a lower energy to lithiate per mole, a higher energy to delithiate per mole, a different solid state diffusivity, and/or a different average particle size. This arrangement counteracts, for example, natural gradient fields and undesirable polarization found in standard lithium-ion batteries.

title: Method for producing nanografiber
abstract: (57) [Summary] [PROBLEMS] To provide a method for producing a nanograph fiber which is uniform and has good reproducibility. SOLUTION: A first carbon electrode 2 serving as a cathode and a second carbon electrode 3 serving as an anode are arranged to face each other, and an arc discharge is generated between these carbon electrodes in a hydrogen gas atmosphere, In the method for producing a nanograph fiber in which a deposit containing the nanograph fiber is formed on the first carbon electrode 2, a projection is provided on a surface of the second carbon electrode 3 facing the first carbon electrode 2, The carbon electrode 2 is brought into contact with the convex portion of the second carbon electrode 3, a current is applied between the carbon electrodes at a first current value, and the interval between these carbon electrodes is increased at a predetermined speed until the distance becomes a predetermined distance. The arc current is generated by spreading the arc discharge, and the output current of the DC power supply 5 is controlled so that the current flowing between the carbon electrodes after a predetermined time has elapsed from the occurrence of the arc discharge becomes the second current value.

title: Processing technological method for gas generated in preparation of butadiene through reaction by oxidative dehydrogenation of butene
abstract: The invention relates to a processing technological method for gas generated in preparation of butadiene through oxidation reaction by using oxidative dehydrogenation of butane. The processing technological method comprises the steps as follows: mixing butane-containing raw material, preheated steam and pressurized air in sequence, and transferring into a reactor to carry out oxidative dehydrogenation reaction; sequentially processing the high-temperature gas produced in the reactor by heat exchanging through a waste heat boiler, cooling through a cooler, performing acid rinsing through a water cooling acid rinsing tower, pressurizing through a compressor, and washing and removing aldehyde through an aldehyde washing tower; then absorbing through C6 oil and high boiling point oil at two stages; recovering organic matters thereof such as C4; processing the exhaust gas with little organic matter through a tail gas processing facility, and then exhausting; processing the organic matter absorbed via C6 oil through a desorber to obtain the primary butadiene; and desorbing the C6 oil and the high boiling point oil, and then recycling. Compared with traditional industrial technology, the processing technological method provided by the invention reduces the polymerization loss of the aldehyde and butadiene during the compressing process as well as the power consumption of a motor by reducing the pressure of an outlet of the compressor; and with the adoption of two-stage absorption, the content and the loss of C6 oil in the exhaust gas can be reduced.

title: Impurity removal using natural zeolite and equipment therefor
abstract:
PURPOSE: To provide a means for effectively removing water from fluid and being used for purification of inert gas, hydrogen gas, etc.  
     CONSTITUTION: A sealed adsorption chamber 10 has openings 23, 24 to allow fluid to pass, and the chamber 10 contains adsorbent 15 essentailly consisting of natural zeolite whose iron content is less than the specified level. Fluid is guided from the opening 23 into the adsorption chamber 10 to allow impurities in the fluid to be adsorbed. The natural zeolite being used is one having Si/Al ratio about 4 or more, and is activated at a temperature specified within about 250W900°C before adsorption.

title: Drive unit, method for providing power, and use of a drive unit
abstract: A drive unit (2) comprises a combustion chamber (26) for combusting a fuel/air mixture, and a fuel cell device (4), wherein the fuel cell device (4) comprises at least one fuel cell (30), which in each case comprises an anode (32) that is couplable to a fuel line (38), a cathode (34) that is couplable to an air source, and a fluid outlet (31) and is arranged upstream of the combustion chamber (26). The combustion chamber (26) further comprises a combustion chamber inlet (27) for supplying the fuel/air mixture, and a combustion chamber outlet (29) for discharging exhaust gas, and the combustion chamber inlet (27) is connected to the fluid outlet (31) of the fuel cell device (4). In this way a hybrid drive unit can be provided which apart from mechanical power also generates electrical power at high efficiency.

title: A kind of high compacted density lithium ion battery silicon-carbon cathode material and preparation method thereof
abstract: A kind of high compacted density lithium ion battery silicon-carbon cathode material and preparation method thereof, the negative electrode material includes carbon base body, silicon particle, the silicon particle is after ball milling activates, it is uniformly mixed with carbon matrix material, it is uniformly embedded in carbon base body after compaction moulding, then, the oxide on pickling removal silicon particle surface, forms gap between silicon particle and carbon base body.Preparation method includes being uniformly mixed after silicon particle ball milling activates with carbon matrix material, compaction moulding, broken, classification, the charing process under protective atmosphere, is washed in hydrofluoric acid solution.Silicon-carbon composite cathode material prepared by the present invention has high compacted density, and specific surface area is small, and initial coulomb efficiency is high；Through silicon-carbon cathode material prepared by the present invention, powder body compacted density is not less than 1.6g/cm 3 , D 50 =10~30 μm, specific surface area is not higher than 10m 2 / g, initial coulomb efficiency are higher than 85%.Preparation process flow is simple, at low cost, is suitble to large-scale production.

title: Three-dimensionally structured lithium anode
abstract: A method is provide for manufacturing a lithium anode and to a lithium anode for a lithium cell and/or a lithium battery. In order to improve the service life, performance capability and safety of a lithium cell and/or a lithium battery equipped with the lithium anode, the lithium anode includes a surface-structured current conductor and/or a surface-structured protective layer having at least one surface section circumscribed by a raised surface section, the surface structuring/structurings forming at least one cavity, and the at least one cavity being, in particular electrochemically, filled with anode active material. Also provided are a lithium cell and a lithium battery equipped with a lithium anode.

title: Method of encapsulating materials in a zeolite in a stable manner
abstract: The present invention relates to compounds, elements or ions encapsulated in a zeolite in a stable manner by adsorption, chemisorption or ion exchange, by using a zeolite treated before together with, or after the introduction of the material to be enclosed with a modifying agent selected from the group consisting of silanes, boranes and germanes, which may be substituted by one or more halogen atoms and/or hydrocarbon groups, and after the introduction closing the pores by a further treatment with the modifying agent and/or by an oxidation treatment.

title: Fuel cell temperature control
abstract: 1,221,537. Fuel cell control. UNITED AIRCRAFT CORP. 23 May, 1968 [23 May, 1967], No. 24750/68. Heading HIB. . A temperature control system for a fuel cell comprises means for supplying coolant to the cell, means for sensing the cell current, means for sensing the cell temperature and means responsive to the current and temperature sensor for adjusting the temperature of the coolant supply. In Fig. 1 fuel cell 18 may comprise aqueous KOH electrolyte. Hydrogen fuel may be supplied from generator 10 to the anodes via valve 12 and pressure regulator 14. Excess hydrogen leaves at outlet 20 and is burned in burner 24. Atmospheric air line 34 is supplied partly through heater 56 and partly through by-pass 46 to control its temperature. At 49 the atmospheric air is mixed with some exhaust air from the cell the proportion being controlled by valve 36. CO 2  scrubber 32 may contain soda-lime. From here the air is pumped by constant volume pump 26 to the cathode. Some of the exhaust air is vented at 44, the remainder being returned for re-use via line 38. Current sensor 102 measures the cell current and controls valve 36. The temperature of the system is controlled by coolant circuit 78 containing ethylene glycol or silicone oil and supplying air heater 56, start-up heater 58 and the cell. The coolant temperature is controlled by valve 66 which determines how much of the coolant passes through heat exchanger 64. The circuit also includes constant volume pump 50 and bleed valve 84. Temperature control 106 measures the temperature at 70 and controls valve 66. The fuel system may be applied to a battery of cells.

title: Method for manufacturing carbohydrate-containing gelling concentrate for confectionary products
abstract:
FIELD: food industry, confectionary industry. 
     SUBSTANCE: the suggested concentrate should be prepared due to pressing the juice out of sugar beet prepared, deodorated with carbon dioxide in supracritical state, extracting residues at mixing water and liquid hydrogen chloride at extraction volume, mixing juice and extract and introducing liquid ammonia into the blend along with supracritical CO 2 -extract out of Pythium irregulare micromycete biomass further extracted according to the preset technique to obtain a solid residue treated with liquid ammonia followed by concentrating the blend, treatment of concentrate with liquid carbon dioxide, mixing Pythium irregulare micromycete biomass with a treated solid residue and heating the mixture up to 60 C, not less. The innovation enables to obtain a concentrate of improved structure-forming properties and increased thermal stability. 
     EFFECT: higher efficiency of manufacturing.

title: Plasma treating method and device
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To increase density and uniformity even in a large aperture by introducing a high frequency electric field and a magnetic field into a chamber, exciting a low mixed wave, generating plasma by the low mixed wave, and treating a substrate. SOLUTION: A substrate 3a is carried in a chamber 2. After the pressure in the chamber 2 is adequately reduced, while hydrogen gas is introduced, the pressure in the chamber 2 is reduced, and the chamber 2 is kept in the specified pressure. The magnetic field is applied to the chamber 2 with a magnetic coil 6, and at the same time a high frequency electric field is introduced into the chamber 2. A low mixed wave is excited in the chamber 2, and high density hydrogen plasma is generated. The excited mixed wave goes in the axial direction of the chamber 2, and at the same time propagates to the inside in the radius direction, converts the mode into an electrostatic wave in the vicinity of the resonance point of the low mixed wave, accelerates bulk electrons, and plasma is generated.  The phase of the high frequency electric field supplied to an antenna 4 is adjusted with a phase shifter 5a, the number of waves in the axial direction of the low mixed wave is varied, and propagation characteristics of the low mixed wave are controlled.

title: The cathode of lithium and its solid state battery of lithium-aluminium alloy surface modification
abstract: The present invention relates to a kind of cathode of lithium and its solid state battery of lithium-aluminium alloy surface modification.The invention belongs to technical field of chemical power.The cathode of lithium of lithium-aluminium alloy surface modification, is characterized in：The cathode of lithium of lithium-aluminium alloy surface modification is used as solid state lithium battery negative material finishing coat using lithium-aluminium alloy powder.Solid state battery, including anode electrode, negative electrode and composite electrolyte are assembled using the cathode of lithium of lithium-aluminium alloy surface modification, are characterized in：Solid state battery uses the cathode of lithium of lithium-aluminium alloy surface modification, and cathode of lithium is used as solid state lithium battery negative material finishing coat using lithium-aluminium alloy layer.There is the negative material of the lithium-aluminium alloy surface modification of the present invention using effect in solid state lithium battery system substantially, can significantly strengthen the compatibility and stability of solid electrolyte, the solid state lithium battery cyclicity assembled is good, the advantages of cycle efficieny is high.

title: Method for producing lactic acid
abstract: The invention is directed to a method for producing lactate. The method of the invention comprises electrochemically oxidising a catalyst at an anode, and using oxidised catalyst to oxidise propylene glycol and form lactate, thereby reducing the said oxidised catalyst.
text: Method for producing lactic acid The invention is directed to a method for producing lactate. title: Cooling using a cryogenic liquid and a contacting gas
abstract: A cooling apparatus includes a dewar and a quantity of liquid nitrogen within the container. Gaseous helium is contacted to the liquid nitrogen, either by contacting its top surface or by being bubbled through the liquid nitrogen. The temperature of the cryogenic liquid is lowered by the contact of the gas.

title: Special graphite cathode for ionic-membrane electrolysis tank
abstract: The invention relates to a special graphite cathode for an ionic-membrane electrolysis tank. The special graphite cathode comprises a graphite plate, wherein water circulating grooves are formed in a front face of the graphite plate, and flow guiding grooves are formed in a rear face of the graphite plate; a material outlet hole and a water outlet hole are formed in the upper part of a left end face of the graphite plate, a material inlet hole and a water inlet hole are formed in the lower part of a right end face of the graphite plate, the material outlet hole is communicated with the upper part of the flow guiding grooves, the material inlet hole is communicated with the lower part of the flow guiding grooves, the water outlet hole is communicated with the upper part of the water circulating grooves, and the water inlet hole is communicated with the lower part of the water circulating grooves; an annular sealing groove is formed in the front face of the graphite plate and is located around the water circulating grooves. The special graphite cathode has a cooling water circulating structure and a cathode material liquid flow guiding structure, so that the graphite cathode, an anode and material liquid can be cooled, the circulation and uniform distribution of cathode material liquid can be accelerated, the escape of hydrogen gas is facilitated, the service life of the graphite cathode and the service life of the anode are prolonged, and the current efficiency and product quality are improved.

title: Beverage Container
abstract: A beverage container containing a hydrogen pouch is provided. The provided beverage container has a plastic container body having an injection port and filled with beverage inside; A plurality of perforated fins protruding from an upper surface of the container main body, and a sealing protrusion protruding from the lower surface of the sealing plate into which the plurality of perforated holes are perforated, And a guide protrusion having a first fixing protrusion and a second fixing protrusion protruded in a lower direction of the rim; A hydrogen pouch filled with hydrogen gas to be pierced by the piercing pin is installed in the inside of the hermetically sealed container, a first locking protrusion in which the first fixing protrusion of the guide protrusion is fixed, A plastic lid having a second locking protrusion for fixing the fixing protrusion; And a control unit.

title: Method for detecting harmful gases which is applicable to broad gas concentration range
abstract: The present invention provides a method by which harmful trace gases in a gaseous mixture containing as such harmful halogen gases, halogenated hydrogen gases, acid gases, oxidizing gases, basic gases, organic acid gases, especially halogen gases or halogenated hydrogen gases, are detected by using tetraphenylporphyrin (TPP) and quantitated from a calibration curve constructed therefrom, where the range of detectable concentration is made adjustable so that harmful gas can be detected and quantitated over a broad range of concentration. Furthermore, this invention also provides a method for extending the accessible range of gas concentration by adjusting the sensitivity of the detector material via control of tetraphenylporphyrin concentration in matrix polymer of the detector material, by controlling the gas concentration range via measurement at a specific wavelength(s), and by the use of a plurality of detector materials with pre-set assay sensitivity.

title: Method of selectively growing ii-vi compound semiconductor thin film
abstract: PURPOSE:To prevent a deposit from being separated on a mask by selectively growing II-VI compound semiconductor thin film under specified growing pressure. CONSTITUTION:An SiO2 film is formed on a GaAs substrate 31 and then the SiO2 film is etched away leaving a mask pattern 32 thereof. This substrate 31 is arranged in a reaction tube 4 of MOCVD device which is exhausted and then filled up with hydrogen gas. Next, dimethyl zinc and dimethyl selenium are bubbled with hydrogen gas to be led into the reaction tube 4 together with carrier gas to grow ZnSe 33 while the substrate 31 is being heated. The growing pressure at this time shall not exceed 300Torr. Thus, ZnSe 33 is not deposited on SiO2 and the mask 32 at all but single crystal ZnSe 33 is epitaxial-grown on the surface of GaAs 31. Through these procedures, the removal process of II-VI compound semiconductor on the mask can be eliminated.

title: Stable hydrogenation process for liquefied coal coil
abstract: A stable hydrogenation process of liquefied coal oil includes filtering the liquefied coal oil, adding it along with hydrogen gas to stable hydrogenation reactor, contacting with hydroprotector and hydrorefining catalyst, flowing in its product through high-pressure separator, low-pressure separator and fractional tower to respectively obtain hydrogen-enriched airflow, light fraction and tail oil fraction, mixing said hydrogen-enriched airflow with fresh hydrogen, and returning the mixture back to said stable hydrogenation reactor. It has high effect on removing impurities from liquefied oil to maximum.

abstract: 1,032,722. Electromagnetic relays. HITACHI SEISAKUSHO KABUSHIKI KAISHA, and HEIKIN HITACHI ENGINEERING KABUSHIKI KAISHA. May 29, 1963 [June 4, 1962; June 5, 1962 (2)], No. 21549/63. title: A ceramics sintering furnace
abstract: The present invention includes a furnace main body having a plurality of heating elements for providing a heat source in the inner passage of a predetermined length and a plurality of thermocouples for measuring the internal temperature; Mixing an atmosphere gas by mixing an air supply line connected to the furnace body with nitrogen, hydrogen gas and oxygen supplied from the nitrogen, hydrogen and oxygen supply lines to supply an atmosphere gas mixed with nitrogen, hydrogen and oxygen into the furnace body. An air supply unit comprising a mixer and a humidifier for adding moisture to the atmosphere gas; An exhaust unit having an exhaust line connected to the furnace body to discharge atmospheric gas from the furnace body to the outside; Operating means for horizontally moving the moving trolley seated on the upper surface of the housing portion on which the ceramic molded body is loaded from the inlet to the outlet of the furnace body; A gas analyzer for measuring a gas concentration by collecting atmospheric gases exhausted to the outside through the exhaust line; and receiving a gas concentration value measured by the gas analyzer and a furnace temperature value measured by the thermocouple, based on the And a controller for controlling the driving of the operating means to adjust the moving speed of the moving cart.

title: Fuel supplying method
abstract: PURPOSE:To enhance the heat emission efficiency by cooling a modified gas, introducing it to a CO2 gas enriched film. concentrating the CO2 gas for separating it, and supplying high purity hydrogen gas to a fuel battery. CONSTITUTION:Methanol 16 converges with water vapor 17 supplied to a modifying device 1 to make reaction, and modification is made to 80% hydrogen gas and 20% CO2 gas. After this modified gas 18 is cooled for ex. to 50 deg.C by a cooler 9 in the backstream, only CO2 is passed selectively to be fed into a CO2 gas enriching film 3 to raise the CO2 concentration. Therein the CO2 in the modified gas is concentrated and separated in the form of a CO2 gas enriched gas 21a, while the residual modified gas is supplied to the negative electrode of fuel battery 4 in the form of high concentration hydrogen gas 20a. Thereby the high concentration hydrogen gas 20a as fuel and an O2 enriched air 30 as air are supplied to the fuel battery 4, which activates electrode reaction in the fuel battery 4 to enhance the power generating efficiency.

title: Removal method of mercury contained in emission gas from incinerator by blast furnace slag and apparatus of the same
abstract: The present invention relates to a method and apparatus for removing mercury in an incinerator flue gas using blast furnace slag, comprising a desulfurization step for desulfurizing flue gas discharged from a boiler through a desulfurization facility, a slag treatment step for improving the surface area of blast furnace slag, and the desulfurization step. Mercury adsorption step of adsorbing mercury by spraying the blast furnace slag through the slag treatment step to the exhaust gas through the slag, slag collection step of collecting the blast furnace slag through the mercury adsorption step in the filter bag, mercury in the slag collection step The present invention relates to a method for removing mercury in an incinerator flue gas using a blast furnace slag, characterized by comprising an exhaust gas discharge step of discharging the removed flue gas through a stack, and further, in a desulfurization system for desulfurizing flue gas discharged from a boiler, Blast Furnace Improved Surface Area for Desulfurized Flue Gas A blast furnace slag injector for injecting mercury by spraying lag, a bag filter for collecting the blast furnace slag adsorbed by mercury in the blast furnace slag injector, and a stack in which the exhaust gas from which mercury is removed from the bag filter is discharged The present invention relates to an apparatus for removing mercury in an incinerator flue gas using blast furnace slag, which exhibits high mercury removal efficiency and is economical, does not cause secondary pollution problems, does not interfere with desulfurization, and recycles resources and atmosphere. Quality can be improved simultaneously, and can be used to remove mercury and other gaseous harmful metal compounds.

title: Aluminum nitride sintered body and process for producing the same
abstract:
An aluminum nitride sintered body characterized by comprising aluminum nitride as the main component, containing a titanium compound, and having a black color, a transmittance of 10% or less with the light having a wavelength in the range of from 500 to 650 nm and a heat conductivity of 120 W/m·K or more. The sintered body is produced by adding 0.05 to 5% by weight, in terms of Ti, of a titanium compound and a sintering aid compound and, if necessary, a compound capable of forming carbon after being thermally decomposed to an aluminum nitride powder, molding the mixture, heating the molding in vacuo, air or a nitrogen gas, a hydrogen gas or an atmosphere comprising a mixture of these gases until the residual carbon content is reduced to 2.0% by weight or less, and sintering the heat-treated mixture in a nonoxidizing atmosphere containing nitrogen at 1600° C. or above. The titanium compound is Ti n  O 2n-1  or a solid solution comprising Ti n  O 2n-1  and nitrogen partly dissolved therein in the solid solution form wherein n is 1 or more, or a compound represented by the formula TiN x  O.sub. y wherein 0<x<1 and y is 3/2 (1-x). 
     There is provided an aluminum nitride sintered body wherein both the light shielding property and the heat conductivity are stabilized.

title: Starting method of fuel cell system
abstract: The present invention provides a simple process for preventing deterioration of an electrode catalyst as much as possible and maintaining good power generation performance.   A starting method of a fuel cell system (10) is that the oxidant gas flow path (32) and the fuel gas flow path (34) of a fuel cell stack (12) filled with air at the time of stop are replaced with the air and hydrogen gas is simultaneously used. A supply step, a step of supplying air to the oxidant gas flow channel 32 after stopping the supply of the hydrogen gas to the oxidant gas flow channel 32, and a load 66 connected to the fuel cell stack 12. title: Hydrogen occlusion alloy electrode, its manufacturing method, and nickel hydrogen storage battery using it
abstract: <P>PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To realize a nickel hydrogen storage battery using a hydrogen occlusion alloy electrode in a negative electrode finishing initial formation in a short time, and having high rate discharge performance and cycle characteristics not inferior to an existing nickel hydrogen storage battery.  <P>SOLUTION: As a hydrogen occlusion alloy powder for the hydrogen occlusion alloy electrode, hydrogen occlusion alloy powder having an average particle size of 13-25 μm and to which surface reforming treatment is preformed, and hydrogen occlusion alloy powder having an average particle size of 30-50 μm and to which the surface reforming treatment is not performed are mixed so that the ratio of the hydrogen occlusion alloy powder to which the surface reforming treatment is not performed is present in a range of 20-80 wt.%.  <P>COPYRIGHT: (C)2003,JPO

title: Method and device for manufacturing thin-film solar cell
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a method and device for manufacturing a thin-film solar cell wherein a thin-film solar cell of high photoelectric conversion efficiency is manufactured at high operation rate. SOLUTION: A plurality of film-forming chambers comprising a reaction chamber 22 where film-forming reaction is performed and a heater chamber 32 provided with a heater 31 are provided. While, in the film-forming camber, a film substrate 1 of long size is step-transported, a thin-film which constitutes a thin-film solar cell is formed on a substrate 1, and the openings of a reaction chamber 22 and heater chamber 32 are sealed up with the substrate in between in film-forming. Here, the opening of the heater chamber 32 is covered with a shielding member 3p. By introducing hydrogen gas or material gas of film into the heater chamber 32, temperature control for the substrate 1 is performed. A material gas introduction opening 23, valves 41 and 42, and a vacuum pump 51 are provided.

title: Impurity concentrating device
abstract: <P>PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide an impurity concentrating device for concentrating impurities included in hydrogen gas by utilizing an electrochemical hydrogen pump, in which a difference in concentration rate of impurities among cells is reduced when the cells constituting the electrochemical hydrogen pump are stacked in a plurality of numbers.  <P>SOLUTION: The electrochemical hydrogen pump comprises a plurality of cells 22A, 22B, in which the cells 22B as a part of the plurality of cells 22A, 22B are low pressure loss cells having lower pressure loss in an anode flow passage than the cells 22A. The exits of anode flow passages of the cells 22A, 22B are connected to each other through an exit manifold 26.  <P>COPYRIGHT: (C)2011,JPO&INPIT

title: Oxidation method of stem guide wire of oscilloscope tube and application thereof
abstract: The invention relates to an oxidation method of a stem guide wire of an oscilloscope tube and application thereof. The method comprises the following steps: firstly, carrying out guide wire surface cleaning; to be specific, placing 4J29 guide wires needing oxidation in a barrel type barreling device, placing grinding fluid and an aluminum oxide grinding material into the device and carrying out barreling; secondly, carrying out temperature rising and decarburization in hydrogen; to be specific, placing 1Kg to 5Kg of 4J29 guide wires in a push-plate type hydrogen furnace each time and particularly in a high-temperature zone directly, and carrying out temperature rising and thermal insulation in a hydrogen gas reduction environment; thirdly, forming an oxidation layer under the high temperature; to be specific, taking out the metal guide wires directly under the temperature of 1050 DEG C to 1200 DEG C without the temperature reduction process and placing the metal guide wires in an atmosphere under the temperature of 0 DEG C to 40 DEG C with rapid temperature reduction to a room temperature; and fourthly, carrying out verification. The advantages are as follows: firstly, because a low-valence oxide is used and is easily soaked by a piece of glass, good sealing between the glass and the guide wires is formed, thereby improving the sealing quality; and secondly, the oxidation method of the stem guide wire can be widely applied to manufacturing of stems of oscilloscope tubes of various types.

title: A kind of negative plate and lithium battery with gradient silicone content
abstract: The invention discloses a kind of negative plates and lithium battery with gradient silicone content, gradient distribution is carried out by the silicone content to active material layer on negative plate, middle layer uses the active material of high silicon content, outer layer uses the active material of low silicon content, form a kind of structure of " sandwich ", the active material of outer layer is since silicone content is low, and volume expansion is small in charging process, the volume expansion that can buffer and inhibit high silicon content middle layer, to improve the performance of battery.

title: Electrochemical synthesis of aryl-alkyl surfactant precursor
abstract: An aryl-alkyl (R-Ar) hydrocarbon is prepared by an electrosynthesis process in an electrolytic cell (100) having an alkali ion conductive membrane (110) positioned between an anolyte compartment (112) configured with an anode (116) and a catholyte compartment (114) configured with a cathode (118). An anolyte solution (124) containing an alkali metal salt of an alkyl carboxylic acid (128) and an aryl compound (130) is introduced into the anolyte compartment (112). The aryl compound (130) may include an alkali metal salt of an aryl carboxylic acid, an arene (aromatic) hydrocarbon, or an aryl alkali metal adduct (Ar -  M + ). The anolyte solution (124) undergoes electrolytic decarboxylation to form an alkyl radical. The alkyl radical reacts with the aryl compound to produce the aryl-alkyl hydrocarbon.

title: Metal foam for electrode of lithium secondary battery, preparing method thereof and lithium secondary battery including the metalfoam
abstract: The present invention relates to a metal foam for an electrode of a lithium secondary battery, a preparing method thereof and a lithium secondary battery including the metal foam and, more specifically, to a metal foam for an electrode of a lithium secondary battery having a contact resistance between an active material and the metal foam minimized and a contact area therebetween maximized by coating the surface thereof and the inner walls of pores with the active material, a preparing method thereof and a lithium secondary battery including the same. The metal foam for an electrode of a lithium secondary battery according to the present invention can realize a lithium secondary battery having excellent cycle properties and charge/discharge efficiency improved eminently by minimizing the contact resistance between the active material and a current collector and maximizing the contact area therebetween because the metal foam has a coating structure so that the active material can directly contact with the inner walls of pores as well as the surface of the metal foam having a specific surface area much higher than that of a metal foil being usually used as a conventional current collector. [Reference numerals] (AA) Example 4; (BB) Example 5; (CC) Example 6; (DD) Comparative example

title: A kind of cleaning plant for waste gas of plasma
abstract: The present invention proposes a kind of cleaning plant for waste gas of plasma, including main box, plasma apparatus, metal filtration layer, photodissociation device, homogenizing plate, activated carbon filter layer and support mounting bracket, one end of the main box is equipped with air intake, the other end of the main box is equipped with outlet air end, the air intake to outlet air end is equipped with plasma apparatus successively, metal filtration layer, photodissociation device, activated carbon filter layer, the plasma apparatus, the inlet side of metal filtration layer is separately installed with homogenizing plate, several support mounting brackets are installed in the main box, the activated carbon filter layer is installed in support mounting bracket, the activated carbon filter layer includes cellular activated carbon, clamp frame, fixed-wing, locked component and gear frame.Using multistage purification, the particulate matter and harmful substance in exhaust gas can be effectively removed, and activated carbon filter layer uses the cellular activated carbon unit of multiple separate removables, can targetedly be replaced, effectively utilize resource.

title: Improvements in Apparatus for De-arsenicating Sulphuric Acid.
abstract: 1500. Bithell, R., and Beck, J. A. Jan. 18. Washers. - An apparatus for de-arsenicating sulphuric acid is built entirely of lead. Vitriol enters the uppermost of a series of superposed chambers by a pipe b and descends from one chamber to the next by pipes c. Sulphuretted hydrogen gas enters the lowermost chamber by a perforated pipe d, bubbles through the liquid and passes upwards from chamber to chamber by pipes e each having an upwardly extending loop preferably encircling the next gas-pipe.

title: Fuel reformer housing vessel, fuel reforming device and fuel reforming system
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a fuel reformer storage container and a fuel reformer with low power generation loss, which can maintain a good degree of vacuum in the fuel reformer storage container.   A substrate 1 having a cavity in which a fuel reformer 9 for generating a reformed gas containing hydrogen gas from fuel is stored, and a lid 4 attached to the upper surface of the substrate 1 so as to cover the cavity. A discharge pipe 5b that communicates the inside and outside of the cavity to discharge the reformed gas from the fuel reformer 9, and a supply pipe 5a that communicates the inside and outside of the cavity to supply fuel to the fuel reformer 9. And a fuel reformer storage container having a power supply lead terminal 2 penetrating the base body 1 or the lid body 4, a gas adsorbent 10 is mounted in the cavity, and the gas adsorption material 10 is adsorbed to the lid body 4 or the base body 1. The window part 4a for introducing the light ray irradiated to the material 10 from the outside was provided.   [Selection] Figure 1

title: Method for preparing valerolactone
abstract: The invention discloses a method for preparing valerolactone. The method comprises the following steps: putting acetylpropionic acid and palladium-loaded aluminum nitride into a reaction device; adding distilled water and introducing hydrogen gas to react at 60 DEG C to 100 DEG C for 1 to 6 hours, wherein the loading amount of palladium is 1 percent to 10 percent, the pressure of the hydrogen gas is 0.5MPa to 2MPa, and the mass ratio of the palladium-loaded aluminum nitride to the acetylpropionic acid is (0.2 to 0.8):1. The method has moderate reaction conditions and has a relatively high valerolactone yield.

title: Wind electric plant with improved alternator field excitation
abstract: An improvement in a wind electric plant of the type disclosed in applicants' U.S. Pat. No. 4,059,771 and applicants' co-pending application Ser. No. 887,373, filed Mar. 16, 1978. The improvement comprises utilization of an exciter including an exciter armature and an associated permanent magnet exciter field to provide the excitation current for the field winding of a three-phase alternator. The use of permanent magnets in the exciter field, in lieu of conventional exciter field windings, eliminates the utilization of battery current as the source of excitation current for the field winding of a three-phase alternator. The improved wind electric power plant is utilized to supply A. C. power to hot water heater loads and/or auxiliary loads such as air conditioners, devices to manufacture hydrogen gas, and the like, which may be solar powered. The present invention also provides associated circuitry operable so that power can be supplied alternatively to either the hot water heater loads or to the auxiliary loads thereby maintaining a constant load on the wind electric power plant to ensure the quiet operation of that plant.

title: Austenite high-manganese stainless steel, manufacturing method therefor, and member using said steel
abstract: An inexpensive stainless steel and inexpensive, high-strength stainless steel that are highly resistant to hydrogen environment embrittlement even when used in a hydrogen environment comprising high-pressure hydrogen gas, at a pressure exceeding 40 MPa, or liquid hydrogen. Said stainless steel contains, by mass, 0.4-1.5% silicon, 8-11% manganese, 15-17% chromium, 5-8% nickel, 1-4% copper, at least 0.01% but less than 0.15% nitrogen, and at most 0.1% carbon, with the remainder comprising iron and unavoidable impurities. The volume ratio of d-ferrite is 10% or less, with the pre-annealing major axis thereof being 0.04 to 0.1 mm.

title: Manufacturing method of alhx
abstract: An aluminum hydride is obtained by easily hydrogenating aluminum even at a general filling pressure of a hydrogen storage container.   A powder container 14 containing Al (preferably aluminum powder PW) is inserted into an electric furnace 12. After making the powder container 14 into the predetermined temperature between 450-550 degreeC with the electric furnace 12, the pressure in the powder container 14 shall be 1 mmHg or less and hold | maintained for 10 to 60 minutes. Furthermore, hydrogen is introduce | transduced in the powder storage container 14, a pressure shall be 4-70 MPa, and it will hold | maintain for 10 to 60 minutes. By repeating the above operation 50 to 150 times, AlHx (0 <x ≦ 3) in which the aluminum powder PW is hydrogenated is obtained.   [Selection] Figure 1

title: Macroporous terpolymer absorbent for chromatography - contg. hydroxyalkyl-, alkyl-, and alkylene bis-acrylate and methacrylate cpds.
abstract: Macroporous polymer absorbents consist of ternary copolymers (I), of, by wt., (a) 1.70% hydroxy-1-6C-alkyl (meth)acrylate, (b) 30-90% 1-18C-alkyl (meth)acrylate and (c) 20-80% 1-6C-alkylene bis-(meth)acrylate. (I) are used for gas chromatography of hydrocarbons, alcohols, organic acids and heterocyclic N cpds. Monomer (c) content can determine polarity of inner surface and chromatographic behaviour of prod. Materials having a high specific surface can be prepd. as spherical particles. Absorbent is produced in 1 reaction step and requires no further chemical conversion before use in chromatography. Sepn. activity is increased. In an example, ternary copolymer contained hydroxyethyl methacrylate, ethylene dimethacrylate and Me methacrylate.

title: Gas treatment system and ship having the same
abstract: A gas treatment system according to the present invention and a ship including the same are a gas treatment system for transferring liquefied gas from a storage tank of a bunkering ship to a fuel tank of a membrane type provided in a gas propulsion ship, wherein the liquefied gas of the storage tank is transferred to the fuel. A bunkering line for bunkering into a tank; A bunkering management unit that cools and returns the boil-off gas of the storage tank with a refrigerant to adjust the internal pressure of the storage tank; And a boil-off gas return line for transferring boil-off gas generated from the fuel tank to the bunkering vessel when bunkering through the bunkering line, wherein the bunkering management unit lowers the internal pressure of the storage tank to a preset pressure or less before bunkering, During bunkering, the internal pressure of the storage tank is maintained below the internal pressure of the fuel tank, so that the boil-off gas is delivered through the boil-off gas return line without compression by a separate compressor.

title: Preparation method for porous silicon nanofiber/carbon composite material
abstract: The invention relates to a preparation method for a porous silicon nanofiber/carbon composite material, and belongs to the technical field of lithium ion battery. The preparation method comprises the steps: firstly, carrying out acid dipping, water washing, filtration and drying on chrysotile asbestos to obtain a silica nanofiber having alkali metal oxide impurities removed; adding a reductive metal into the silica nanofiber, mixing evenly, then carrying out a reduction reaction to obtain a reduzate, carrying out pickling, water washing and drying on the reduzate to obtain a porous silicon nanofiber; and evenly mixing the obtained porous silicon nanofiber with a carbon precursor to obtain a mixed material, then dissolving the mixed material in a solvent, dispersing to obtain a mixed solution under an ultrasonic condition, and carrying out pyrolysis carbonization or hydrothermal carbonization of the dispersed mixed solution under the inert gas protection to obtain the porous silicon nanofiber/carbon composite material. The method takes natural minerals as raw materials, is low in cost and simple; and the porous silicon nanofiber/carbon composite material has the characteristics of large capacity density, high first-time Kulun efficiency, stable cyclic performance and the like.

title: Method of synthesizing monodisperse hexagonal rare earth fluoride nanosheet by adopting rheological phase reaction method
abstract: The invention discloses a method of synthesizing a monodisperse hexagonal rare earth fluoride nanosheet by adopting a rheological phase reaction method. The method is characterized by comprising the following steps: fully mixing and uniformly grinding a rare earth metal compound, a fluoride and a mixed solvent of an organic solvent and water to a rheological phase matter; and keeping the reaction time in a sealed reaction kettle at constant temperature to obtain the monodisperse hexagonal rare earth fluoride nanosheet. The rare earth fluoride nanosheet prepared by the invention is widely applied in aspects such as luminescent materials, magnetic materials, hydrogen storage materials, gas sensitive materials, lubricant additives, chemical sensors and electrode materials. The method disclosed by the invention is simple in process, free from organic wastewater discharge and environment-friendly, and satisfies the environmental requirements. The reaction is carried out in a sealed system to avoid dust generated by industrialized production. Meanwhile, the product has good uniformity, high purity and low cost, and is suitable for industrialized production on a large scale.

title: Method of firing movable electrically heated charge cannon and movable electrical heated charge cannon
abstract:
PURPOSE: To prevent an explosion out of cannon barrel and to get a high bullet speed by a method wherein a bullet charge is thermally decomposed by aluminum gas and high temperature hydrogen gas of water generated by a Joule heat and then the bullet is fired.  
     CONSTITUTION: For example, the amount of water 24 in a container 20 is 3mol, the total weight of aluminum member 23 is about 27g (1mol) and then the weight ratio of water 24 to aluminum member 23 is 2:1. As a switch 11 is turned on and a heat of about 5 kilo Joule is fed to the aluminum member 23, the water 24 is changed into water vapor. Aluminum member 23 is vaporized instantaneously to become aluminum gas. Water 24 and aluminum gas are reacted to each other to generate hydrogen gas of 1.5mol. An electrical conductive lid 22 is broken by pressure of hydrogen gas. The pressure is transmitted to a bullet chemical 25. The bullet 2 and the bullet chemical 25 are accelerated in the direction of arrow A. Arc current generated when the aluminum member 23 is evaporated is made to flow between an anode 10 and the conductive film 22, resulting in that the temperature of the hydrogen gas exceeds 1300°C, the bullet charge 25 is thermally decomposed and the bullet 2 is fired from a firing port 1a. 
     COPYRIGHT: (C)1992,JPO&Japio

title: Fuel supply method of gas turbine combustor
abstract: 【Task】   The object of the present invention is to suppress the hydrogen concentration in the unburned mixture of fuel and air containing hydrogen downstream of the combustor from being in the combustible range before the burner ignition.   [Solution]   In order to achieve the above object, the present invention includes a fuel supply system that supplies a mixed fuel containing hydrogen, and a liner that forms a combustion chamber for burning the mixed fuel supplied from the fuel supply system. A fuel supply method for a gas turbine combustor having a hydrogen storage alloy in a supply system, wherein before the ignition, hydrogen in the mixed fuel is adsorbed by the hydrogen storage alloy, and a fuel with a reduced hydrogen amount is used as an ignition fuel. Is supplied to the liner, and after ignition, hydrogen adsorbed by the hydrogen storage alloy is released into the mixed fuel, and the mixed fuel is supplied to the liner.   [Selection] Figure 1

title: Structure comprising carbon fiber and its support and production thereof
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To obtain the subject structure having carbon fibers existing in high density useful for hydrogen storage material by oxidizing Si-Fe-O based material in air or the like at the predetermined temperature and forming carbon fibers under the predetermined condition to control location of catalyst particles and growth of the carbon fiber. SOLUTION: This structure comprising carbon fiber and its support is obtained by oxidizing the Si-Fe-O based material in air or an atmosphere including oxygen at 500-800 deg.C to decompose into two phases comprising silica and iron oxide, forming pores by removing a part of the iron oxide with an acid or the like in the two phases, forming carbon fibers by a growth from vapor using hydrocarbon gas such as ethylene or the like using catalyst obtained by the reduction of the remaining iron oxide. This structure is composed of the support mainly comprising silica having pores and carbon fibers grown from the bottom of the pores.

title: Evacuating device
abstract: (57) Abstract: In a vacuum evacuation apparatus, the evacuation chamber can be freely opened to the atmosphere, and the evacuation performance for water and hydrogen is improved without substantially lowering the evacuation performance of a turbo molecular pump. . SOLUTION: A turbo molecular pump 19 is provided in an evacuation chamber 11a, and the ultimate pressure of the evacuation chamber is set to  10-1  Pa or less.   The turbo molecular pump is attached to the outside of the exhaust port 12 of the vacuum exhaust chamber, and is provided on the cryogenic valve 13 at the exhaust port of the vacuum exhaust chamber. The cryogenic valve exhausts the hydrogen storage alloy 10 and water molecules to the turbo molecular pump side. And a cooling panel 16 for performing   A normal temperature valve body 15 is provided on the vacuum exhaust chamber side. The cryovalve opens and closes as a gate valve that controls the evacuation operation of the evacuation chamber.

title: Hydrogen reactor and regenerative chemical method
abstract: The invention relates to a thermal method with the aid of a pressure pipe reactor for carrying out, under continuous flow conditions, some chemical reactions on the surface of at least one solid reactant in a gas/solid phase reaction. The core of the invention is a solar-thermal method based on concentrated solar energy with trough collectors, and the product is a pressure pipe reactor for continuous production of split hydrogen gas (H2) from water vapour gas (H2Og). Owing to the absorption and/or adsorption on the surface of metal mixtures of certain metals, the following reactions are performed at temperatures ranging from 500°C to 1350°C: cleaving, autooxidation, substitution, decomposition. The method has four main processes: water splitting, regeneration, roasting, flushing and evacuation. Problem: Hydrogen first has to be produced artificially and with lower degrees of efficacy from other energy sources (fossil energy, nuclear energy, or renewable energies). By contrast, concepts for future hydrogen economies usually provide for hydrogen recovery from renewable energies. Solution: The pressure pipe reactor and the regenerative above method; my invention. The costs of hydrogen manufacture are thus reduced, and in the long term large-scale production is made possible. My innovation constitutes the new generation of renewable energies and is devoid of harmful emissions. Merely schematic regeneration sequence (no reaction equation) (I)

title: A combined magnetohydrodynamic and electrochemical method and facility for namely electric power generation
abstract: A combined magnetohydrodynamic and electrochemical method for namely electric power generation through a hydrogen-oxygen fusion in a hydrogen fuel cell uses an electrolytic process of decomposing water to hydrogen and oxygen in a spiral magnetic electrolyser under the surface of a water environment, where the dynamization of the water environment in the water supply system of the spiral magnetic electrolyser is induced by negative pressure resulting from water being decomposed at the outlet of the spiral magnetic electrolyser. A combined magnetohydrodynamic and electrochemical facility for namely electric power generation comprising a hydrogen fuel cell including at least one spiral magnetic electrolyser (1), with its inlet (2) submerged under the surface of a water environment (3). Connected to the outlet (4) of such positioned spiral magnetic electrolyser (1) is a gas separator (5) separating produced hydrogen from oxygen and at least one hydrogen fuel cell (6) with an outlet for water (7).

title: Gas generator
abstract: The present invention provides a gas generator and comprises an electrolytic cell, a condensate filter device, and an atomizing device. The electrolytic cell is for electrolyzing electrolyzed water to generate a gas with hydrogen. The condensate filter device includes a gas pathway, a filter, and an isolated component. The isolated component is used for limiting the movement of the filter inside the gas pathway. The gas generated from the electrolytic cell is condensed and filtered through the filter for generating a filtered gas with hydrogen. The atomizing device is used for generating an atomizing gas to be mixed with the filtered gas to generate a healthy gas. The present invention uses the condensate filter device to filter out the electrolyte from the filtered gas with hydrogen to be mixed with the atomizing gas for generating the healthy gas.

title: Method for power generation by sweet potato fuel ethanol waste water
abstract: The invention relates to a method for power generation by sweet potato fuel ethanol waste water. According to the invention, raw materials are waste fermentation liquor obtained in sweet potato ethanol fermentation, and other additives. A microbiological fuel cell device comprises two electrodes, a cathode and an anode, and has a main body made of organic glass. The operation comprises the following steps: adding the raw materials into a device, performing biofilm culturing in an incubator; and replacing a substrate when an output voltage is less than 50 mV. When biofilm culturing of the microbiological fuel cell succeeds, power generation is carried out by using waste water with a concentration of 5000 mg/L and without PBS as the substrate (with the pH being adjusted to 7.3-7.5). Beneficial effects of the invention are that the microbiological fuel cell technology is used in treatment of sweet potato fuel ethanol waste water; a part of energy can be recovered during waste water treatment; thus the treatment cost of industrial waste water is reduced; and the technology has wide application potential in the field of waste water treatment.

title: Preparation method of high-stability lithium metal microsphere powder
abstract: The invention relates to a preparation method of high-stability lithium metal microsphere powder. For lithium metal microspheres, the solid metal lithium is treated in a high-temperature heating manner in high-boiling inert organic solvents, the lithium metal microspheres are fused into the liquid lithium, then the liquid lithium is quickly stirred to be dispersed into liquid-drop microsphere emulsion, protective agent molecules are added during the cooling to coat the surfaces of lithium metal liquid drops, the liquid drops are prevented from gathering, and a passivated layer is formed on the surface to prevent the lithium metal microspheres from reacting with air and water, so that the high-stability lithium metal microsphere powder which can be used in the air is generated. When the high-stability lithium metal microsphere powder is used as an electrode material additive of the lithium batteries, the first effect, the energy density and the cycle performance of the lithium batteries can be greatly improved.

title: System of fuel elements
abstract:
FIELD: power engineering. 
     SUBSTANCE: IR resistance of each single cell (2) is measured (stage S14), and highest voltage of single cell is set as threshold voltage on the basis of IR resistance and load current (stage S18). Setting highest voltage of single cell uses data chart that approximates volt-ampere characteristics of single cell in case of insufficient supply of fuel gas. In this case highest voltage of single cell is determined on the basis of voltage relative to load current produced from data chart, and IR losses calculated on the basis of IR resistance and load current. This highest voltage of single cell is compared to measured voltage of single cell (stage S22). If voltage of single cell is less than highest voltage of single cell, generation of fuel element capacity is stopped or limited (stage S24). 
     EFFECT: invention prevents degradation of membrane electrodes set, taking place as a result of insufficient supply of fuel gas. 
     10 cl, 9 dwg

title: Production of novel 6-membered ring lactone
abstract:
PURPOSE: To obtain the titled compound in high optical selectivity via a novel intermediate from an optically active 5-substituted-oxy-4-hydroxyl-1-pentyne by a carboxylation process, a reducing process and a dehydrative cyclization process performed under specific conditions.  
     CONSTITUTION: A novel substance of formula II is produced by reacting a compound of formula I [R is OH-protecting group; the asymmetric carbon atom. labeled with * is (S) OR (R)] with carbon dioxide gas in the presence of n-butyllithium. The product is reduced with hydrogen gas in the presence of a palladium-based catalyst reacted to add hydrogen atom. in the cis-direction of an acetylene bond. The resultant compound of formula III can be converted to the objective compound of formula IV useful as an intermediate for perfum ery or pharmaceuticals (antibacterial agent) in high optical selectively by dehydrative cyclization reaction. The starting compound of formula I can be produced from an optically active compound of formula V which can be easily synthesized from D-mannose.

title: Preparation of high pressure BF3/H2 mixtures
abstract: A method of filling a gas mixture supply vessel with a constituent gas is described to achieve a precise gas mixture composition wherein the gas mixture comprises at least two constituent gases. A tandem fill technique can be employed that involves flowing gas from a single source container to multiple target containers or from multiple source containers to a single target container. The method can be used to form a dopant gas mixture such as boron trifluoride and hydrogen for ion implantation applications.

title: Arrangement of a sensor element on a vacuum-insulated container system, particularly on a cryotank
abstract: The invention relates to an arrangement of a temperature sensor (6) on a vacuum-insulated container system, particularly for storing cryogenic hydrogen. The container system consists of an interior container (2) for storing a medium, a vacuum insulation layer (4) surrounding this interior container and an exterior container (1) retaining said vacuum, from which exterior container the interior container is suspended in bearing elements (3a, 3b) and in which exterior container an evacuation opening closed off by means of a closure stopper (5) is provided. The sensor system has a measuring probe (6a) arranged on the interior container and an electronics unit (6b) connected to the probe, the signals of which unit can be picked up outside the exterior container via an electrical contact. The electronics unit connected via a lead to the measuring probe is arranged on or outside the closure stopper through which this lead is run, in such a manner that the electronics unit can be replaced without breaking the vacuum insulation layer and said lead is run between the measuring probe and electronics unit through a bearing element.

title: Negative active material for rechargeable lithium battery, and rechargeable lithium battery icluding the same
abstract: Disclosed is a negative active material for a lithium secondary battery. The present invention comprises two or more inert intermetallic compounds consisting of a silicon fine particle and two or more elements, wherein the inert intermetallic compounds are distributed around the silicon fine particle, such that two or more kinds of intermetallic compounds are formed in a composite structure composed of silicon and an intermetallic compound when manufacturing a silicon negative electrode active material by using a rapid solidification process, thereby suppressing the occurrence of coarse area of silicon.

title: High-purity ferritic stainless steel having excellent corrosion resistance, and method for producing same
abstract: Disclosed is a low-alloy high-purity ferritic stainless steel having excellent corrosion resistance. Also disclosed is a method for producing the ferritic stainless steel. Specifically disclosed is a high-purity ferritic stainless steel having excellent corrosion resistance, which is characterized by comprising 0.001 to 0.02% of C, 0.01 to 0.6% of Si, 0.01 to 0.6% of Mn, 0.005 to 0.04% of P, 0.0001 to 0.01% of S, 13 to 22% of Cr, 0.001 to 0.02% of N, 0.005 to 0.05% of Al and 0.001 to 1% of Sn, with the remainder being Fe and unavoidable impurities, and which meets two requirements represented by the following formulae (1) and (2). 0 < I(Fe)/I(Cr) < 5 Formula (1), 0 < I(O)/I(Sn) < 3 Formula (2). [In the formulae, I(Fe), I(Cr), I(Sn) and I(O) represent the X-ray intensities of a Fe oxide, a Cr oxide, an Sn oxide and an oxide other than the aforementioned oxides, respectively, as measured on the surface of the steel by means of an X-ray photoelectron spectrometer]. For the purpose of increasing the modification effect of the coating film by the addition of Sn, the coating film is subjected to a finishing annealing procedure at a temperature higher than 800 DEG C, cooled to 700 DEG C or lower at a cooling rate of 10 DEG C/sec. or more, cooled by retaining at a temperature ranging from 200 to 700 DEG C for 1 minute or longer, subjected to an acid washing treatment in an aqueous solution containing nitric acid at a concentration of 5% by mass or more or, alternatively, the coating film is subjected to a bright annealing procedure under the conditions where the atmosphere gas comprises 50% by volume or more of a hydrogen gas, with the remainder being a nitrogen gas, so that the dew point of the atmosphere gas becomes -50 to -20 DEG C inclusive.

title: High-pressure hydrogen storing bottle combined valve
abstract: The invention relates to a combination valve for a high-pressure hydrogen storage cylinder, which comprises a charging check valve, a manual stop valve, an electromagnetic valve, a pressure reducing adjusting valve and a safe pressure releasing device; wherein a gas flue of a charging inlet of the hydrogen storage cylinder is provided with the check valve and the manual stop valve, a gas supply flue is introduced between the two valves and supplies gas outwards through the electromagnetic valve and the pressure reducing adjusting valve; a pressure releasing gas flue is introduced between the manual stop valve and the gas flue connected into the combination and releases pressure outwards through the safe pressure releasing device. As a cylinder valve is provided with the pressure reducing adjusting, considering the integration of the cylinder valve and the mensurability and output controllability of the gas, the invention can implement the low-pressure output; therefore, a gas supply output system has very high safe reliability and simultaneously can be used as a main valve of a hydrogen storage cylinder group; other gas cylinders of the hydrogen storage cylinder group can supply gas outwards after pressure reduction through the main valve.

title: Application of iron (II) -doped molybdenum sulfide material in self-powered piezoelectricity-enhanced hydrogen production
abstract: The invention provides an application of an iron-doped molybdenum sulfide material in self-powered piezoelectricity-enhanced hydrogen production. The iron-doped molybdenum sulfide material (Fe-MoS) 2 ) Comprising molybdenum sulphide MoS responsive to near-infrared light 2 The material and ferrous iron ions doped in the molybdenum sulfide and on the surface of the molybdenum sulfide. The iron-doped molybdenum sulfide material provided by the invention can effectively utilize the mechanical energy (such as vibration, noise, ultrasonic wave, stirring and other energy) in the nature, and the hydrogen content is as high as 19.6The ammonia borane with the weight percent is used as a hydrogen storage material to prepare hydrogen energy at normal temperature and normal pressure, and the self-powered piezoelectric enhanced hydrogen production is realized. And the hydrogen fuel can be used along with the preparation, thus solving the problem that the hydrogen is difficult to store. The preparation method of the iron-doped molybdenum sulfide material provided by the invention is simple and feasible, and is green and environment-friendly.

abstract: A lithium solid-state battery (1) is proposed, comprising a lithium anode (10), a cathode (20), a first separator layer (30) for electrically separating the lithium anode (10) from the cathode (20), wherein the first separator layer (30) a sulfide solid electrolyte, and a second separator layer (40) for electrically separating the lithium anode (10) from the cathode (20), wherein the second separator layer (40) is disposed between the first separator layer (30) and the lithium anode (10) and the second separator layer (40) comprises a sulfided solid electrolyte, wherein the first separator layer (30) is arranged between the cathode (20) and the second separator layer (40) and has a greater layer thickness than the second separator layer (40), in particular the first one Separator layer (30) has a layer thickness at least twice as large as the second separator layer (40), preferably the first separator layer (30 ) has a layer thickness at least ten times as great as the second separator layer (40), the porosity of the second separator layer (40) being in a range from about 0% to about 4%, preferably in a range from about 0% to about 3%, more preferably in a range of about 0% to about 1%.

title: Device for manufacturing semiconductor
abstract:
PURPOSE: To reduce the surface defect density of an epitaxial film by providing a high vacuum space or an inert gas filling space between a growing chamber of the film using a solid material and a growing chamber using gas material, and conveying a wafer through the spaces.  
     CONSTITUTION: A substrate 11 is secured to a substrate holder 12 in a system of high vacuum chamber 1, material gas, such as hydrogen gas, organic metal and arsine, etc. is introduced to the chamber, fed in vacuum toward the substrate 11, thermally decomposed to accumulate an epitaxial film of GaAs, GaAlAs on the substrate. Solid materials, such as arsenic 25, silicon 26, aluminum 27, gallium 28 are filled in high temperature furnace 24 in a high vacuum chamber 2, they are fed in high vacuum as molecular beam toward the substrate 11 at the time of heating to epitaxially grow by selectively doping GaAs, GaAlAs on the substrate. Thus, a semiconductor thin film in which the defect density on the film is low and excellent controllability for an ultrafine film can be formed.

abstract: <P>PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To solve a problem that a fuel nozzle designed to be used with a natural gas fuel is not completely adaptable to be used with a fuel of high reactivity, as in many cases, a hydrogen fuel has high reactivity in comparison with the natural gas fuel, and combustion of the hydrogen fuel more easily progresses.  <P>SOLUTION: The fuel injection nozzle (100) includes a body member (102) having an upstream wall (104) opposing a downstream wall (106), a baffle member (108), a first chamber (112), a second chamber (110) communicating with the first chamber (112), a fuel inlet communicating with the first chamber (112) operated to emit a first gas into the first chamber (112), and a plurality of mixing tubes (114), each of the mixing tubes (114) having a first inlet (116) communicating with an aperture in the upstream wall operated to receive a second gas, a second inlet communicating with a tube outer surface and a tube inner surface operated to translate the first gas into the mixing tube, a mixing portion operated to mix the first gas and the second gas, and an outlet communicating with an aperture in the downstream wall operated to emit the mixed first and second gasses.  <P>COPYRIGHT: (C)2011,JPO&amp;INPIT

title: Process for preparing nano-bars with high air-sensitive property to ethanol by inducing zinc oxide
abstract: The invention provides a method for making a tin oxide nano rod which is highly gas-sensitive to ethanol by zinc oxide inducing. The zinc acetate solution and the sodium hydroxide solution are mixed and undergo the ultrasound step; the solution mixture is put in a high pressure kettle, heated and then cooled down to the room temperature, depositions generated are washed by distilled water and ethanol and dried, and the zinc oxide is obtained; the zinc oxide is dispersed in the ethanol water solution, and the ethanol water solution is added with potassium stannate and carbamide respectively and evenly mixed; the mixture is heated in the high pressure kettle and cooled down to the room temperature, depositions generated are washed by distilled water and ethanol and dried, and the tin oxide nano rod is obtained. At the low working temperature of 250 DEG C, the gas sensitivities of the tin oxide nano rod prepared by the invention to ethanol gas with the concentration of 10ppm and ethanol gas with the concentration of 300ppm are as high as 12.9 and 77.8 respectively; and in the range of between 10 and 500ppm, the sensitivity is in linear relation with the ethanol concentration; on the contrary, for hydrogen gas with the concentration 500ppm, methane with the concentration 1000ppm and butane with the concentration 1000ppm, sensitivities of the tin oxide nano rod are below 2.

title: Desulfurizing agent and method of desulfurization with the same
abstract:
The invention provides a desulfurizing agent which attains effective removal of sulfur from a hydrocarbon feedstock and/or an oxygen-containing hydrocarbon feedstock so as to attain a considerably low sulfur level and which has a long service life; a process for producing hydrogen for fuel cells, which process includes steam-reforming, partial-oxidation-reforming, or autothermal-reforming of a hydrocarbon feedstock and/or an oxygen-containing hydrocarbon feedstock which has been desulfurized by use of the desulfurizing agent; a fuel cell system employing hydrogen produced through the process. 
     The desulfurizing agent for removing a sulfur compound from a hydrocarbon feedstock and/or an oxygen-containing hydrocarbon feedstock, the agent containing nickel, or a combination of nickel and copper, and silicon, and having a bulk density of 0.95 to 2.0 g/cm 3 , a pore volume of 0.10 to 0.40 mL/g, a micropore surface area of 100 to 250 m 2 /g, and an external surface area of 100 m 2 /g or less. The process for producing hydrogen for fuel cells employs the desulfurizing agent. The fuel cell system employs hydrogen produced through the process.

title: Nonaqueous Eletrolyte Comprising Oxyanion and Lithium Secondary Battery Using the Same
abstract: Disclosed is an electrolyte comprising: (a) an electrolyte salt; (b) a non-aqueous electrolyte solvent; and (c) a binary or multinary metal oxide salt. An electrochemical device comprising the same electrolyte is also disclosed. The metal oxide salt used in the electrolyte is dissolved in a non-aqueous solvent and generates oxyanions capable of improving corrosion resistance of metals. Therefore. the electrolyte prevents corrosion of metallic materials present in an electrochemical device, caused by extreme conditions, such as overcharge, overdischarge and high-temperature storage conditions, to which the device is exposed. Further, the electrolyte prevents degradation in the quality of an electrochemical device, caused by corrosion of metallic materials.

title: Negative electrode for lithium rechargeable battery and lithium rechargeable battery adopting the same
abstract:
An active material layer having a metal-carbon composite active material particle composed of at least one active material selected from a metal or a metal oxide and a carbon-based active material, soft graphite particles, the active material particles, and a binder for bonding and fixing the graphite particles to each other. And a current collector in which the active material layer is laminated on the surface, wherein the soft graphite particles have a pellet density of 1.6 to 1.9 g / when the pellet is formed at a press pressure of 1 ton per square centimeter. Disclosed is a negative electrode for a lithium secondary battery and a secondary battery employing the same, wherein the negative electrode is a cc (cubic centimeter). 
     According to the present invention, the soft graphite acts as a buffer during the volume expansion of the metal-carbon composite active material particles according to the filling, thereby suppressing the volume expansion caused by the filling when viewed as a whole of the active material layer, and between the active material particles including the soft graphite. By maintaining and securing the conductive contact point, it is possible to prevent battery capacity deterioration caused by repeated charge and discharge and to increase battery cycle life.

title: Oxygen generator
abstract:
PURPOSE: To stably generate gaseous oxygen with a simple generator without producing noise by pouring an aq. CuSO 4  soln. as an electrolytic soln. into an electrolytic cell provided with an insoluble anode, supplying electric current and feeding a Cu(OH) 2  suspension.  
     CONSTITUTION: 1N aq. soln. P of a strong acid salt of a metal such as CuSO 4  is poured into an electrolytic cell 1 divided with a diaphragm 4 and electric current is supplied between an insoluble graphite anode 3 and a Cu cathode 2 to generate gaseous O 2  from the surface of the anode 3 by an electrolytic reaction. At this time, Cu in the electrolytic soln. P is deposited on the cathode 2 to avoid the generation of H 2  and to prevent the danger of explosion. In accordance with the progress of the electrolytic reaction, Cu ions as cations deposit on the cathode 2, the concn. of Cu ions lowers and the concn. of H 2  ions increases in opposition to anions. In order to keep the concn. of Cu ions constant, a Cu(OH) 2  suspension Q is fed from a tank 5 in the form of paste.

text: Oxygen generator PURPOSE: To stably generate gaseous oxygen with a simple generator without producing noise by pouring an aq. CuSO 4 soln. as an electrolytic soln. into an electrolytic cell provided with an insoluble anode, supplying electric current and feeding a Cu(OH) 2 suspension. CONSTITUTION: 1N aq. soln. P of a strong acid salt of a metal such as CuSO 4 is poured into an electrolytic cell 1 divided with a diaphragm 4 and electric current is supplied between an insoluble graphite anode 3 and a Cu cathode 2 to generate gaseous O 2 from the surface of the anode 3 by an electrolytic reaction. At this time, Cu in the electrolytic soln. P is deposited on the cathode 2 to avoid the generation of H 2 and to prevent the danger of explosion. In accordance with the progress of the electrolytic reaction, Cu ions as cations deposit on the cathode 2, the concn. of Cu ions lowers and the concn. of H 2 ions increases in opposition to anions. In order to keep the concn. of Cu ions constant, a Cu(OH) 2 suspension Q is fed from a tank 5 in the form of paste. COPYRIGHT: (C)1988,JPO&Japio

title: Method of heat treatment of ni based superalloy for wave type grain-boundary and ni based superalloy the same
abstract: To improve the resistance to creep, fatigue and stress corrosion cracking, and to provide an economical and convenient heat treatment method for nickel-based alloys and alloys thereby. The method and the alloy in the heat treatment process after the production and processing of the nickel-based alloy, first subjected to the solution treatment in the high temperature region, then immediately cooled to 1 to 15 ℃ / min to the middle temperature region for aging treatment, and then aged Aging treatment is carried out by maintaining for a predetermined time in the middle temperature region. It is then air cooled after aging.

title: Method for producing highly pure zinc sulfide by sodium hydrosulfite filter residue
abstract: The invention provides a method for producing highly pure zinc sulfide by sodium hydrosulfite filter residue, comprising the following steps of: preparing the sodium hydrosulfite filter residue into a suspension and also preparing a diluted hydrochloric acid solution at the same time, reacting under the condition of stirring to obtain a solution containing zinc chloride; purifying and then carrying out vulcanization reaction with sulfureted hydrogen gas to obtain a mixture containing zinc sulfide sediment and a hydrochloric acid dilute solution; and filtering the mixture to obtain a clear hydrochloric acid dilute solution and a zinc sulfide cake, and then washing, drying and crushing the zinc sulfide to obtain the products of high-purity zinc sulfide powder. By the method, zinc hydroxide in the sodium hydrosulfite filter residue is transferred into the zinc sulfide with more important economic value, thus the invention fully develops and utilizes the hidden economic value of the sodium hydrosulfite filter residue and also provides a new way for the comprehensive utilization and governance of the sodium hydrosulfite filter residue. In addition, the invention has simple operation, low equipment investment cost and few by-products, and is suitable for promotion and application.

title: Lng carrier having lng loading/unloading system
abstract:
The present invention relates to a liquefied natural gas carrier having a liquefied gas loading / unloading system, comprising: an STL apparatus installed in a carrier to flow in and out natural gas, and a liquefied apparatus for liquefying natural gas introduced through the STL apparatus in a cryogenic state; At least one independent cargo hold installed on the carrier to store or liquefy the liquefied gas liquefied by the liquefier and one or more membrane cargo hold installed on the carrier in series with the independent cargo hold; And a vaporization device for vaporizing the liquefied gas stored in the independent cargo hold. Therefore, according to the present invention, the membrane-shaped cargo hold and the independent cargo hold are mixed, and the loading / unloading of the liquefied gas is performed through the independent cargo hold of the mixed cargo hold, thereby minimizing the effect of sloshing from the independent cargo hold, thereby preventing safety accidents. The risk can be prevented in advance, and especially when the bow side of an independent cargo hold having excellent structural strength is satisfied, the operating conditions of the polar region can also be satisfied.

title: Manufacture of semiconductor device
abstract: (57) [Abstract] [PROBLEMS] To provide a method for manufacturing a semiconductor device, and particularly to a method for forming an insulating film for flattening the surface of a semiconductor substrate, having an excellent reflow property and capable of flattening the substrate surface It is an object to provide a method for forming a film. [Structure] Silicon alkoxide, phosphorus alkoxide, and boron alkoxide are bubbled by using hydrogen gas, respectively, to substitute a methyl group or an ethyl group with hydrogen to form a hydroxyl group, and a silicon alkoxide and a phosphorus alkoxide having a hydroxyl group are formed. Boron alkoxide and ozone are brought into contact with the substrate to grow a boron phosphorus-doped glass film, and non-doped glass and phosphorus-doped glass can be similarly grown.
