title: Process for the Continuous Preparation of Saturated and Unsaturated Secondary Fatty Amines.
abstract: Aliphatic saturated or unsaturated secondary fatty amines are prepared by hydrogenating a saturated or unsaturated fatty acid nitrile containing 8-22 C atoms and continuously passing the nitrile, at a temperature of 80- 250 C. and a pressure of 30-200 atmospheres, with hydrogen over a fixed-bed copper-chromium catalyst, the partial pressure of NH 3 in the hydrogen gas being maintained at 3-15% by volume. Preferred starting materials are the nitriles of caprylic, capric, lauric, myristic, palmitic, stearic, arachidic and behemic acids, or mixtures of two or more. Thus tallow fatty acid, coconut oil acid, oleic acid and soy bean oil acid may be used. The catalyst is preferably prepared by depositing a copper compound, a chromium compound and an alkali metal compound on a silica gel carrier and is reduced in a hydrogen flow at elevated temperature before use. Examples slow the hydrogenation, under various conditions, of the nitriles of tallow and soy bean fatty acids and stearic acid.

title: Distributed power supply system
abstract: The distributed power system of the present invention comprises an energy conversion device, a power storage device, a first power supply circuit, a water electrolysis device, a hydrogen storage device, a fuel cell device, and a second power supply circuit. The energy conversion device converts and outputs the renewable energy into the first electrical energy. The power storage device is electrically connected to the energy conversion device for receiving and storing the first electrical energy, and can output the second electrical energy. The first power supply circuit is electrically connected to the power storage device to deliver the second electrical energy. The water electrolysis device is electrically connected to the energy conversion device for electrolyzing water to generate hydrogen and oxygen. The hydrogen storage device is connected to the water electrolysis device for storing and outputting hydrogen. The fuel cell device is coupled to the hydrogen storage device for receiving hydrogen gas and converting and outputting the hydrogen gas into a third electrical energy. The second power supply circuit is electrically coupled to the fuel cell device to deliver a third electrical energy. Wherein, when the electrical energy stored in the electrical storage device is full, the water electrolysis device receives the first electrical energy to electrolyze the water to generate hydrogen and oxygen.

title: Gas stove
abstract: The invention provides a fuel gas kitchen range for supplying heat by using renewable energy resources, which uses fuel and a kitchen range to achieve the combustion between fuel and oxygen in the kitchen range, and is provided with a wind power direct current generator, an electrolysis water unit, a hydrogen storage unit and a combustion kitchen range, wherein the wind power direct current generator is driven by wind power to generate direct current; the electrolysis water unit generates hydrogen which is stored in the hydrogen storage unit; when used, the hydrogen is burnt in the combustion kitchen range so as to generate heat and achieve heat supply, thus the fuel gas kitchen range makes use of the low price of wind power to generate the hydrogen to achieve energy storage, can avoid the instability of the wind power, can satisfy the requirement on a great deal of instant heat supply in a kitchen, achieve energy resource recycling, and is suitable for citizens in windy areas and coastal area, and the like.

title: Composite hard-surface material and manufacturing method thereof
abstract: The invention provides a manufacturing method of a composite hard-surface material. The manufacturing method includes the steps that multiple hard alloy slices are dispersedly fixed to the surface of a metal matrix; bead weld is conducted on the hard alloy slices and the metal matrix through welding flux and the composite hard-surface material is obtained, wherein the welding flux comprises nickel-based alloy powder, tungsten carbide particles and boron nitride powder. In the process of manufacturing the composite hard-surface material, the adopted welding flux comprises the nickel-based alloy powder, the tungsten carbide particles and the boron nitride powder, fluidity and corrosion resistance can be improved through the nickel-based alloy powder, hardness can be improved through the tungsten carbide particles, the friction coefficient can be reduced effectively through the boron nitride powder, the welding flux is good in fluidity, high in hardness and good in weldability, and the composite hard-surface material manufactured through the melding flux has good wearproof performance. The invention further provides the composite hard-surface material.

title: Silicon-carbon negative electrode material, preparation method and lithium ion battery
abstract: The invention discloses a silicon-carbon negative electrode material, a preparation method and a lithium ion battery. The silicon-carbon negative electrode material comprises: the shell coats the inner core, the inner core is made of composite materials of expanded graphite and porous silicon, the particle size of the porous silicon is in a nanometer level, the particle size of the expanded graphite is in a micrometer level, and the shell is an amorphous carbon layer. Therefore, the silicon-carbon negative electrode material has smaller volume expansion rate, higher cycle efficiency and good rate performance, so that a lithium ion battery using the silicon-carbon negative electrode material has good service performance.

title: Systems and methods for fuel desulfurization
abstract: Methods and apparatus are provided for a fuel cell including a fuel desulfurization system. The fuel cell system includes a source of fuel and a fuel desulfurization system fluidly coupled to the source of fuel to receive the fuel in a liquid phase. The fuel desulfurization system includes a vacuum system that depressurizes the fuel to convert at least a portion of the fuel from the liquid phase to a gaseous phase. The fuel cell system also includes a fuel cell stack fluidly coupled to the fuel desulfurization system to receive fuel from the fuel desulfurization system in the gaseous phase.

title: Polymer Battery Cell Comprising Jelly-roll Typed Electrode Assembly
abstract: The present invention relates to a battery cell having a structure of arranging a jelly-roll type electrode assembly inside a battery case, wherein the jelly-roll type electrode assembly includes a positive electrode/separation membrane/negative electrode structure. The positive electrode and the negative electrode have a structure including an electrode mixing layer containing an electrode active material applied to one side or both sides of a sheet type current collector. The jelly-roll type electrode assembly has a shape arranging a shorter axis in the direction having relatively little volumetric expansion, and a longer axis in the direction having relatively large volumetric expansion.

title: Lithium cells
abstract: A lithium cell comprising a negative electrode containing lithium, a positive electrode, and an electrolyte disposed between the positive and negative electrodes is improved in internal resistance and shelf life when the negative electrode is surface treated with a composition containing from more than 1% to 5% by weight of water.

title: Healthy water preparation system
abstract: The utility model discloses a healthy water preparation system. The preparation system comprises hydrogen gas conveying equipment, a membrane separation device, oxygen gas conveying equipment and water generation equipment, wherein the hydrogen gas conveying equipment acquires hydrogen gas through preparation or is only used for conveying prepared hydrogen gas; the hydrogen gas conveying equipment is connected with the membrane separation device, protium is separated from the hydrogen gas through the membrane separation device, and deuterium and tritium in the hydrogen gas cannot be filtered from the membrane separation device; the oxygen gas conveying equipment supplies oxygen gas through preparation or is only used for conveying prepared oxygen gas; the water generation equipment is used for receiving the hydrogen gas prepared by hydrogen gas preparation equipment and the oxygen gas conveyed by the oxygen gas conveying equipment, so as to prepare healthy water. According to the healthy water preparation system disclosed by the utility model, light water which does not contain radioactive elements, namely deuterium and tritium, can be prepared, so that the quality of lives of people is improved, and the demands of people on health are met; in addition, the system is portable and is conveniently carried by people.

title: Manufacture of aluminum-water type heat pipe
abstract: PURPOSE:To suppress corrosion reaction between aluminum and water and consequently improve the long term reliability of the operating performance of a heat pipe by a method wherein organic additive such as triethanolamine or the like is added to water in advance and, after that, the water is sealed in a vessel. CONSTITUTION:A water type heat pipe consists of a closed cylindrical vessel 2, which is made of aluminum or the surface of which is treated by boehmite process and in the interior of which wick 3 and working liquid 4 are sealed. The working liquid 4 is prepared by adding 0.05-2.5g/l of organic additive consisting of triethanolamine to ion-exchanged water or distilled water. When the heat pipe is in action under the temperature of 100-250 deg.C, the working liquid 4 and its stream turn to circulate within the interior of the container 2, resulting in producing dense boehmite film onto the inner surface part of the container 2. Because no hydrogen gas generates, no hydrogen gas stagnates in a condenser end as non-condensable gas and consequently no deterioration of the operating performance occurs, resulting in improving the reliability of the heat pipe.

title: Oxyhydrogen gas generator
abstract: The utility model discloses an oxyhydrogen gas generator which belongs to the technical field of energy production and comprises an electrolysis box and a water-gas separator, wherein the electrolysis box comprises a positive plate and a negative plate; at least two hollow gland bushes are arranged between the positive plate and the negative plate; a pole piece is arranged between every two gland bushes; pressure plates are arranged outside the positive plate and the negative plate; the pressure plates, polar plates, the gland bushes and the pole pieces are fixed into a whole; water inlets and water gas outlets are respectively formed on the positive plate and the negative plate and communicated with the hollow gland bushes; a venthole and a limber hole are formed on each pole piece; and the water gas outlets are connected with the water-gas separator. The oxyhydrogen gas generator increases the water amount of electrolysis at a time and improves the electrolysis efficiency effectively, and can satisfy the gas consumption requirement in a job site.

title: Method for heat treating metallic materials and apparatus therefor
abstract: The present invention presents a method and an apparatus for heat treating a metallic material by performing hydrogen absorption in or desorption from the metallic material. The method includes the steps of recovering the hydrogen gas released from a desorption step and recycling it to a hydrogen absorbing step. Hydrogen recovering can be performed either in a device containing a hydrogen absorbing alloy or in a second heat treating furnace containing the metallic material. Various controlling devices are used to regulate the process of heat treating and recovering of hydrogen gas.

title: Negative electrode plate and secondary battery comprising the same
abstract: The disclosure relates to a secondary battery comprising a negative electrode plate the same. Specifically, the present disclosure provides secondary battery, comprising a negative electrode plate, the negative electrode plate comprises a negative electrode current collector and a negative electrode layer coated on at least one surface of the negative electrode current collector, the negative electrode layer comprising a negative electrode active material, wherein the negative electrode active material comprises a graphite material, and the negative electrode layer fulfills the condition: 0.75≤7.8/D50+1.9*D50/(VOI)2≤2.0, wherein D50 represents a volume distribution average particle diameter of particles of the negative electrode active material in micron; VOI represents the OI value of the negative electrode layer. The secondary battery has the combination of high energy density, fast charge, and long cycle life.

title: The preparation method of support type reduced precious metal catalyst
abstract: The present invention provides a kind of preparation methods of support type reduced precious metal catalyst, comprising the following steps: precursor solution of the preparation containing soluble precious-metal；Catalyst carrier is immersed in the precursor solution, ultrasonic vibration, is stirred, suspension is made；Solid after solid-liquid suspension is separated is heat-treated；Solid after heat treatment is subjected to gas phase photo-reduction to get support type reduced precious metal catalyst.The mass content of noble metal is 0.01-10% in catalyst obtained.Support type reduced precious metal catalyst of the invention have many advantages, such as preparation process is simple, precious metal dispersion is good, good stability, preparation and reaction condition it is mild.

title: Unpowered independent deep-water operation pontoon inflating device and method
abstract: The invention relates to a deep-water operation pontoon inflating device and a deep-water operation pontoon inflating method, and aims to provide an unpowered independent deep-water operation pontoon inflating device and an unpowered independent deep-water operation pontoon inflating method. The device comprises a pontoon, a sodium borohydride container and a tubular reactor, wherein a water outlet is reserved at the bottom of the pontoon; the upper part of the tubular reactor is connected to the upper end of the pontoon through a pipeline; the bottom of the tubular reactor is connected to the bottom of the sodium borohydride container through the pipeline; a water inlet is reserved on the upper part of the sodium borohydride container; a mechanical timing pressure open valve and a one-way valve are arranged on the pipeline of the water inlet; sodium borohydride powder is filled in the sodium borohydride container; and a porous catalyst with a three-dimensional structure is filled into the tubular reactor. The device does not need an air compressor pump; and the suspension depth of the pontoon is determined according to a set pressure of the pressure open valve, so that the suspension depth can be controlled accurately to realize timing recovery. The device and the method have a broad application prospect in the aspects such as marine scientific research, sea-bottom survey, submarine cable detection and the like.

title: Water seal tank of dry quenching furnace
abstract: The invention discloses a water seal tank of a dry quenching furnace. The water seal tank is characterized in that a water inlet tube 3 is arranged inside the water seal tank 2. During usage, cold water directly enters a water seal tank 1 from the water inlet tube 3, namely enters between an inner side wall 4 of the water seal tank 2 and a skirt side 5 of a furnace lid ), so that cold water is supplemented continuously to the inside of the water seal tank 2, and water inside does not boil, and therefore, water vapor does not enter a recycle gas of the dry quenching furnace, hydrogen gas content of the recycle gas is lowered, and safe operation of the dry quenching furnace is guaranteed.

title: Detecting agent
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To enable the detecting of steam and fine powder of phosphorus, arsenic, and selenium contained in a gas by utilizing molybdic acid and/or a molybdate salt carried by inorganic carriers as discoloring elements. SOLUTION: This detecting agent is used for the detection of phosphorus, arsenic, and selenium contained in nitrogen, hydrogen, argon, helium, air, etc. Molybdic acid and/or a molybdate salt are used as the discoloring elements of the detecting agent. Or a cupric salt is used together with molybdic acid and/or a molybdate salt. These discoloring elements are carried by inorganic carriers to be a detecting agent. When the steam or fine powder of phosphorus, arsenic, and selenium comes into contact with this detecting agent, the color of the detecting agent sensitively turns from light yellow to bluish green - dark blue. Therefore, they can be detected with high accuracy. As this detecting agent is not affected by hydrogen, air, or hydrogen gas, incorrect detection does not occur even in the case when contained in nitrogen, air, and hydrogen.

title: Method for producing alcohols
abstract: A method for producing alcohols which comprises reducing esters or lactones with hydrogen gas in the presence of a catalyst comprising (i) a ruthenium compound, (ii) a monodentate monophosphine or a bidentate bisphosphine, and (iii) an amine. Examples of the catalyst include a ruthenium (Ru) complex represented by the formula:RuX 1 X 2 (L P ) m (L N ) n  [X 1  and X 2  each represent an anionic ligand, L P  represents a phosphine ligand, m is 1 when L P  is bidentate, while m is 2 when L P  is monodentate, L N  represents an amine ligand, and n is 1 when L N  is bidentate, while n is 2 when L N  is monodentate.] and a catalyst comprising an amine and a ruthenium (Ru) complex of the formula: RuX 1 X 2 (L P   1 ) r  [L P   1  represents a monophosphine ligand and r is 3 or 4.].

title: Semiconductor device and its manufacture
abstract:
PURPOSE: To prevent a defect due to damage by a plasma while an effect to enhance a characteristic of a TFT is being secured by a method wherein a semiconductor device whose channel region of an insulated-gate field-effect transistor is composed of a non-single-crystal semiconductor is exposed to a plasma atmosphere containing ammonia.  
     CONSTITUTION: A non-single-crystal silicon thin film is deposited on an insulating amorphous material 1-1 ; after that, a thin film 1-2 is formed; a gate oxide film 1-4 is formed by a thermal oxidation method. By this oxidation process, a crystal growth operation of the thin film 1-2 progresses; this thin film is transformed into a polycrystalline silicon thin film 1-3. Then, a gate electrode 1-5 is formed; ions of an impurity element are implanted by making use of the gate electrode 1-5 as a mask; a source region 1-6 and a drain region 1-7 are formed. Then, an interlayer insulating film 1-8 is deposited; a heat treatment is executed; after that, a substrate is set in a reaction chamber; a gas containing hydrogen gas or ammonia gas is introduced. When high-frequency power is applied, a plasma is generated easily; a hydrogen radical is introduced into the substrate. Thereby, a high performance of a transistor can be realized without a defect due to damage by the plasma.

title: Method for estimating remaining amount of electrolyte of fuel battery
abstract: PURPOSE:To estimate remaining amount of electrolyte of matrix layer by supplying test gases to both electrode layers in different concentration and detecting an electromotive force generated by a fuel battery during such period. CONSTITUTION:As a gas which generates electro-chemical reaction with both electrode layers, there is a hydrogen gas, for example, in case the electrolyte is phosphoric acid. Those which reacts with any of the positive and negative electrodes and becomes anion or cation in the electrolyte may be used. When the test gases in different concentration are supplied to both electrode layers, an electromotive force is respectively generated in each electrode layer in accordance with the concentration of test gas and difference of such electromotive forces is generated as an electromotive force of a battery. When vacant holes in the porous matrix layer are not filled with electrolyte, the test gas flows, even in the least amount, toward low concentration side from high concentration side. As a result, difference between both electrode layers becomes small and an electromotive force of battery is lowered.

title: Secondary zinc electrode additive and its preparing method
abstract: The present invention relates to a secondary zinc electrode additive agent which has the main chemical composition of Re and C, wherein the Re is ZnO or SnO2 or a mixture of the ZnO and the SnO2, and the c is graphite. The additive agent can also contain the conductive oxides of Sb2O3, Bi2O3, Al2O3, SiO2, PbO, and TiO2, each oxide and the graphite are mixed and sintered to be a forming. The present invention has the advantages that a zinc oxide based conductive oxide can serve as a crystal center when a zinc electrode is charged, the utilization rate and the discharge capacity of the active substances of the zinc electrode can be improved, the generation of zinc dendritic crystal can be reduced and stopped, and the cycle life of the electrode can be extended; because the composite oxides have fine conductivity, the internal resistance of the electrode can be reduced, and the solution and the hydrogen gas precipitation of zinc in the electrolyte can be stopped; the present invention has fine effect that the synthetical electrochemical performance of the secondary zinc electrode is improved.

title: Fuel cell unconverted gas combustion processing device
abstract: (57) [Problem] To provide an unreacted gas combustion treatment device for a fuel cell, in which the device can be simplified and reduced in cost and ignition can be easily confirmed. A combustion chamber (3) for performing combustion, a blower (4) for supplying combustion air to the combustion chamber (3), an exhaust gas supply line (5) having a fuel discharge hole (6) at the tip, and the fuel discharge hole The burner unit 1 is constituted by a ceramic heater 7 provided above the fuel discharge hole 6 and igniting unreacted gas ejected from the fuel discharge hole 6. In the burner section 1, unreacted hydrogen gas discharged from the fuel cell is diffused and burned. Further, a flame monitoring device 9 is provided, and the flame detecting means 8   The ignition of the burner is confirmed by monitoring the resistance value of the ceramic heater 7 as appropriate.

title: Cryogenic liquid system.
abstract: Cryosystem for cryogenic liquids which contain small amounts of foreign fluids with higher melting and boiling points, wherein the cryosystem comprises at least one tank, pipelines and assembly units and is used under defined gravitation conditions. The pipelines slope, at least in some areas, toward the tank, and areas of the pipelines and assembly units, from which the cryogenic liquid and the foreign fluids cannot flow back into the tank under the action of gravity, are provided with drain openings and drain lines, which open into the tank or into at least one additional collection tank.

title: Method and equipment for electrochemically generating ozone by oxygen-contained gas
abstract: The invention discloses a method and equipment for electrochemically generating ozone by oxygen-contained gas. The method is to electrolyze water by at least two water electrolysis pools, wherein the electricity is supplied to one water electrolysis pool by a hydrogen fuel cell; hydrogen generated by cathode sides of the water electrolysis pools and ozone and oxygen generated by anode sides are collected; the oxygen-contained gas and the collected hydrogen are respectively supplied to a positive pole and a negative pole of the hydrogen fuel cell; and water generated by the hydrogen fuel cell is transported to the water electrolysis pools. The equipment is provided with one hydrogen fuel cell; an exhaust port of the hydrogen fuel cell is connected with a gas-liquid separator; a water outlet of the gas-liquid separator is connected with raw material inlets of at least two water electrolysis pools; an electric output end of the hydrogen fuel cell is connected with one water electrolysis pool; hydrogen outlets of the water electrolysis pools are connected with the negative pole of the hydrogen fuel cell; and ozone and oxygen outlets of the water electrolysis pools are connected with a valve.

title: System for generating combined energy
abstract:
The present invention relates to a system for generating electricity using water, which is a brown gas obtained by electrochemically electrolyzing water using a brown gas generator to generate electric power by driving a brown gas engine. Part of the gas can be recycled to the brown gas generator, and at the same time, the exhaust gas of the brown gas engine and the heat generated from the brown gas generator can be recovered and used as high temperature hot water, and the high temperature cooling generated when the brown gas engine is operated. Water is heat-treated to produce a low temperature cooling water to cool the Brown gas engine. 
     Therefore, the present invention generates electricity as brown gas obtained by electrolysis of water, and can smoothly supply heating and electricity necessary for real life, and does not cause any environmental pollution.

title: Vessel Including Engines and Method of Reliquefying BOG for The Same
abstract: Disclosed is a vessel including an engine. The vessel including an engine includes: a self-heat exchanger exchanging heat of evaporative gas discharged from a storage tank; a multistep compressor compressing the evaporative gas, passing through the self-heat exchanger, in a multistep mode; a first decompressor expanding a part of the evaporative gas passing through the self-heat exchanger after being compressed by the multistep compressor; and a second decompressor expanding another part of the evaporative gas passing through the self-heat exchanger after compressed by the multistep compressor. The self-heat exchanger cools the evaporative gas, compressed by the multistep compressor, by using the evaporative gas, discharged from the storage tank, and the evaporative gas, expanded by the first decompressor, as a refrigerant.

title: Stable ammonia absorbents
abstract: The present disclosure relates to systems and methods of making ammonia using stable ammonia absorbents. The system and method for producing ammonia, comprises a reactor comprising a catalyst that converts at least a portion of nitrogen feed gas and at least a portion of hydrogen feed gas to ammonia (NH3) forming a reaction mixture comprising the ammonia, unreacted nitrogen, and unreacted hydrogen. An absorber configured to selectively absorb ammonia from the reaction mixture at a temperature of about 180 deg. C to 330 deg. C and a pressure of about 1-20 bar, the absorber comprising a solid absorbent. Preferably the solid absorbent is at least one metal halide and a solid support. The unabsorbed ammonium, the unreacted nitrogen, and unreacted hydrogen gas are recycled to the reactor.

title: Method for producing methanol by use of nuclear heat and power generating plant
abstract: Provided herein is a process for producing methanol which was developed to reduce the emission of carbon dioxide which is responsible for global warming. The process involves the steps of generating steam by the use of nuclear heat of a high-temperature gas-cooled nuclear reactor, decomposing the steam into hydrogen by means of a steam electrolyzer, and synthesizing methanol from this hydrogen and carbon dioxide obtained from a carbon dioxide source. The process also involves the steps of converting carbon dioxide and hydrogen into carbon monoxide and steam by means of a reverse shift reactor, forming hydrogen and carbon monoxide of almost the same composition as the conventional one, and reacting said hydrogen and carbon monoxide into methanol by means of a methanol synthesis column. The process permits the use of an existing methanol production facility. The process may be modified such that the gasified gas composed of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen is mixed with hydrogen gas generated by the steam electrolyzer, and the resulting mixed gas is converted into methanol. Also, a power generating plant is provided in which oxygen enriched air, generated by the steam electrolyzer using nuclear heat of the high-temperature gas-cooled nuclear reactor, is used as combustion air.

title: A gas purification apparatus
abstract: The present invention relates to a gas purification apparatus, comprising a plurality of column with each adsorbent; a plurality of valves which control gas paths to purify gas through selected columns; a sensor part which detects concentration of components of gas adsorbed to the adsorbent; a control part which controls the valve based on a result of comparing concentration detected from the sensor part with predetermined concentration, thereby selecting columns to purify gas; and a regeneration part for regenerating the adsorbent of the column used for purifying gas wherein the gas purification apparatus is allowed to detect concentration of components adsorbed to the adsorbent and accordingly calculates time of regenerating the adsorbent, thereby increasing reliability for purifying gas and reducing cost because of reducing unnecessary regeneration of the adsorbent.

title: Method for manufacturing iron-silicon sintered soft magnetic alloy
abstract:
The present invention relates to a method for producing a Fe-Si soft magnetic alloy used as a magnetic core material such as a transformer, a motor, a generator core, and a yoke material of a magnetic circuit. The present invention provides a method for producing a Fe-Si-based sintered soft magnetic alloy having excellent soft magnetic properties even at low temperatures.
     
    In order to achieve the above object, the present invention comprises a Fe-Si + Mx compound composition in the method for producing a soft magnetic alloy containing Fe-Si as a main component, M is B, Sn, Cr, V, P, C, One or two or more selected from Al and Zn, and X is melted by a plasma arc in an inert atmosphere in a composition range of 0.1-2% by weight, and then cooled to a rotation speed range of 8-35 m / sec. Preparing a quench powder;   Grinding the quench powder and classifying it into a particle size range of 38-150 μm;   Adding a binder in the range of 0.5-3% by weight relative to the total Fe-Si alloy powder weight, and mixing the powder to form a pressure in the range of 1-10 ton / cm  2  ; And   
    Sintering the molded body in a temperature range of 1100-1350 ℃ for 0.5-4 hours; It relates to a method for manufacturing a Fe-Si-based sintered soft magnetic alloy comprising the technical gist.

title: Oxygen generation apparatus and method
abstract: An oxygen generator for an oxygen-generation apparatus has a proton-conducting membrane (60), a cathode (50) contacting a first side, or cathodic side, of the membrane, an anode (70) contacting a second side, or anodic side, of the membrane, and a source of water for supply to the membrane. In use, an electrolysis voltage applied between the cathode and the anode causes electrolysis of the water to generate oxygen gas at the anode. Atmospheric oxygen, i.e. oxygen in the air, is substantially prevented from coming into contact with the cathode. For an acidic proton-conducting membrane this substantially prevents the formation of hydrogen peroxide at the cathode.

title: LNG tank having re-liquefaction apparatus
abstract: According to an embodiment of the present invention, a liquefied gas tank having a re-liquefaction apparatus is provided. The liquefied gas tank having a re-liquefaction apparatus, according to an embodiment of the present invention, comprises: a tank which stores liquefied gas therein; a re-liquefaction tank which has one or more surfaces coming in contact with the tank and comes in contact with liquefied gas or evaporation gas stored in the tank; a first line which supplies the evaporation gas to the re-liquefaction tank; a second line which supplies the liquefied gas to the re-liquefaction tank; and a third line which moves the liquefied gas stored in the re-liquefaction tank to the tank by connecting the re-liquefaction tank to the tank.

title: A kind of button lithium ion battery
abstract: The invention discloses a kind of button lithium ion batteries, including negative cover, positive cover and round separator；The edge of negative cover and positive cover is sealed by mould-injection part, and positive cover and negative cover surround a closed cavity, the coating of the inner surface of positive cover and negative cover has the anti-corrosion of metal layer of high oxidation potential, multiple arcs protrusion is evenly equipped on the inner surface of negative cover, and the negative pole currect collecting net being bonded with arc convex is equipped at the inner surface center of negative cover, separator is set in positive cover, the edge of separator upwardly extends to form edge, multiple heat release holes are transversely provided on edge, the bottom of separator is equipped with the annular protrusion of the coaxial heart；It is equipped with diaphragm between negative cover and separator, negative electrode material is equipped between negative cover and diaphragm, positive electrode is equipped between diaphragm and separator.The present invention can be greatly improved the self-discharge performance of lithium ion battery and reduce the internal resistance of lithium ion battery, be a kind of button lithium ion battery with good electrochemical anti-corrosive performance.

title: Production of grain-oriented high silicon steel core
abstract:
PURPOSE: To provide a steel core with excellent magnetic properties and good workability by rolling a high silicon steel slab, executing primary recrystallization annealing, thereafter working it into a core, coating this core with a separation agent for annealing and executing finish annealing.  
     CONSTITUTION: A high silicon steel slab having a compsn. constituted of, by weight, 4.8 to 7.1% Si, 0.015 to 0.055% acid soluble Al, ≥0.0045% N and the balance Fe with inevitable impurities is heated, is hot-rolled, is next annealed and is warm-rolled. Next, this slab is subjected to primary recrystallization annealing in a wet hydrogen gas or the like and is worked into a core. This core is subjected to nitriding treatment or the like, is thereafter coated with a separation agent for annealing such as MgO or the like and is subjected to finish annealing. In this way, easy working into the shape of a core is easily executed, its purification is easily executed and the properties of core loss can be secured. 
     COPYRIGHT: (C)1992,JPO&Japio

title: Aqueous emulsion coating material and coating method
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide an aqueous emulsion coating material including a metallic zinc powder and an aqueous emulsion binder, using water as the main solvent, wherein aggregation of the metallic zinc powder and gelling of the coating material are suppressed by improving dispersion stability of the metallic zinc powder, and coating workability is improved by suppressing generation of hydrogen gas.SOLUTION: This aqueous emulsion coating material includes at least one aqueous emulsion binder selected from an aqueous solution of a water-soluble resin and an aqueous dispersion of a water-dispersible resin, a water-soluble polymer compound, a metallic zinc powder and an organic solvent.

title: The hydroxide aluminum manufacturing method which uses the aluminium drosses

In more detail, the raw material preparation step (S100) for pulverizing the aluminum dross to remove the oxide, and to mix evenly distributed metal aluminum (Al) contained in the crushed aluminum dross; A pretreatment step (S110) of removing impurities of iron (Fe) included in the raw material prepared by the raw material preparation step (S100) in a solid phase or a liquid phase; The raw material from which impurities are removed by the preliminary treatment step (S110) is added to a sealed container containing caustic soda solution (NaOH + H₂O), and the reaction heat according to hydrolysis and phase change or high temperature including heat from outside is added. A high temperature pressurized leaching step (S120) of leaching sodium aluminate (NaAlO₂) by raising the leaching temperature by the generated reaction gas pressure; A (carbonization) hydrogen trap (S130) for trapping hydrocarbon (CH) and hydrogen (H) generated in the high temperature pressurized leaching step (S120); An ammonia (Trap) step (S140) for collecting ammonia (NH₃) generated in the high temperature pressurized leaching step (S120); A primary solid-liquid separation step (S150) for filtering the leach generated by the high temperature pressurized leaching step (S120) to separate the sodium aluminate solution (NaAlO₂) and the residue; A residue washing step (S151) for washing the residue separated by the first solid-liquid separation step (S150) with washing water; A residue drying step (S152) of dehydrating and drying the residue washed by the residue washing step (S151); The seed (seed, Al (OH) ₃) was mixed with sodium aluminate solution (NaAlO₂) and sodium aluminate solution (NaAlO₂) in the residue separated by the first solid-liquid separation step (S150), and precipitated while cooling / stirring. Precipitation step (S160) and; A second solid-liquid separation step (S170) of filtering the precipitate precipitated by the precipitation step (S160) again to separate it into aluminum hydroxide (Al (OH) ₃) and the precipitation residue; A concentration adjusting step (S171) of adjusting the (OH) concentration of the precipitated residue in the aluminum hydroxide (Al (OH) ₃) and the precipitated residue separated by the second solid-liquid separation step (S170); A washing step (S180) of washing aluminum hydroxide (Al (OH) ₃) and aluminum hydroxide (Al (OH) ₃) in the precipitated residue separated by the second solid-liquid separation step (S170); The drying step (S190) to dry the aluminum hydroxide (Al (OH) ₃) washed by the washing step (S180) to complete the aluminum hydroxide; consisting of, or by grinding the aluminum dross to remove the oxide Raw material preparation step (S100) for mixing so that the metal aluminum (Al) contained in the crushed aluminum dross is evenly distributed; An aluminum hydrate mixing step (S200) of mixing aluminum hydrate containing aluminum hydroxide to the raw material prepared by the raw material preparation step (S100); A pretreatment step (S110) of removing impurities of iron (Fe) contained in a raw material in which aluminum hydrate containing aluminum hydroxide is mixed by the mixing step (S200); The raw material from which impurities are removed by the preliminary treatment step (S110) is added to a sealed container containing caustic soda solution (NaOH + H₂O), and the reaction heat according to hydrolysis and phase change or high temperature including heat from outside is added. A concentration adjusting step (S171) of adjusting the (OH) concentration of the precipitated residue in the aluminum hydroxide (Al (OH) ₃) and the precipitated residue separated by the second solid-liquid separation step (S170); A washing step (S180) of washing aluminum hydroxide (Al (OH) ₃) and aluminum hydroxide (Al (OH) ₃) in the precipitated residue separated by the second solid-liquid separation step (S170); The drying step (S190) for drying the aluminum hydroxide (Al (OH) ₃) washed by the washing step (S180) to complete the aluminum hydroxide; consisting of, but in the high-temperature pressurization leaching step (S120) Caustic soda solution ratio for   / l) is limited to the range of 0.01 ~ 0.1, the caustic soda solution ratio (a) to the metal aluminum in the high-temperature pressurization step (S120)   / l) in the state fixed in the range of 0.01 ~ 0.1 caustic soda concentration is adjusted to 100 ~ 250g / ℓ, caustic soda solution (NaOH + from the reaction heat generated when leaching in the high-temperature pressurization step (S120) H 2 O) leaching temperature is raised above the saturation temperature under atmospheric pressure, and the pressure of the reaction gas is controlled to 2 ~ 50kg / ㎠, heterogeneous elements Na, Mg, K, Ca, in the hot pressurized leaching step (S120) By mixing a compound containing any one of the heterogeneous elements of Si alternatively, to prepare a sodium aluminate solution (NaAlO₂) containing a heterogeneous element, and to collect the ammonia collected by the ammonia (Trap) step (S140) Converted into ammonia water, and supplied to adjust the pH of the residue and aluminum hydroxide (Al (OH) ₃) separated in the first solid-liquid separation step (S150) and the second solid-liquid separation step (S170), aluminum dross and caustic soda Aqueous solution (NaOH + H₂O) By reacting with each other, and using the reaction gas and heat generated during the reaction to leach the atmosphere in the container by applying a high temperature / pressurization to the metal atmosphere, such as aluminum nitride, aluminum carbide, aluminum oxide, as well as metal aluminum contained in aluminum dross. By changing the aluminum source into aluminum hydrate and precipitating it, the recovery rate of aluminum hydroxide is improved and it is economically useful for aluminum dross and aluminum dust with low metal aluminum content. Provided is a method for producing aluminum hydroxide using aluminum dross to produce aluminum hydroxide having improved quality by mixing with aluminum dross having a high aluminum metal content in the aluminum hydrate and leaching in a caustic soda solution at a high temperature / pressurized atmosphere.

title: Lithium-sulfur battery positive electrode active material with self-repairing function and preparation method thereof
abstract: The invention discloses a lithium-sulfur battery positive electrode active material with a self-repairing function and a preparation method thereof, and relates to a lithium-sulfur battery positive electrode material and a preparation method thereof, aiming at solving the problem that the active material of the positive electrode can be α -S in the charging and discharging processes of the conventional lithium-sulfur battery 8 And Li 2 The transition between S causes the technical problem of volume change thereof. The lithium-sulfur battery positive active material with the self-repairing function consists of sulfur and amino silicone oil (chitosan, polyethyleneimine) -terephthalaldehyde (glutaraldehyde and trimesic aldehyde). The invention leads the surface of sulfur to adsorb a layer of self-repairing network, relieves the positive active substance body in the charging and discharging process when being used as the positive active substance of the lithium-sulfur batteryThe product changes, avoids the positive active substance from entering the electrolyte, reduces the polysulfide shuttle effect, and is beneficial to improving the loading capacity and the utilization rate of the positive active substance.

title: A kind of gases at high pressure charging system
abstract: The invention discloses a kind of gases at high pressure charging system, including gasifier and charging device, emergency cut-off system is provided with after the gasifier, before charging device, the emergency cut-off system includes atmospheric valve, quick action emergency valve, stop valve control valve, and the atmospheric valve is located at after gasifier, and the quick action emergency valve is connected with stop valve control valve, and be controlled by it, the stop valve control valve is connected with electromagnetic relay, and the switch of electromagnetic relay is in safety cage, beneficial effects of the present invention：A kind of gases at high pressure charging system that the present invention is provided, when an emergency happens, in order to ensure personal safety, far as possible from the scene of the accident, operator by PLC system can remotely close stop valve in safety cage, and then prevent hazardous gas from going successively to fill workshop, especially carry out filling work under high pressure, unsafe factor is more more, and the system can go successively to fill workshop in very first time remote control cut-out gas.

title: High-capacity electrode active material for lithium secondary battery and lithium secondary battery using same
abstract: Disclosed are an electrode active material for a lithium secondary battery, an electrode comprising the electrode active material, and a lithium secondary battery comprising the electrode, the electrode active material comprising: a first material containing a carbon material; a second material comprising a nanostructure containing at least one kind selected from the group consisting of metals and metalloids capable of being reversibly alloyed with lithium formed on the first material; and a third material existing on the surface of at least one of the first and second materials and controlling a side reaction with an electrolyte.

abstract: <P>PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a battery capable of improving a cycle characteristic and a swelling characteristic.  <P>SOLUTION: This battery is provided with a positive electrode 21, a negative electrode 22 and an electrolyte, and the electrolyte is impregnated in a separator 23 arranged between the positive electrode 21 and the negative electrode 22. The negative electrode 22 includes a coating film 22C on a negative electrode active material layer 22B formed on a negative electrode collector 22A. The coating film 22C contains a metal salt, and the metal salt has a hydroxyl group, and at least either of a sulfonic acid group and a carboxylic acid group. Lithium ions are easily stored and released at the negative electrode 22, and decomposition of the electrolyte is suppressed.  <P>COPYRIGHT: (C)2009,JPO&INPIT

title: Heat recovery process and system in hydrogenated and reduced tail gas
abstract: The invention discloses a heat recovery process in hydrogenated and reduced tail gas. The heat recovery process comprises the following steps: a1, the tail gas at the temperature of 80-150 DEG C is cooled by a water cooler and heat exchange of circulating water, the temperature of the tail gas is reduced to 40-60 DEG C; a2, the tail gas at the temperature of 40-80 DEG C and analyzed hydrogen chloride liquid with the low temperature of minus 40 DEG C are subjected to heat-exchang by a vaporizer, and the hydrogen chloride liquid with the temperature of minus 40 DEG C is heated into hydrogen chloride gas with the temperature of 10-20 DEG C for synthesis use of trichlorosilane; and a3, the flow of the tail gas with the temperature of 40-60 DEG C entering into the vaporizer is adjusted by a bypass valve, and the vaporizing amount of the liquid hydrogen chloride with the temperature of minus 40 DEG C in the vaporizer and the outlet temperature of the hydrogen chloride of the vaporizer are controlled. The heat recovery process disclosed by the invention realizes comprehensive utilization of a great amount of heat energy carried by hydrogenated tail gas or reduced tail gas and a great amount of cold energy carried by the hydrogen chloride liquid with the temperature of minus 40 DEG C. The invention also discloses a heat recovery system in the hydrogenated and reduced tail gas.

title: Manufacture of semiconducting carbon thin film
abstract:
PURPOSE: To facilitate the concentration control of a dopant in the title film by a method wherein a mixed gas containing hydrocarbon, hydrogen gas and nitrogen gas, but not the poisonous phosphine, is used as a sputtering gas.  
     CONSTITUTION: A compound target of gallium GaP is set up as a target electrode 9. After respectively setting up deposited substrates 17, 18, 21, a vacuum vessel 1 is pressure-reduced down to 1.33×10 -5 Pa(10 -7  torr). Then, a mixed gas comprising 90 vol.% of gas mixed with 90 vol.% of hydrogen gas H 2  and 10 vol.% of nitrogen gas N 2  as well as 10 vol.% of methane gas CH is led in from a leading-in pipe 6 up to the pressure of 67 Pa (0.5 torr). Next, after the pressure in the vessel 1 is stabilized, the target 9 is set up at high-frequency (11.56 MHz) and power (6.8 W/cm 2 ) to be sputtered for three hours. Through these procedures, the poisonous phosphine PH 3  need not be used for sputtering gas so that the concentration control of dopant in the title film may be facilitated. 
     COPYRIGHT: (C)1988,JPO&Japio

title: Preparation of styrene
abstract:
PURPOSE: To obtain styrene from an inexpensive raw material, by oxidizing sec- butylbenzene with molecular oxygen, hydrogenating the resultant acetophenone, and dehydrating the α-methylbenzyl alcohol thus obtained.  
     CONSTITUTION: sec-Butylbenzene prepared readily from n-butene and benzene is oxidized in the liquid phase with molecular oxygen in the presence of a catalyst, e.g. a heavy metallic compound like Co, Mn, Ni or Cr, at 80W150°C and converted into acetopheno, which is brought into contact with hydrogen gas preferably in the liquid phase in the presence of a catalyst, e.g. Pd, Ni or copper chromite on a carrier, to form α-methylbenzyl alcohol. The resultant alcohol is then dehydrated to give styrene.  
     EFFECT: The n-butene is inexpensive as a raw material, and said method is also an effective one to utilize the n-butene. 
     COPYRIGHT: (C)1981,JPO&Japio

title: Alkaline zing storage battery
abstract: PURPOSE:To improve the cycle life of a battery as well as to control the size magnification of zinc active substance particles and to prevent the capacity deterioration of a zinc electrode, by applying zinc particles, on whose surfaces zinc oxide layers of 10 to 50wt% are formed, as a negative electrode active substance. CONSTITUTION:As a negative electrode active substance, are used zinc particles on whose surfaces zinc oxide layers of 10 to 50 Wt% are formed. When the zinc oxide layer on the surface is less than 10 wt%, the performance of the zinc particles is similar to that of the metallic zinc, dendrites are liable to be generated, and magnified particles are also liable to be generated. Furthermore, when the battery is overcharged, the zinc oxide layer on the surface is charged, leaving little room of chargable reserve, and sometimes generates hydrogen gas improperly. On the other hand, when the zinc oxide layer is more than 50wt%, the form of the zinc particles containing the zinc oxide layers is changed from the ball form of the metallic zinc to the column form of the crystalline zinc oxide, and the performance of the particles is considered to be similar to that of the zinc oxide. Such particles containing a higher ratio of zinc oxide disturb the permeation of the electrolyte into the zinc particles, making the conductivity lower, resulting in a lower charge efficiency and deteriorated cycle life property.

title: The preparation method of sulfide-graphene composite material photoelectric
abstract: The invention discloses a kind of preparation methods of sulfide graphene composite material photoelectric, first disperse graphene oxide powder in water, obtain graphene oxide dispersion；It is added soluble metallic salt into dispersion liquid again, after standing, washing precipitate, drying, grinding obtain the graphene oxide powder of metal ion mixing；It is dispersed again in deionized water again, after stirring, sulfide precursor salt solution is added, thiocarbamide is added and simultaneously stirs, then reaction product is obtained by one step hydro thermal method, after cooled to room temperature, by reaction product centrifuge washing, is dried to obtain solid powder.Synthesizing sulfide of the present invention/graphene complex preparation method is simple, the seed layer that metal ion is grown as the interface bridging agent and sulfide of sulfide and graphene surface, it is evenly dispersed to promote sulfide lamella, inhibit the stacking of graphene sheet layer, big specific surface area can provide more active site, then effectively improve the catalytic performance of composite material.

title: Anode active material hybridizing carbon nano fibers for lithium secondary battery
abstract: The present invention is to provide anode active material hybridized with carbon nano fibers for lithium secondary battery prepared by following steps comprising, i) dispersing the nano size metal catalyst to the surface of anode material selected from graphite, amorphous silicon or the complex of graphite and amorphous silicon; and ii) growing the carbon nano fiber by chemical vapor deposition method, wherein carbon nano fibers are grown in a vine form and surround the surface of anode active material.

title: Apparatus of Reactive Cathodic Arc Evaporator for Plating Lithium-Compound Thin Film and Method Thereof
abstract: An apparatus is provided for plating a lithium (Li)-compound thin film. In the thin film, Li is obtained through thermal evaporation, and titanium (Ti) or other metal by using arc plasma. The elements converted into gas phase are co-deposited in a plasma environment with a reaction gas (oxygen) to be activated as excited atoms or molecules for reaction. In the end, all of the constituent elements are deposited on a substrate to form the Li-compound thin film. Thus, reaction efficiency is high with a fast deposition rate. The composition ratio of each element is independently determined to control its yield according to the requirement. Hence, the present invention greatly enhances the fabrication rate with lowered production cost for applications in the thin-film battery industries.

title: Reducing physiological saline solutions and process for the production thereof
abstract: Reducing physiological saline solutions having oxidation-reduction potentials of -10mV to -2000mV, which can be produced by dissolving hydrogen gas at -180˚C to 90˚C and a pressure of 0.1 to 800 atm in a physiological saline solution and then bringing the resulting solution into ordinary temperature and ordinary pressure. It is preferable that the reducing physiological saline solutions have pH values of 4.5 to 8.0. The obtained physiological saline solutions have extremely low oxidation-reduction potentials and therefore act as physiological saline solutions having potent reducing effect, even when the pH values are within a weakly acid region. Thus, they are expected to exert not only effects inherent in ordinary physiological saline solutions but also physiological effects due to the reducing effect.

title: Preparation of formaldehyde
abstract:
PURPOSE: To prepare low-methanol content, high concentration formaldehyde from methanol using a metallic catalyst, safely, saving the resources and energy, by recycling the burnt exhaust gas as an inert gas.  
     CONSTITUTION: In the preparation of formaldehyde from a mixed gas consisting of methanol vapor and air, and optionally steam, using a metallic catalyst, the exhaust gas from the reaction system is burnt, and cooled to a proper temperature to recover the heat. A part of the treated gas is recycled to the formaldehyde producing process. The recycled gas is pref. 0.75W1.50 parts by volume based on 1 part of the methanol vapor.  
     EFFECT: The partial pressure of hydrogen gas in the gas fed to the reactor can be reduced, which contributes to the safety of the reaction. The combustion heat can be effectively utilized.

title: Process for preparing carbon black by converting acetylene by plasma
abstract: A process for preparing carbon black from acetylene by plasma technique is characterized by that DC pulses are applied to the one of two unsymmetrical electrodes in reactor to generate corona discharge between said two electrodes, resulting in generation of plasma to convert acetylene to carbon black while generating pure hydrogen gas.

title: Manufacture of semiconductor element
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a method of manufacturing a semiconductor element, which can make a recovery from the damages to the crystal of a polycrystalline semiconductor layer, which is generated by a collision of ions in an impurity doping process, and also can make compensation for uncombined species. SOLUTION: A polycrystalline semiconductor layer 10 is heated, immediately after ions are implanted in the layer 10 or an energy beam or an infrared lamp is radiated on the layer 10, whereby the recovery from the damages to the crystallizability of the layer 10 are made and moreover, by continuously performing heating of the temperature lower than 350 deg.C to the layer 10 in a hydrogen gas atmosphere, the recovery from the damages to the crystallizability of the layer 10 and a compensation of uncombied species can be made.

title: Power generation systems and methods
abstract: A power generation system utilizing an electrochemical cell comprising a consumable metal anode, a cathode spaced from the anode to define an electrochemical reaction zone therebetween and an electrolyte comprising an aqueous solution of the hydroxide of the consumable metal is disclosed. The electrolyte is circulated through the reaction zone to electrochemically generate electrical power and to form the hydroxide of the consumable metal. The hydroxide of the consumable metal is converted to water and at least one oxide of the consumable metal. The water is separated from the consumable metal oxide and is added to the electrolyte. The invention also comprehends corresponding methods of power generation.

title: Microwave Desorption ? Catalistic Cleaning Desorber of the Volertile Organic Compound
abstract:
The present invention is an apparatus applied to a facility for desorption and regeneration of the adsorbent of the adsorption tower. 
     Adsorption towers (Fig. 1), which have been mainly used in the past, have been widely used as VOC removal facilities because of their simple structure and low equipment cost. However, the adsorption adsorption characteristics require excessive replacement cost of adsorbents. Installation has solved the problem. 
     The desorption method that has been generally used has been the method of desorption and condensation by applying steam heat (Fig. 2 method), but the desorption device itself is expensive and the equipment cost is high due to the use of corrosion resistant materials by using steam. Or a separate utility such as cooling water had a problem. 
     In recent years, by applying a catalytic combustion system, the air heated by a desorption hot fan, a catalytic combustion device, and a heater mounted on the catalytic combustion device makes hot air and desorbs, and the VOC generated during desorption is injected into the catalytic combustion device again. After the catalytic combustion decomposition was discharged to the atmosphere (FIG. 3 method) was used. 
     However, this method has a fatal problem that takes a long time because the desorption time takes a long time and a large amount of adsorbent is desorbed by hot air for a long time. 
     In addition, this method has a problem in that separate design such as two adsorption towers are required in the continuous operation process because it detaches during operation. 
     Thus, the present invention, as shown in Figure 5 method by removing the saturated adsorbent by a certain amount and transported to a microwave type desorption apparatus (8), which is a separate vessel and irradiated with microwaves to the adsorbent introduced into the microwave generator 801 from here. Desorbs the moving adsorbent in a short time, and the VOC desorbed from the adsorbent is exhausted by the desorption hot fan (6), decomposed by the catalytic combustion device (7), purified, and discharged into the atmosphere. This problem was solved by desorption in a short time by microwave.

title: A kind of preparation method and device of high performance silicon carbon based negative electrodes material
abstract: The invention belongs to lithium ion battery negative material fields, specifically disclose the preparation method and device of a kind of high performance silicon carbon based negative electrodes material, and silica-base material and carbon-based material are uniformly mixed and obtain presoma；So that presoma is in fluidisation state and carry out chemical vapor deposition, obtains Si carbon-base negative electrode material.The present invention carries out carbon coating to silicon-carbon sill using vibratory liquefaction gas-phase deposition system and constructs, material granule is set to reach fluidized state first, it is suspended in gas phase, then 360 ° of uniformly cladding carbon-coatings are carried out on material granule surface by chemical vapour deposition technique again, reach the carbon-coating even compact of cladding, the good purpose of consistency.The Si carbon-base negative electrode material reversible specific capacity with higher and higher coulombic efficiency for the first time, show excellent conductance property and good stable circulation performance.

title: Fluidic distribution system and related methods
abstract: Embodiments of the present invention relate to a fluid distribution system. The system may include one or more electrochemical cell layers, a bulk distribution manifold having an inlet, a cell layer feeding manifold in direct fluidic contact with the electrochemical cell layer and a separation layer that separates the bulk distribution manifold from the cell feeding manifold, providing at least two independent paths for fluid to flow from the bulk distribution manifold to the cell feeding manifold.

title: Process for protecting copper-zinc alloy by sol-gel process
abstract: The invention relates to a process for obtaining an alloy part comprising copper and zinc coated with a layer of inorganic or organo-mineral polymer or copolymer with a layer thickness of between 20 and 30 nm in order to give to the piece of aesthetic properties similar to gold and qualities of resistance to shocks, chemical and ultraviolet aggressions, characterized in that: - A foundry alloy is produced whose copper content is between 84.5% and 85.5%. , 5%, the zinc content is between 14.5% and 15.5%, the lead content is less than 0.005%; - A bar is prepared by work hardening and / or by rolling and / or shaving and / or by surface drawing and / or annealing in a hydrogen gas atmosphere; - We perform a first tribofinition, type of wear; - We perform a second tribofinition, type wear; - We carry out a third tribofinition, of type glossinage; - It is degreased with an organic solvent of the acetone type; - We degrease with a laundry; - Rinsed with water, preferably demineralized; - It is stripped with a hydrochloric acid solution; - Rinsed with water, preferably demineralized; Said part is immersed in a solution comprising SiOR at a speed of between 200 and 400 mm / min, where R is a radical; Said part is removed from said solution and said part is heated to a temperature of between 80 and 100 ° C. for a period of 5 to 15 minutes.

title: Composite aluminum-air battery system
abstract: The invention provides a composite aluminum-air battery system. The composite aluminum-air battery system comprises an aluminum-air battery stack, a hydrogen fuel battery stack, a supercapacitor group, a lithium battery pack, an electrolyte controller and a running controller, wherein the aluminum-air battery stack is used for running power generation and generating hydrogen, the hydrogen fuel battery stack is used for collecting and utilizing the generated hydrogen to generate power, the supercapacitor group is used for power conversion, the lithium battery pack is used for energy storage, the electrolyte controller is connected with the aluminum-air battery stack and used for controlling the running of the aluminum-air battery stack, and the running controller is used for adjusting the generated power and carrying out output control. The composite aluminum-air battery system provided by the invention has the characteristics of high energy density, large output power, working safety and reliability, high environmental protection and high performance ratio, a complicated battery management system is also not needed, and the system is simple and reliable in running.

title: Method for preparing nano grade tungsten powder
abstract: The invention relates to a method for producing nanometer tungsten powder, wherein it comprises putting the non-crystal forward powder prepared from atomization thermal exchange method into the exhaust reducing furnace, at 550-700Deg. C, for 30-60min, and the hydrogen gas or decompose ammonia section flux is 30-60ml/cm2 .min; obtaining the nanometer tungsten powder whose SAXS average diameter is 35-80nm. The invention can reduce the reaction temperature and improve produce efficiency.

title: Hydrogen gas sensor
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a hydrogen gas sensor, utilizing a characteristic of a Pd-Ag alloy layer storing hydrogen to expand. SOLUTION: In this hydrogen gas sensor, a Pd or Pt thin film is formed on the Pd-Ag alloy layer deposited on a substrate, and the expansion of the Pd-Ag alloy layer caused by the hydrogen storage is detected as strain of the substrate. After SO2 treatment of the Pd-Ag alloy layer, the Pd or Pt thin film can be laminated.

title: Hybrid power supply control system and method
abstract: This application relates to a control system and method for use in a hybrid power supply apparatus comprising a current generating device, such as a fuel cell, and an energy storage device, such as a battery. The invention ensures that the fuel cell operates in a quasi steady state mode irrespective of fluctuations in load power demands. By restricting the operation of the fuel cell to discrete steady modes with minimal state changes, the useful service life of the fuel cell is prolonged. The control scheme ensures that the current generating device will run in a near steady mode even in the presence of a dynamic load.

title: Additive for increasing lifespan of rechargeable zinc-anode batteries
abstract: A rechargeable battery can include a cathode, an anode current collector, an anode comprising zinc, and an electrolyte in ionic communication with both the cathode and the anode current collector. The electrolyte can include an organic ammonium halide. The organic ammonium halide can include an ammonium bromide in some instances.

title: Purifying and extruding attapulgite ceramisite filter material
abstract: The invention relates to ceramic filtration materials, in particular to a purifying and extruding attapulgite ceramisite filter material. A method for preparing the extruding ceramisite filter material by performing wet purification on attapulgite clay lies in that after purification, attapulgite clay is stirred, uniformly blended, kneaded, extruded for shaping, and is further dried and roasted to obtain the finished product. The purifying and extruding attapulgite ceramisite filter material provided by the invention can efficiently adsorb and purify pollutants in the water, and can also be used for efficiently removing harmful gases such as NOx, VOCs, NH3, H2S and SO2, and the advantages of high mechanical strength, small air resistance, high air porosity, large specific surface area, high adsorption capacity, good stability, low cost, reusability and reproducibility can be achieved.

abstract: In combination with the hydrogen diffusion preventing film (23, 27, 28), a hydrogen storage conductive material having a property of absorbing hydrogen in order to surely prevent the generated hydrogen from entering the ferroelectric film (25). With this configuration, it is possible to reliably prevent water and hydrogen from entering the inside with a relatively simple configuration, and to maintain the high performance of the ferroelectric capacitor structure (30). title: A kind of bag cage special steel material and preparation method thereof
abstract: The present invention is directed to Environmental-protecting dust-removing bag cage steel demand, provides a kind of special steel material for making Environmental-protecting dust-removing bag cage and the production technology for making guarantor's dedusting bag cage.The composition proportion of the special steel material is as follows: by percentage to the quality, steel scrap 90.5-95.5%, pig iron 1.35-2.65%, nickel 0.82-1.85%, chromium 1.43-2.85%, molybdenum 0.25-0.52%, manganese 0.58-1.55% and silicon 0.07-0.21%.High performance dedusting bag cage can be obtained by this special steel material.

title: Photocatalyst, method for preparation, photolysis system
abstract: The present invention relates to a photocatalyst for the generation of diatomic hydrogen from a hydrogen containing precursor under the influence of actinic radiation comprising a semiconductor support with one or more noble and/or transition metal(s) deposited on said semiconductor support, wherein said metal is covered at least in part with a layer of the semiconductor support. Further disclosed is a method for preparing such catalyst and a method for generating diatomic hydrogen by photolysis.

title: Sealed and thermally insulating wall for a tank for storing fluid
abstract: The invention relates to a sealed and thermally insulating wall for a tank for storing fluid, having: • an insulating panel (1) having an inner face; and • a sealing plate (7) having an inner face, intended to be in contact with the fluid contained in the tank, and an outer face anchored to the inner face of the panel (1) at a plurality of anchoring zones (14), said sealing plate (7) having at least one corrugation (8; 9), protruding from the side of the inner face of the sealing plate (7), extending in a direction d 1  (x; y); wherein: • the inner face of the insulating panel (1) has, between two adjacent anchoring zones (14) that are disposed on either side of said corrugation (8; 9), a relaxation slot (15; 16) having an axis extending in the direction d 1  (x, y) so as to allow a deformation of the corrugation transversely to the direction d 1  (x, y); and • the relaxation slot (15; 16) has a length less than the dimension of the insulating panel (1) along the axis of the relaxation slot (15; 16).

title: A kind of prelithiation method for lithium an- ode protection
abstract: The invention belongs to field of lithium, disclose a kind of prelithiation method for lithium an- ode protection, which comprises the following steps: (1) coat to form organic polymer thin film in collection liquid surface；(2) collector coated with organic polymer thin film is assembled into battery together with lithium piece, diaphragm, electrolyte and carries out discharge treatment, and lithium metal is deposited on collection liquid surface；(3) collector that surface is deposited with lithium metal is taken out to get negative electrode material that is having protective film to surface and can be used in lithium metal battery.The lithium anode prepared using prelithiation method of the invention; on the one hand it is able to suppress the generation of Li dendrite; on the other hand the protective film being formed in situ can prevent lithium metal and electrolyte to react again and generate dead lithium, and the Li dendrite phenomenon and lithium anode that can effectively solve nonuniform deposition generation of the common metal cathode of lithium in cyclic process react the phenomenon that dead lithium generated is to reduce cycle performance with electrolyte.

title: Method of mounting a fuel pellet in a laser-excited fusion reactor
abstract: 1386988 Thermonuclear fusion apparatus UNITED STATES ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION 4 Dec 1972 [23 Dec 1971] 55918/72 Heading G6P In thermonuclear fusion apparatus, a spherical target 67 (Fig. 2) is mounted, by means of a support 93, in a holder 81 forming a generally elliptical reflector and laser radiation 13 enters a chamber (Fig. 1), in which the target is positioned, via an aperture which at least partially protects the laser 11 from the reaction products produced in the chamber. In the embodiment shown, the aperture 59 is provided in a rotatable shutter 49 positioned within a neutron shield 51 so that the aperture 59 aligns with a passage 38 through the shield. Pulses of laser radiation are thus passed into the chamber to interact with the target in a "oneshot" operation. The target 67 may comprise a 1 : 1 D-T mixture or liquid hydrogen, deuterium or tritium, or lithium hydride. The target 67 and its mounting can be fabricated in a fabricator and supplied directly to the chamber. The holder 81 and support 93 may be of lithium, the target 67 being disposed at the focus of the reflector. A bottom cut-away portion 92<SP>1</SP> of the holder 81 enables some radiation to be reflected on to the support 93 to cause the latter to be vaporized. The reflector may have a copper or gold reflective coating.  The capsule 15 constituted by the target 67, support 93 and holder 81 may be held in a jig in the chamber or may be dropped by free fall into a liquid lithium vortex therein. Heat can be extracted from the chamber by a circulating fluid and used to produce power by means of a heat-engine, MHD converter, thermoelectric, or thermionic converter. Some of the electrical energy produced can be used to energize the laser.

title: Regenerating method of iron @(3754/24)iii) chloride solution
abstract: (57) [Abstract] [Purpose] To recycle waste liquid of ferric chloride containing metal ions without waste. [Structure] A ferric chloride waste liquid containing nickel ions is reduced by hydrogen gas using, for example, a reduction catalyst in which a platinum group metal is attached to a water-repellent substance. Then, the reduction treatment liquid is subjected to diffusion dialysis to diffuse hydrochloric acid to the solvent side to remove the hydrochloric acid generated in the reduction step. After that, iron scrap is introduced into the treatment liquid to deposit and desorb nickel, and ferrous chloride produced thereby and ferrous chloride obtained in the reduction step are chlorinated to form ferric chloride. In the reduction process, ferrous chloride is 1: 1: ferric chloride.   Since it is produced at a ratio of 1, the regeneration ratio of ferric chloride can be finally suppressed to a low level, and there is no waste.

title: Production of alpha, omega-diols
abstract: Disclosed herein are processes for preparing an α,ω-C n -diol, wherein n is 5 or greater, from a feedstock comprising a C?n#191 oxygenate. In one embodiment, the process comprises contacting the feedstock with hydrogen gas in the presence of a catalyst comprising Pt, Cu, Ni, Pd, Pt, Rh, Ir, Ru, or Fe on a WO 3  or WO x  support. In one embodiment, the process comprises contacting the feedstock with hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst comprising a metal M1 and a metal M2 or an oxide of M2, and optionally a support. In one embodiment, M1 is Pd, Pt, or Ir; and M2 is Mo, W, V, Mn, Re, Zr, Ni, Cu, Zn, Cr, Ge, Sn, Ti, Au, or Co. The C n  oxygenate may be obtained from a biorenewable resource.

title: Continuous heating apparatus
abstract:
PURPOSE: To reduce the gas feeding amount and the space for installation by slanting the both end part of a furnace core downward at an acute angle and allowing the gas lighter than air to stagnate at the center part of the upper furnace core and preventing the leakage at both end openings.  
     CONSTITUTION: An object to be heated which has been worked on a working table 38 is put on a suspending tool and carried in a furnace core 18. Nitrogen gas is supplied from each of the pipes 40a and 41a and stagnates in the vicinity of said both end openings to form a curtain between hydrogen gas and the air. The object to be heated is heated in hydrogen gas environment in the furnace core 18, and oxidation is prevented. The heated material is cooled to the temperature at which oxidation does not occurr even in the contact with air at a cooling part 28, then taken down a suspending tool 36X in the lower frame 24 of the furnace core 18, put on a suspending tool 36Y, and after the carrying in the faurnace core 18 from the inlet opening 20 of the furnace core, on the suspending tool 36Y, said cooled material is heated again.

title: Carbon dioxide refining method
abstract: The invention relates to a method for producing carbon dioxide, which can restrain the steam generated by abundant oxygen and produce carbon dioxide effectively, when processing the oxidization reaction of carbon monoxide and nitrogen oxide in reaction tower. Wherein, said method comprises: guiding the mixed gas whose main component is carbon dioxide into reaction tower 16, as well as removing the nitrogen oxide of mixed gas via the reduction reaction with hydrogen gas, and removing the carbon monoxide gas via the oxidization reaction with oxygen gas; and the reaction condition is set as the outlet gas of reaction tower guided outside the reaction tower has non-reacted oxygen gas.

title: Process method for treating octanol waste gas pollution
abstract: A process method for treating octanol waste gas pollution belongs to the technical field of treatment methods of organic pollutants in waste gas. The method is characterized in that: the treatment process comprises the following steps: soaking carbon fiber in the activating solution by taking a compound of aryl phosphine or a derivative thereof and phosphoric acid or phosphate as an activating agent, heating and activating in inert gas, and cooling to obtain modified activated carbon fiber; loading activated carbon fiber into an adsorber of an adsorption device; the gas containing octanol firstly enters an absorber for adsorption, and the absorber which finishes adsorption is desorbed; and condensing the organic mixed steam generated by desorption in a condenser, and then carrying out liquid-liquid separation in a separator, wherein the upper layer liquid is organic octanol, the lower layer liquid is water, and octanol is recovered in an overflowing manner. The method has high desorption efficiency and long service life of the activated carbon fiber, and fundamentally solves the problem of low octanol absorption and desorption efficiency.

title: Particulate heterogeneous solid CO2 absorbent composition, process feed for its preparation and process the feed for a separating CO2 from process gases using mixtures
abstract: Particulate heterogeneous solid CO2 absorbent mixture comprising decomposition of Ca3A12O6 after being heated to a temperature between 500 ° C and 925 ° C in the presence of H2O and CO2 for a time sufficient to allow Ca3A12O6 to react and form the particulate heterogeneous absorbent composition exhibits a higher concentration of aluminum than calcium inside the particles, but a higher concentration, but a higher concentration of calcium than aluminum at the particle surface.  The invention also comprises a method for preparing the absorbing composition comprising the steps of a) providing a feedstock comprising Ca3A12O6, and b) heating the feedstock to a temperature between 500 ° C and 925 ° C in the presence of H2O and CO2, for a period sufficient to allowing the raw material to react and form the particulate heterogeneous, solid CO2 absorbent composition, and a method of using the mixture for separating CO2 from a process gas.
