abstract: The invention discloses a zinc-based battery, which adopts sponge Zn as a negative electrode, a porous pp film and non-woven fabric as a diaphragm, NiOOH as a positive electrode, Zn (OH) 2 The mixed solution of KOH/LiOH is used as electrolyte, the cycle performance is good, and no dendritic crystal is generated.

title: Hard alloy roller ring
abstract: The invention discloses a hard alloy roller ring and relates to the technical field of hard alloy. The hard alloy roller ring comprises a roller ring base and a hard alloy ring. The roller ring base is made from, by weight, 60-70 parts of iron powder, 15-26 parts of silicon powder, 20-30 parts of carbon powder, 6-10 parts of copper powder, 11-17 parts of chromium powder, 9-18 parts of nickel powder, 3-7 parts of molybdenum powder, 6-15 parts of a deslagging agent and 2-6 parts of a nodulizing agent. The hard alloy ring is made from, by weight, 50-65 parts of tungsten carbide powder, 30-42 parts of cobalt powder, 17-28 parts of nickel powder, 25-38 parts of titanium carbide powder and 11-20 parts of chromium powder. The roller ring base and the hard alloy ring are connected in a welded manner. Compared with the prior art, the bonding strength of the roller ring base and the hard alloy ring is high.

title: Brown gas auto supply apparatus using an air cooling electrolyzer
abstract: An apparatus for automatically supplying Brown gas is provided to make it possible to cool an electrolyte by an air cooling process instead of a freezing process by forming a gas generator body integrally having an electrolysis chamber in which a large capacity ventilator and projection type electrolyzers are installed and a water supply equipment chamber in which an auxiliary water supply equipment is installed. An apparatus for automatically supplying Brown gas using air cooling type electrolyzers comprises: an electrolysis equipment(101) including an electrolysis chamber(A) having predetermined numbers of air inflow ports formed on the bottom thereof and a ventilator(150) installed on an upper surface thereof, air cooling type electrolyzers(120) installed above the air inflow ports, electrolyte tanks(130) connected to the electrolyzers by electrolyte inflow hoses and gas exhaust hoses, and a power supply(140) for supplying a DC power to the electrolyzers to generate electrolysis; and an automatic auxiliary water supply equipment installed in a water supply equipment chamber(B), the automatic auxiliary water supply equipment including a water purifier(160) connected to a water pipe to automatically supply auxiliary water into the electrolyte tanks, a water tank(170) for storing purified water, and a compressing pump(181) and a compressing tank(182) for pressurizing water of the water tank to supply the pressurized water into the electrolyte tanks.

title: Fuel reforming method and system
abstract: A fuel reforming method includes the step of supplying carbon-containing fuel and steam to a reactor filled with a fuel reforming catalyst and a CO 2  absorbent and discharging CO 2 , and setting the absorbent at an absorption temperature, thereby converting the carbon-containing fuel into reformed fuel, and separating CO 2  from the reformed fuel, the step of obtaining a product gas by oxidizing a portion of the reformed fuel and/or the carbon-containing fuel with an oxidizer, and heating the absorbent with this product gas to a regeneration temperature, thereby regenerating the absorbent and storing heat in this absorbent, and the step of supplying the carbon-containing fuel and steam to the reactor, thereby cooling, to the absorption temperature, the absorbent heated to the regeneration temperature, and converting the carbon-containing fuel into reformed fuel by heat energy stored in the CO 2  absorbent. An apparatus for the method is also disclosed.

title: Improvements in and relating to the concentration and purification of hydrogen peroxide by distillation
abstract: <PICT:0590439/III/1> Hydrogen peroxide of concentration up to 98 per cent. is obtained by distilling aqueous hydrogen peroxide solutions under a pressure not exceeding 6 inches of mercury absolute in a climbing film evaporator 2, the solution being introduced at the base of the evaporator at a pH between 3 and 5, and the vapour produced passed into a separator 5, from which hydrogen peroxide vapour is withdrawn as the overhead product and liquid hydrogen peroxide and impurities as the bottom products; the latter are passed through the reservoir 9 back to the evaporator.  The vapour is then passed through a fractionating column 7 where it is subjected to fractional distillation under reduced pressure with the top of the column cooled, and concentrated liquid hydrogen peroxide having a concentration of hydrogen peroxide of at least 75 per cent. weight/weight is removed as the bottom product.  Further concentration may be effected in a second evaporator, 16 and separator 19, the vapour passing overhead back to the bottom of the fractionating column and further concentrated liquid hydrogen peroxide collecting in a receiver 21.  Alternatively, a second fractionating column may be used from which the distillate passes to the first column and liquid residue to the receiver 21.  When making hydrogen peroxide solutions of concentration greater than 90 per cent. the absolute pressure is below 2 inches of mercury. Specifications 445,334 and 546,530 are referred to.

title: Enclosed alkaline zinc storage battery
abstract: PURPOSE:To suppress a decrease in an electrolytic solution by building a hydrogen absorbing alloy and a vapor phase reaction catalyst pole for a contact reaction of an oxygen gas and a hydrogen gas in a similar case. CONSTITUTION:When a hydrogen absorbing alloy 5, that is, LaNi5H6 is closely built in a battery case, a hydrogen gas is discharged from the hydrogen alloy and so the battery is filled with the hydrogen gas. Therefore, from the beginning of using the battery, this hydrogen gas reacts to an oxygen gas, which is generated at a cathode by overcharge, on a gas phase reaction catalyst pole 4, and immediate reflux of water is obtained. When the hydrogen gas in the battery is consumed, an internal pressure's drop due to this consumption causes the hydrogen gas to be discharged. Because the oxygen gas generated at the cathode by the overcharge can be effectively consumed and refluxed into water in this way, a decrease in an electrolytic solution can be suppressed.

title: Lithium ion battery stratiform silicon/carbon composite and preparation method thereof
abstract: The present invention discloses a kind of preparation method of lithium ion battery stratiform silicon/carbon composite, using nano silicon particles and carbon material as raw material, using ultrasonic dispersion and vacuum filtration method, it is characterized in that utilizing ultrasonic dispersion, control the ratio of nano silicon particles and carbon material, nano silicon particles and carbon material are evenly spread in solvent, silicon/carbon dispersion liquid is obtained；Above-mentioned silicon/carbon dispersion liquid is handled by vacuum filtration system suction filtration again, obtains silicon/carbon-coating shape material precursor；Then it is carbonized, priming reaction, is obtained with stratiform silicon/carbon composite.Stratiform silicon/the carbon composite manufactured using the formula and technique; take full advantage of the protective effect of stratiform carbon material; the SEI layer transfer for being grown in high expansion silicon materials surface is formed into the stratiform carbon material surface to opposite low bulk; destruction of the volume expansion in silicon materials charge and discharge process to SEI layers is reduced, to improve the stability of silicium cathode and good charge-discharge velocity, capacity retention ratio and cycle life in lithium ion battery.

title: Preparation process of negative electrode material for lithium battery
abstract: The invention belongs to the technical field of power batteries, and particularly discloses a preparation process of a negative electrode material for a lithium battery, which comprises the following steps: 1) mixing nano silicon, magnesium powder and antimony powder, and presintering in inert atmosphere to obtain a precursor 1; 2) dispersing terephthalic acid diboronic acid in a solvent, adding methanesulfonic acid, stirring uniformly, then placing in a vacuum environment for reaction, washing with water, and spray-drying to obtain a precursor 2; 3) and mixing the precursor 1 and the precursor 2 in a solvent, adding a carbon source for hydrothermal reaction, drying, and calcining at high temperature to obtain the battery cathode material. When the battery cathode material prepared by the method disclosed by the invention is practically applied, the specific capacity and the cycle performance of the battery can be effectively improved.

title: Method preparing superfine metallic nickel powder with high frequency plasma
abstract: The invention relates to a method preparing spherical metallic nickel powder with high frequency plasma. The method takes the hydrate of metallic nickel or subcarbonate as raw material and carries out hydrogen reduction through the high frequency plasma technology to prepare the superfine spherical nickel powder. Plasma arc is added to the raw material. The plasma is heated in high temperature through high frequency induction and generates reduction reaction with high-activity hydrogen plasma. The superfine spherical nickel powder can be obtained through a shape controller in the condensation process. The utility model is characterized in that the nickel hydroxide or the subcarbonate are used as the raw material, in the reduction process of the plasma, the particle size and the shape of the nickel powder can be controlled easily, the process flow is short and the production process is environment-friendly.
text: Method preparing superfine metallic nickel powder with high frequency plasma The invention relates to a method preparing spherical metallic nickel powder with high frequency plasma. The method takes the hydrate of metallic nickel or subcarbonate as raw material and carries out hydrogen reduction through the high frequency plasma technology to prepare the superfine spherical nickel powder. Plasma arc is added to the raw material. The plasma is heated in high temperature through high frequency induction and generates reduction reaction with high-activity hydrogen plasma. The superfine spherical nickel powder can be obtained through a shape controller in the condensation process. The utility model is characterized in that the nickel hydroxide or the subcarbonate are used as the raw material, in the reduction process of the plasma, the particle size and the shape of the nickel powder can be controlled easily, the process flow is short and the production process is environment-friendly.

title: Dry cell
abstract: PURPOSE:To prevent the pH decrease of electrolytic solution, by adding at least one of the substances of zinc oxide and zinc hydroxide into the electrolyte. CONSTITUTION:Zinc oxide or zinc hydroxide is added to electrolytic solution. A flat plate-shaped dry cell is utilized for a dry cell, and it comprises the positive electrode mixture 1 of manganese dioxide and acetylene black, the negative electrode mixture 6 of unhardened zinc powder, the separator 5 of nonwoven fabric, a resin sealing body 4, and current collecting bodies 2, 7 which are made by laminating a hard aluminum and a conductive film in which carbon filler is kneaded to a resin. Thus, the pH decrease of the electrolyte is held down, the generation of hydrogen gas from the zinc being a negative electrode active material can be restrained without using mercury, so that a dry cell which is excellent in safety and has high industrial utility value can be obtained.

title: Atmosphere modifier
abstract: A refrigerated environment, an atmosphere refiner and method of prolonging the life of perishable items stored in said environment. The environment, refiner and method all comprise an oxygen removal means. The oxygen removal means comprises a hydrogen fuel cell. The hydrogen fuel cell removes oxygen by combusting the oxygen within the environment producing H2O and electricity to power the fridge. The hydrogen fuel cell can also be a dual fuel cell. The refiner or environment can also be provided with a hydrogen generator. The heat generated by the hydrogen fuel cell can be used to warm the refrigerant. The environment or refiner can be part of a domestic refrigerator, industrial practice, transportation process, storage facility, container, food production, refrigeration unit or medical environment. The environment or refiner can also have an anti-microbial means. Parts of the oxygen removal means can be provided internally or externally (See figures 1 and 2).

title: Selective removal of nitrogen from natural gas by pressure swing adsorption
abstract: A pressure swing adsorption process for the separation of nitrogen from natural gas utilizes two separate pressure swing adsorption stages, the first containing a hydrocarbon-selective adsorbent and the second containing a nitrogen-selective adsorbent. In the process, the product stream from the first pressure swing adsorption unit contains a natural gas stream having a reduced hydrocarbon content and the product stream from the second pressure swing adsorption unit is a natural gas stream having a reduced nitrogen concentration. The product from the second pressure swing adsorption unit is used to desorb the hydrocarbons from the first pressure swing adsorption unit so as to add the hydrocarbons, which have heat value to the product natural gas stream. Periodically, heating the nitrogen-selective adsorbent with heated product stream from the second pressure swing adsorption unit has been found to improve the capacity of the nitrogen-selective adsorbent to adsorb nitrogen.

title: Absorptive freezer
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To operate an absorptive freezer stably for a long period by preventing the embrittlement of a palladium cell. SOLUTION: An absorptive freezer is provided with a gas storage valve 72 at the inlet of a main tank 73, and at start of cooling operation the current application to the electric heater 84 of a hydrogen gas discharger 80 connected to the main tank part 73 is started with the gas storage valve 72 open, and after a specified time, the opening and closing control of the gas storage valve 72 is started. At finish of the cooling operation, only the opening and closing control of the gas storage valve 72 is terminated, and the gas storage valve 72 is opened, and after passage of a specified time, the current application to the electric heater 84 is stopped. The temperature up and temperature down of a container 82 for hydrogen discharge are performed when there is no hydrogen gas within the container 82 for hydrogen gas discharge, so this absorptive freezer can prevent the embrittlement.  At operation stoppage, a current is not applied to the electric heater 84 during waiting, so this can save the energy.

title: Dehydrogenation catalyst and its production method
abstract: An object of the present invention is to provide a dehydrogenation catalyst which is difficult to peel off from a substrate and has high hydrogen generation efficiency, and a method for producing the same.   A dehydrogenation catalyst (11) welded to a substrate (11c) includes a Ni porous body (11a) and a catalyst (11b) supported in pores of the Ni porous body (11a), and is composed of a hydrocarbon-based fuel. In particular, it can be suitably used for the dehydrogenation reaction of decalin or tetralin. Further, the dehydrogenation catalyst 11 can be suitably used as a reaction means of a hydrogen gas generator for supplying hydrogen gas to a hydrogen using device.   [Selection diagram] Fig. 1

title: Nitrogen/hydrogen flow mixing device
abstract: A nitrogen/hydrogen flow mixing device for production of float glass comprises a nitrogen gas inlet pipe and a nitrogen/hydrogen mixed gas outlet pipe that are arranged on the two sides of the deviceand a hydrogen gas inlet pipe arranged at the middle part of the device. The three pipes are assembled through a tee joint. The nitrogen gas inlet pipe and the nitrogen/hydrogen mixed gas outlet pipeare both a pipe with aperture gradually reducing from the outside end to the middle part of the device in a streamline pattern, and an annular hydrogen gas input cavity and an annular hydrogen gas outlet that are communicated with the hydrogen gas inlet pipe are arranged at the opposite ends of the nitrogen gas inlet pipe and the nitrogen/hydrogen mixed gas outlet pipe. The nitrogen/hydrogen flowmixing device has the characteristics of simple structure, convenient assembly, and safe and reliable usage.

title: Water management and cooling during electrolysis
abstract: A method of electrolysing water, using a water electrolyser having cathode and anode compartments respectively on either side of a hydrophilic polymer cation-exchange membrane, the method comprising: (i) adding water to the anode compartment only, such that the cathode compartment is predominantly free of water in liquid form; (ii) electrolysing the water to form hydrogen gas in the cathode compartment and oxygen gas in the anode compartment; and (iii) re-circulating the hydrogen gas through the cathode compartment.

title: Reactant gas supplying device for fuel cell power generating installation
abstract: PURPOSE:To suppress unnecessary gas consumption thereby improve maintainability of reactant gas supplying device by supplying the reactant gas to be consumed in a fuel cell body from an auxiliary reactant gas supplying device of a small capacity separately installed, when the installation is out of power generation such as in the case of shut down or the like. CONSTITUTION:A hydrogen gas bomb 2 and an oxygen gas bomb 3 as reactant gases suppliers are pipe-connected with a fuel cell body 1 through reactnat gases supplying lines 4, 5 which supply fuel and oxidizing agent. The reactant gases are supplied into the cell by adjusting the gas pressure through pressure control valves 6. Meanwhile, separately from the reactant gas suppliers of of the bombs 2, 3, an auxiliary reactant gas generator 10 of a small capacity is pipe-connected with the branching lines 8, 9 led out from the lines 4, 5 in the device of the power generating installation. The reactant gases consumed in the fuel cell body 1 is supplied from the gas generator 10, while the installation is out of power generation such as in the case of the installation shut down or the like. With the above constitution, unnecessary gas consumption is suppressed and maintainability is improved at the reactant gas supplier device.

title: Method for dehalogenating halogen compound
abstract: PURPOSE:To efficiently dehalogenate halogen compound under a hydrogen pressure condition having high safety by using a specific halogen compound and a hydrogen storage alloy having a CaCu5 type crystal structure of hexagonal system comprising a rare earth element or Ca and Ni as essential elements. CONSTITUTION:In dehalogenating a halogen compound of the formula R-X (R is organic atomic group; X is halogen) [e.g. 3-chloro-2-(4-isobutylphenyl) propionic acid], the compound is brought into contact with a hydrogen storage alloy (e.g. CaNi5) having a CaCu5 type crystal structure of hexagonal system comprising N (a rare earth element or Ca element) and Ni as essential elements so that the halide is efficiently dehalogenated under <10kg/cm<2> hydrogen pressure of highly safe condition to give a corresponding dehalogenated substance [e.g. 2-(4-isobutylphenyl)propionic acid].

title: Rapid forming of an electrode
abstract: A method for forming a negative electrode for a lithium-ion cell, comprising the steps: first constant-current charging with a first charging current until a first half-cell potential versus a reference electrode is reached, first constant-voltage charging at the first half-cell potential versus the reference electrode until a second charging current is reached, second constant-current charging with the second charging current until a second half-cell potential versus the reference electrode is reached, second constant-voltage charging at the second half-cell potential versus the reference electrode until a final charging current is reached.

abstract: A positive electrode composition is presented. title: Home fuel cell boiler system having combustion catalysis device
abstract: The present invention relates to a domestic fuel cell boiler system having a catalytic combustor. This is the fuel cell stack 23 in the home fuel cell boiler system 3 in which fuel from the fuel supply unit 21 is supplied as hydrogen gas through the reformer 22 to generate electricity in the fuel cell stack 23. A catalyst combustor 31 receiving combustion gas discharged from the combustion gas and burning the catalyst by a catalytic reaction, and injecting the fuel supplied from the fuel supply part 21 to further burn the catalyst by the catalytic reaction when hot water is needed; A reaction heat exchanger (32) for transferring heat generated in the combustion process of the catalytic combustor (31) to the reformer (22) normally; And a hot water heat exchanger 33 for receiving hot water from the reaction heat heat exchanger 32 to generate hot water when necessary. As a result, there is no emission of pollutants, no need for a separate hot water storage tank and auxiliary boiler for waste heat recovery, an improvement in fuel cell power generation efficiency, and a reduction in the volume of the entire system. Provide effect.

title: Process and device for heating reformer
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To implement a technique for efficiently heating a reformer using a burner of a Bunsen's combustion method with a high primary air ratio or a totally aerated primary combustion method and for heating the reformer by safely burning a refined gas or an off-gas before the gas components stabilize. SOLUTION: The device is a heating device for a reformer 42 which reforms a hydrocarbon gas into a hydrogen gas-containing reformed gas and supplies it to a fuel cell 36. This heating device is equipped with a reformer burner 46, a pilot burner 54, a second pipeline 23b for supplying the reformed gas to the pilot burner 54, a third pipeline 23c for supplying the reformed gas to the fuel cell 36, a fifth pipeline 23e for supplying the off-gas which had passed through the fuel cell 36 to the reformer burner 46 and switching valves SV4 and SV5 for opening either the second pipeline 23b or the third pipeline 23c and blocking the other. In this heating device, after the reformed gas components have stabilized, the switching valves SV4 and SV5 are switched to block the second pipeline 23b and to open the third pipeline 23c.

title: Primary galvanic cell with electrolessly zinc-plated negative electrode lead
abstract: In a primary galvanic cell containing alkaline electrolyte, the lead for the negative electrode is provided with an electrolessly deposited zinc coating up to the end which comes within the sealing region, including the head part, and the zinc-plated part is additionally protected by a paraffin skin. The negative electrode preferably consists of Hg-free zinc. The pore-free zinc layer gives the lead approximately the same electrochemical potential as that of the anode zinc and thereby makes the cell less susceptible to hydrogen gassing.

title: Novel negative electrode material, preparation method and application thereof
abstract: The invention relates to a novel cathode material, the chemical formula of which is SiO x /C(0.88≤x≤0.98)，SiO x The particle surface is coated by carbon, the carbon content is 1.0-10.0 wt%, a Si (111) characteristic peak exists in the range of 26-30 degrees corresponding to 2 theta on an X-ray diffraction pattern, the Si crystal grain size is 2.0-10.0 nm calculated according to the Sheer formula, and the particle size volume distribution is D 10 ≥1.0μm，D 50 ＝2.0～7.0μm，D 90 ＝4.0～14.0μm，D max Less than or equal to 25.0 mu m. The novel negative electrode material is charged and discharged under 0-1.5V, and has high reversible specific capacity (more than or equal to 1600mAh/g) and first timeThe coulombic efficiency (more than or equal to 77.0 percent) has lower volume expansion effect and excellent cycle performance (the reversible specific capacity of mixed graphite is about 450mAh/g, the capacity retention rate is more than or equal to 98.5 percent after 50 cycles, and the maximum lithium-embedded pole piece expansion is less than or equal to 21.0 percent).

title: The covalent organic framework that lithium-sulfur cell is adulterated with selenium-sulphur anode composite material and its synthetic method
abstract: The covalent organic framework that lithium-sulfur cell is adulterated with selenium-sulphur anode composite material and its synthetic method, the anode composite material include covalent organic framework and load to Se and S in covalent organic carbon skeleton.Synthetic method of the present invention includes the following steps: that by selenium, nitrile and sulphur be that 0.01~0.2:0.1~0.4:1 carries out grinding or ball milling with mass ratio, obtains mixed-powder；It will be heat-treated after mixed-powder vacuum sealing, obtain covalent organic framework-sulphur anode composite material of selenium doping, the heat treatment method are as follows: mixed-powder is heat-treated 12~24 hours at 100~200 DEG C, is then warming up to 400~600 DEG C, is heat-treated 20~40 hours.Anode composite material prepared by the present invention is applied to lithium-sulfur cell, substantially increases the discharge capacity of lithium-sulfur cell, significantly reduces the capacity attenuation rate of lithium-sulfur cell.

title: Method and apparatus for forming gas
abstract: <P>PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a method and an apparatus for forming a gas safely and automatically forming a gas in a simple structure.  <P>SOLUTION: In a reaction vessel 10; a gas pressure in a gas storing vessel 30 and a liquid feeding pressure from a water storing vessel 20 act with each other, when the gas pressure of the gas storing vessel 30 becomes relatively larger than the liquid feeding pressure, water in the reaction vessel 10 returns automatically to the water storing vessel 20 side and is brought into non-contact with a solid raw material 2, and the reaction stops, when the gas pressure of the gas storing vessel 30 becomes relatively smaller than the liquid feeding pressure, the solid raw material 2 is brought into contact with a gas by feeding water into the reaction vessel 10 for forming the gas, and the gas of a predetermined pressure is stored in the gas storing vessel 30 by the pressure balance in the reaction vessel 10.  <P>COPYRIGHT: (C)2010,JPO&INPIT

title: Heat exchanger for air conditioner using metal hydride
abstract:
The present invention relates to a heat exchanger of a heating and cooling device using a hydrogen storage alloy. As an example of the heat exchanger according to the present invention, the heat exchanger tube 22, the mesh tube 32, the support member 34, and the heat dissipation member 24 may be configured. The heat transfer tube 22 accommodates the hydrogen storage alloy therein and is arranged in parallel in plurality. The mesh tube 32 is a network structure for passing hydrogen separated or bonded to the hydrogen storage alloy, and has a structure in which at least one is accommodated inside the heat transfer tube 22 and exposed up and down. The support member 34 is mounted on the upper and lower ends so as to maintain a gap between the heat transfer tube 22 and the mesh tube 32 and prevents the hydrogen storage alloy from being separated from the heat transfer tube 22. The heat dissipation member 24 is connected between the respective heat transfer tubes 22. 
     Therefore, according to the amount and pressure of the hydrogen to be transferred to the heat exchanger, it is easy to combine and separate the hydrogen, there is an effect that can achieve high efficiency by rapid heat transfer.

title: Fuel processor for fuel cell system
abstract: The present invention prevents incomplete combustion or extinguishing of burners by detecting the oxygen concentration contained in the exhaust gas and controlling the amount of oxidant supplied by the oxidant supply in the fuel processing device for a fuel cell. In order to provide a fuel cell fuel processing apparatus which can be reduced, a fuel processor having an exhaust port of exhaust gas according to the present invention; An oxidant supplier for controlling and supplying an amount of oxidant supplied to the fuel processor in accordance with the electrical oxidant supply control signal; A fuel supplier for controlling and supplying an amount of fuel gas supplied to the fuel processor according to the electrical fuel gas supply control signal; A flow rate meter for measuring a flow rate of the fuel gas supplied by the fuel supplier and providing the flow rate as an electrical flow rate measurement signal; An oxygen sensor detecting an oxygen concentration contained in the exhaust gas; And a controller for outputting an oxidant supply control signal to the oxidant supply to adjust the amount of the oxidant supplied by the oxidant supply based on the oxygen concentration detected from the oxygen sensor.

title: Catalytic combustion type reactor
abstract: PURPOSE:To reduce thermal expansion and to prevent the pulverization and damage of a combustion catalyst by forming the combustion catalyst in a combustion zone and a reaction tube into a block having a specified size, and constituting the combustion zone by piling up the blocks. CONSTITUTION:A combustion zone 5 of a catalytic combustion type reactor is composed of an aggregate of a block 15 wherein a reaction tube 8 and combustion catalyst 9 are united to a body. For example, in the vertical direction of the combustion zone 5 an expanded tube part 17 of the block 15 is fitted to a junction 18 of a tube plate 3 to connect with a bonding material or the like and piled. The expanded tube part 17 of the next block 15 is successively piled on the projection of the former block 15. Meanwhile, the blocks 15 are successively arranged in order in horizontal direction to constitute the combustion zone 5.

title: Pyrolisis Gasification melting grate cremation outomatic system
abstract:
The present invention is a high temperature hydrogen gas obtained from the heat-activated catalytic chemical reaction using waste heat generated during the cremation of the carcass, and the body and animal carcasses can be completely burned to minimize the generation of pollutants and improve the efficiency of the land. The present invention relates to a carcass pyrolysis gasification liquefaction system using hydrogen gas.
     
    The present invention can improve the efficiency of the land, and in the saridification system through carcass cremation to drastically reduce the graveyard area, by using hydrogen gas as the fuel in the furnace, the high heat generated by the thermal decomposition of the carcass cremation itself, A process and a device for minimizing the volume required for cremation and minimizing the size of the body to be thermally decomposed by the high temperature pyrolysis to produce a combustible gas; The high temperature gas generated at this time is utilized as a heat source for maintaining the reaction of pyrolysis gasification by the thermally active catalyst chemical reaction so that the hydrogen gas is produced by itself, and the hydrogen gas produced through the thermally active catalyst chemical reaction is fueled. A combustion unit melting furnace using hydrogen gas provided as a fuel; A cooling water for directly transferring the solid carbide residue remaining in the pyrolyzed part to a melting part to be melted with hydrogen gas, and cooling the char discharged from the melting furnace to solidify it; Low temperature plasma to ionize the gas by the combined reaction with the hydrogen gas to the exhaust gas discharged through the pyrolysis gasification and melt-sintering to the rear end equipment to remove the harmful gas; A selective catalytic reduction reaction column connected to the low temperature plasma reactor and configured to monitor the exhaust gas in real time as a purified gas; Including a stack connected to a selective catalytic reduction reaction tower, it is possible to make permanent preservation with a very small salivation as a whole, and to carry out a process for removing harmful substances. to provide.  
   
    In addition, by installing a plurality of carcass pipe in the pyrolysis furnace in which the carcass makeup is made, it is easy to process a plurality of carcasses per hour, thereby providing a system capable of obtaining a high heat amount generated during cremation.

title: Power plants which include at least one hot gas engine
abstract: 1508996 Hot gas engines AUTOMOTIVE PRODUCTS Ltd 15 May 1975 [20 May 1974] 22347/74 Heading F1S [Also in Division F4] In a power-plant including one or more hot gas engines 1, 2 the combustion means 42 and the cooling means 31, 32 of the engine or of one of the engines are inter-connected by conduit means 41 for conveying fluid from those cooling means to the combustion means for participating in combustion in the combustion means, fluid so conveyed having been used as cooling fluid in these cooling means and having had its physical state changed by heat which it absorbed in those cooling means and which had been extracted from working fluid in the second working fluid space 25, 26 of the engine of which those cooling means form part and the combustion means 42 are associated with the heating means 29, 30 of the engine or at least a first of the engines so that when the power-plant is in operation heat generated in the combustion means is supplied to the heating means for heating working fluid in the first working fluid space 23, 24 of the engine or of each of the engines of which the heating means forms a part. As shown the fluid conveyed to the combustion means through the conduit 41 is hydrogen from a tank 38 containing liquid hydrogen 37. The fuel employed may be propane or butane. In a modification (not shown) liquid oxygen is employed for cooling one of the hot gas engines and the oxygen is vapourized by heat exchange with the cooling water and then fed to the combustion means 42. In another modification (not shown) the heater of one hot gas engine is heated by solar energy. Alternatively it is stated that river water may be used instead of fluid heated by solar energy for supply to the engine heater.

title: Preparation method of porous copper with high porosity in thin walled tube shape
abstract: A preparation method of porous copper with a high porosity in a thin walled tube shape is characterized by comprising the following steps: preparing ball-shaped copper powder and a binding agent according to a volume percentage of 50%-60%:50%-40%, heating the binding agent to a temperature of 80 to 95 DEG C, when the binding agent is totally melted, adding the ball-shaped copper powder, mixing at a temperature of 80 to 95 DEG C for 1 to 1.5 hours, pouring the fluid mixture into a cavity of a mould with a temperature of 80 to 90 DEG C, forming so as to obtain a blank, cooling, demoulding; placing the formed blank into a degreasing furnace, burying the blank into Al2O3 powder, in the protection of hydrogen gas, heating to a temperature of 45 to 55 DEG C at a heating speed of 8 to 12 DEG C per minute, maintaining the temperature for 20 to 40 minutes, heating to a temperature of 210 to 230 DEG C at a heating speed of 1 to 3 DEG C per minute, maintaining the temperature for 1 to 4 hours, heating to a temperature of 810 to 890 DEG C at a heating speed of 8 to 12 DEG C per minute, and sintering for 1 to 3 hours. The defects that mould forming is difficult to achieve after the addition of a pore-forming agent and the sintering manufacture cost is high are solved; the prepared porous copper in a thin walled tube shape can obtain a perforated structure, and moreover the preparation method has the advantages of simple technology and low manufacture cost.

title: Parahydrogen adiabatic conversion refrigerating device
abstract: The utility model provides a parahydrogen adiabatic conversion refrigerating device. The refrigerating device mainly comprises a liquid hydrogen storing tank, a converter, a catalyst, a throttling hole, a hydrogen guiding pipe, a condensing heat exchanger, an emptying leading-out pipe and a valve, wherein the converter, the throttling hole, the hydrogen guiding pipe and the condensing heat exchanger are mounted at the top end of the inside of the liquid hydrogen storing tank; a parahydrogen conversion catalyst is contained in the converter; one end of the throttling hole is connected with the converter while the other end is connected with the hydrogen guiding pipe; two ends of the condensing heat exchanger are respectively connected with the hydrogen guiding pipe and the emptying leading-out pipe; and the emptying leading-out pipe extends out of the liquid hydrogen storing tank and is connected with the valve. According to the refrigerating device, the parahydrogen in the storing tank can be converted into orthohydrogen; and the hydrogen in the tank is liquefied partially by utilizing the converted refrigerating energy, so that the evaporation loss of the liquid hydrogen storage is reduced. The refrigerating device is free of requiring a moving component and extra power and is high in reliability, thereby being widely applied to the liquid hydrogen storage on the ground and in the space.

title: Sulfur-carrying composite cathode material and preparation method and application thereof
abstract: The invention provides a sulfur-carrying composite anode material and a preparation method thereof, after mixing sulfur-containing suspension and a carrier material, large-particle isolated sulfur is converted into a sulfur coating on the surface of a carrier uniformly by utilizing the solid-liquid-solid phase conversion process of elemental sulfur in a solvent, and then an organic solvent is removed to obtain the sulfur-carrying composite anode material; the support material comprises a carbon material. The preparation method is simple in preparation process and convenient to operate, the dissolving-precipitating balance of elemental sulfur in the selected organic solvent is utilized, the carbon material and the elemental sulfur dissolved in the organic solvent are strongly interacted, so that the sulfur in the solution is continuously deposited on the surface of the carrier material, the elemental sulfur which is not originally dissolved in the organic solvent is continuously dissolved in the organic solvent and further continuously deposited on the surface of the sulfur-carrying material, and the uniformly distributed sulfur-carrying composite anode material can be obtained.

title: External compact coating method and coating device for silicon-carbon negative electrode material intermediate
abstract: The invention discloses an external compact coating device for a silicon-carbon cathode material intermediate, which comprises two rotating shafts arranged horizontally in parallel and upright posts arranged at the front end and the rear end of each rotating shaft in a rolling manner, wherein a winding wheel and a driven gear I are fixedly arranged on each rotating shaft; a driving motor is fixedly installed on the upright post, and a driving gear meshed with the driven gear is installed on an output shaft of the driving motor; and a pull rope is wound on the winding wheel. Has the advantages that: the external compact coating method and the coating device for the silicon-carbon negative electrode material intermediate can quickly and uniformly coat asphalt on the surface of the silicon-carbon negative electrode material intermediate, ensure that the problem of asphalt agglomeration is avoided, and have the advantages of uniform coating, good coating effect and strong practicability.

title: Method for producing power utilizing natural heat energy and prime move
abstract: The present invention relates to a method for using natural heat energy to produce power and a prime move thereof. The prime move produces energy by the manner that under atmospheric pressure or slightly higher pressure, the liquid (propane, isobutene, Freon or ammonia can be used) with the boiling point approximate to normal atmosphere temperature is arranged in a pressure vessel, and the heat energy can collect the heat source formed naturally, such as solar energy, hot well, terrestrial heat or other heat energy sources. The heat energy is conducted into the pressure vessel containing liquid-gas phase liquid to increase the liquid temperature up to the boiling point and produce gas. The liquid is turned into gas, so the size can expand hundreds times and great pressure is produced, and the produced pressure is big enough to drive the prime move. The present invention can be used in automobile, engine, electrical machine, ship, family, factory or other applicable occasions to rotate a motor, thus reducing the use of petrochemical fuel further.

title: Improvements in or relating to an akylation process
abstract: An olefinic hydrocarbon fraction containing a mono-olefin of at least 7 carbon atoms, and preferentially oil-soluble, neutral, oxygen-containing organic compounds, including those having a carbonyl group, is contacted with an alkylatable compound, and a catalyst comprising liquid hydrogen fluoride, under alkylating conditions, alkylation products, and a liquid catalyst phase comprising said oxygen-containing compounds being separated from the reaction mixture.  C10-C15 fractions synthesized from carbon monoxide and hydrogen may be used to alkylate monocyclic aromatic hydrocarbons such as benzene and toluene and the alkylates sulphonated to produce detergents.  The catalyst phase may be treated to recover the oxygenated compounds and the catalyst recycled.  The alkylation temperature may be from about 50-250 DEG  F.  The amount of hydrogen fluoride may be 3-100 per cent or more by volume of the total reactants and should be sufficient to give two liquid phases. Alkylatable compounds which may be used include monocyclic aromatic hydrocarbons and substitution derivatives, e.g. benzene, toluene, xylene, ethylbenzene, n- and iso-propyl benzenes, ethyl toluenes, pseudocumene, butylbenzenes, chlorobenzene, phenol, cresols, anisol, phenetol, and benzoic acid, polycyclic compounds, e.g. naphthalene, anthracene, diphenyl, and naphthols, paraffins such as isobutane, isopentane, methylcyclopentane, and methylcyclohexane, and mixtures such as are produced by hydroforming of naphthas or solvent extraction of cracked gas oils.  The catalyst may contain minor amounts, such as 0.5-10 per cent by weight, of BF3, H2SO4, H3PO4, H3BO2F2, FSO3H, fluorophosphoric acids, SO2Cl2, ethane or toluene sulphonic acid, trifluoracetic acid or the like.  A C12 "Synthol" fraction may be introduced into a mixture of toluene and HF, the reactants being pre-dried, and the products passed to a stratifier. The lower catalyst layer may be passed to a recovery tower stripping gas being introduced if desired to separate HF and recover oxygenated compounds, viz. aldehydes and ketones.  The upper layer may be stripped of HF and fractionated to separate toluene from alkylate and unreacted olefins and paraffins.  The paraffins may be separated and worked up for use as detergents by conversion to alkyl halides and alkylation of aromatics followed by sulphonation or by conversion to alkanesulphonyl halides and thence to sulphonates.  Polyalkylates may be separated and recycled.  Comparative experiments in alkylation with HF, AlCl3, H2SO4 and solid phosphoric acid catalyst are given.

title: Cooling device in fuel cell vehicle
abstract: An object of the present invention is to more effectively use air that has passed through a fuel cell.   A fuel cell motorcycle includes a fuel cell for generating predetermined power using hydrogen gas and air as a reaction source, a fuel cylinder for supplying hydrogen gas to the fuel cell through a hydrogen supply path, and an external air A supercharger 70 that supplies air to the fuel cell 12, a conduit 420 that discharges air from the fuel cell 12 to the outside, an in-wheel motor that is a drive source of the fuel cell motorcycle, and a motor driver that drives the in-wheel motor 134, and in the air system system 400, the path of the pipeline and the outlet thereof are arranged toward the motor driver 134, and the motor driver 134 is applied by applying exhaust air that has passed through the fuel cell 12 to the heat sink 135. Is cooled.   [Selection] Figure 11

title: Alkaline battery
abstract:
PURPOSE: To suppress an internal pressure rise caused by the hydrogen gas generation during storage by making a gelled negative electrode contain an indium compound and a specified compound as the anticorrosive of zing alloy powder in respective specified ratios to the zinc alloy powder.  
     CONSTITUTION: A gelled load containing an alkaline electrolyte is provided together with a zinc alloy powder as an active material, and the gelled load contains an indium compound in an amount of 0.01-1.0wt.% by indium conversion to the zinc alloy powder and a compound represented by the formula I in an amount of 0.01-1.0wt.% to the zinc alloy powder. In the formula I, A represents a phosphoric group (-PO 2 H 2 ), a sulfonic group (-SO 3 H), methylenecarboxylic group (-CH 2 COOH), alkali metal salts of these groups, or hydrogen atom, and n is an integral number from 1 to 60. Thus, the internal pressure rise accompanied by the hydrogen gas generation during storage can be suppressed, and the leak of an electrolyte and the breakage of a battery can be prevented. 
     COPYRIGHT: (C)1992,JPO&Japio

title: Normal temperature adsorbent groove on low temperature storage tank
abstract: The invention discloses a normal temperature adsorbent tank on a low temperature storage tank, which is arranged at a normal temperature adsorbent feeding port of an outer tank and comprises a tank body covering the normal temperature adsorbent feeding port, wherein the tank body is hermetically welded with the outer tank; the groove body is provided with a filling port communicated with the inside and the outside, a normal temperature adsorbent can enter the groove body from the filling port, and a sealing plate for plugging the filling port is arranged at the filling port; and a filter screen is welded at the feeding port of the normal-temperature adsorbent and used for preventing the normal-temperature adsorbent from entering the outer tank. The normal temperature adsorbent is directly poured into the tank body from the filling port, so that the contact time with air is reduced, and the effective time of the adsorbent is prolonged; only need remove the shrouding when the normal atmospheric temperature adsorbent is changed in the later stage, pour the normal atmospheric temperature adsorbent in the cell body, mend new normal atmospheric temperature adsorbent can, change in-process convenience, swift, labour saving and time saving.

title: Fuel cell-fuel cell hybrid system
abstract: A device for converting chemical energy to electricity is provided, the device comprising a high temperature fuel cell with the ability for partially oxidizing and completely reforming fuel, and a low temperature fuel cell juxtaposed to said high temperature fuel cell so as to utilize remaining reformed fuel from the high temperature fuel cell. Also provided is a method for producing electricity comprising directing fuel to a first fuel cell, completely oxidizing a first portion of the fuel and partially oxidizing a second portion of the fuel, directing the second fuel portion to a second fuel cell, allowing the first fuel cell to utilize the first portion of the fuel to produce electricity; and allowing the second fuel cell to utilize the second portion of the fuel to produce electricity.

title: PROCESS FOR DISCHARGE OF PRESSURE LIQUID NATURAL GAS CONTAINERS.
abstract: A process for discharging a plurality of containers (1, 2) having liquefied gas under pressure contained therein is disclosed. A gas is supplied to pressurize (10) a first container or group of containers to discharge the liquefied gas therefrom. The gas is then removed to displace from the first container or group of containers and is separated into a first stream of steam and into a second stream of steam. The first steam stream (52) is heated and passed to the first container or group of containers. The second steam stream is fed to a second container or group of containers to discharge the liquefied gas from them. Communication is cut between the first container or group of containers and the second container or group of containers, and the previous steps are repeated for all the containers in succession, with only the last container or group of containers emptying of liquid remaining at the pressure of the gas to displace, and all the containers being filled at the end of the process, except the last container or group of containers, with a lower pressure steam.

title: Method for producing succinic acid
abstract: A method for producing high-purity succinic acid in which malic acid by-product is suppressed is provided.   An aqueous solvent and a noble metal catalyst are charged into a pressurized reactor and then hydrogen gas is supplied until the pressurized state is reached. Then, a raw material maleic anhydride or maleic acid is added to the reactor to carry out a hydrogenation reaction. In the process for producing succinic acid, 10% by weight or more of maleic anhydride or maleic acid added to the pressure reactor is added by the sequential addition method.   [Selection figure] None

title: Production of algaas epitaxial wafer for red led
abstract:
PURPOSE: To obtain a product having high luminance by heat-treating an epitaxial wafer grown by liquid-phase epitaxial method, under a specific condition.  
     CONSTITUTION: A retaining tool 2 holding plural sheets of Al x Ga 1-x As epitaxial wafer 1 for red LED having 0.25-0.40 crystal ratio having single or double hetero structure and 0.25-0.40 crystal ratio (X) grown by liquid-phase epitaxial method is arranged in a quartz reaction tube 3. Then the water 1 is heated by a heater 4 at a position corresponding to the retaining tool 2 at the outer peripheral of the reaction tube 3 at 600-1,050°C for 15 minutes to 4 hours. The heat treatment is carried out in a hydrogen gas treatment. When the temperature of heat treatment is ≤ the lower limit temperature, growth completion temperature of ordinary epitaxial wafer is 600°C and the heat treatment has slight effect and when the temperature of heat treatment exceeds the upper limit, the wafer is extremely damaged. When the heat-treating time is ≥ lower limit, effects can not be expected and when the heat-treating time exceeds the upper limit, deterioration of bonding characteristics caused by diffusion of impurities and disadvantage of mass production are brought about. 
     COPYRIGHT: (C)1991,JPO&Japio

title: Acid ndfeb magnet recycling
abstract: recycling of ndfeb magnets in an acid medium, belonging to the sector of magnets or hard magnetic bodies of inorganic materials containing microparticles of micron-scale grain particles, refers to the recycling of nd-fe-b magnets in acid medium, more specifically by leaching these magnets with solutions of hydrochloric acid, nitric acid and selective precipitation of nd³ + ions such as neodymium oxalate and their subsequent conversion to neodymium oxide by heat treatment, the capture of hydrogen gas generated in the process and the copper used as magnet coating.

title: Preparation method of organic-transition metal hydride complexes containing aryl group or alkyl group
abstract: The present invention relates to a more advanced preparation method of organic-transition metal hydride as a hydrogen storage material, precisely a more advanced preparation method of organic-transition metal hydride containing aryl or alkyl group that facilitates safe and reverse storage of massive amount of hydrogen. The present invention relates to a preparation method of an organic-transition metal hydride comprising the steps of preparing a complex reducing agent composition by reacting alkali metal, alkali earth metal or a mixture thereof and (C10-C20) aromatic compound in aprotic polar solvent; and preparing organic-transition metal hydride by reacting the prepared complex reducing agent composition and organic-transition metal halide. The method of the present invention has advantages of minimizing the numbers and the amounts of byproducts by using a complex reducing agent and producing organic-transition metal hydride safely without denaturation under more moderate reaction conditions.

title: Device for decomposition of water with addition of ironmaking hot-blast stove thermal force with microwaves
abstract: The invention relates to a device for decomposition of water with addition of ironmaking hot-blast stove thermal force with microwaves. A hot-blast stove provides a high temperature of 1100 DEG C, water is instantly steamed, and the steamed steam is at least 800 DEG C. The high temperature steam of the time contains huge molecular energy, the structure of water molecules is allowed to be in unstable state, and the water molecules are struck with each other at high temperature; then electromagnetic radiation is emitted through a magnetic control type electromagnetic wave radiation pipe, and the internal structure balance of the water molecules at the high temperature steam is further broken. At the time, fragile water molecule chains contain huge energy, and hydrogen atoms and oxygen atoms are collapsed at any time and are fragile in connection; mini lightning is emitted through a Tesla discharger, independent positrons and electrons of hydrogen atoms and oxygen atoms are added, and in the case of sufficient positrons and electrons, the water molecules are instantly decomposed into independent hydrogen atoms and oxygen atoms; in a U-shaped pipe, the hydrogen atoms and the oxygen atoms are respectively adsorbed by positive and negative electric fields and are finally condensed in a hydrogen gas collection tank and an oxygen gas collection tank. By the method, the water can be decomposed quickly and efficiently to produce required hydrogen gas and oxygen gas used in industrial production and combustion chambers.

title: A kind of MXene- metallic composite and preparation method thereof
abstract: The present invention relates to a kind of MXene- metallic composite and preparation method thereof, the technical solution adopted by the present invention are as follows: (1) by metal salt particle and hydrofluoric acid mixing wiring solution-forming, then MAX raw material is put into solution and are stirred.(2) to after the reaction was completed, be centrifuged to reaction solution in step 1), the electrode material of MXene- metal composite is can be obtained in solid washing, vacuum drying to obtaining after centrifugation.The present invention makes full use of the hydrogen exhaust gas during preparing MXene using MAX, the MXene- metal composite electrode material of preparation has the advantages that be uniformly dispersed, specific energy height, the good, simple process of cyclicity etc. have, simultaneously, the exhaust gas generated in reaction process is efficiently used, production process and cost are reduced, high production efficiency can preferably meet the needs of industrialized production, realize large-scale production, great application prospect.

title: Manufacture of amorphous silicon photosensitive body
abstract: PURPOSE:To raise an adhesive strength of a photosensitive body film and a supporting body by resolving a gas evaporated from a halogenide of silicon by a plasma discharge, and forming an amorphous silicon film on the supporting body whose temperature is controlled. CONSTITUTION:The inside of a vacuum vessel is exhausted by an oil diffusing pump 16 and an oil rotating pump 17a, and a temperature of a supporting body is controlled by a base plate heating heater 13. When an internal pressure of the vacuum vessel 20 reaches a prescribed pressure, when a carrier gas is made to flow to a pipe 6c, the gas passes through in a liquid 9 of SiCl4, and vapor of SiCl4 is carried to a pipe 6d. On the other hand, hydrogen whose pressure is controlled and whose flow rate is controlled by a mass flow controller 5b is made to flow through a pipe 6b from a bomb 2. Also, a diborane gas diluted by helium is made to flow from a doping gas bomb 3. These gases are mixed in a pipe 6e and blown out from an electrode 10. In this state, when a high frequency electric power is applied between the electrode 10 and a supporting body 12, a plasma discharge occurs, and an a-Si film of a boron dope is formed on an alminum supporting body. As for an a-Si photosensitive body film manufactured by this method, its adhesive strength is good.

title: Gold nanoparticle-loaded lanthanum ferrite film and preparation method and application thereof
abstract: The invention relates to lanthanum ferrite (LaFeO) loaded with gold nanoparticles 3 LFO) film and a preparation method and application thereof. The invention adopts a sol-gel method to prepare mixed liquid containing chloroauric acid and LFO sol precursor with adjustable proportion, combines a spin coating technology and a heat treatment process to form an Au @ LFO gel precursor film with controllable thickness on FTO glass, and forms a crystallized Au @ LFO film after annealing. The test shows that the LFO film is in a net structure, and Au is distributed on the surface and inside of the film in the form of nano particles. After the nano gold particles with the content of 0.33 to 0.83 percent are added, the photocurrent density of the LFO is from 5.9 mu A/cm 2 Is increased to 23.5 mu A/cm 2 The photocurrent is increased by 3 times. Meanwhile, Au @ LFO enhances the absorption of visible light, promotes the generation of hot electrons and holes and the separation of hot charges, and increases the photocurrent. The film enriches the selection of photoelectrochemical electrode materials, and the loaded metal nano material can be expanded to other plasmon metals, and has potential application value for efficient conversion of solar energy to chemical energy.
text: Gold nanoparticle-loaded lanthanum ferrite film and preparation method and application thereof The invention relates to lanthanum ferrite (LaFeO) loaded with gold nanoparticles 3 LFO) film and a preparation method and application thereof. The invention adopts a sol-gel method to prepare mixed liquid containing chloroauric acid and LFO sol precursor with adjustable proportion, combines a spin coating technology and a heat treatment process to form an Au @ LFO gel precursor film with controllable thickness on FTO glass, and forms a crystallized Au @ LFO film after annealing. The test shows that the LFO film is in a net structure, and Au is distributed on the surface and inside of the film in the form of nano particles. After the nano gold particles with the content of 0.33 to 0.83 percent are added, the photocurrent density of the LFO is from 5.9 mu A/cm 2 Is increased to 23.5 mu A/cm 2 The photocurrent is increased by 3 times. Meanwhile, Au @ LFO enhances the absorption of visible light, promotes the generation of hot electrons and holes and the separation of hot charges, and increases the photocurrent. The film enriches the selection of photoelectrochemical electrode materials, and the loaded metal nano material can be expanded to other plasmon metals, and has potential application value for efficient conversion of solar energy to chemical energy.

title: Boil-off gas recovery system of hydrogenation station
abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of hydrogenation stations, and discloses an evaporated gas recovery system of a hydrogenation station. The boil-off gas recovery system of the hydrogen filling station comprises a hydrogen storage tank, a hydrogen liquefying system, a liquid hydrogen gasifying system, a liquid hydrogen filling system, a hydrogen filling system and a hydrogen cache system, wherein the hydrogen storage tank comprises a liquid hydrogen storage space and a low-temperature gas phase space, the inlet end of the hydrogen liquefying system is respectively communicated with the liquid hydrogen storage space and the low-temperature gas phase space, the outlet end of the hydrogen liquefying system is respectively communicated with the liquid hydrogen filling system and the liquid hydrogen gasifying system, the outlet end of the liquid hydrogen gasifying system is communicated with the hydrogen filling system, the hydrogen filling system is communicated with the low-temperature gas phase space, the hydrogen cache system is connected in parallel on a pipeline communicated with the hydrogen filling system and the low-temperature gas phase space, and the hydrogen cache system is communicated with the. The boil-off gas recovery system of the hydrogen filling station can realize mixed filling, can fully recycle the hydrogen boil-off gas in the station, and realizes energy conservation and emission reduction.

title: Automatic oxyhydrogen machine of dual-connected tanks for removing carbon deposit of engine
abstract: The invention provides an automatic oxyhydrogen machine of dual-connected tanks for removing carbon deposit of an engine. The inlet of a condenser is communicated with the outlet of an electrolytic cell; the outlet of the condenser is communicated with the inlet of a first gas-water separator; the lower part of the first gas-water separator is communicated with the lower part of a second gas-water separator; a gas outlet of the first gas-water separator is communicated with a gas inlet of the second gas-water separator; an electrolyte outlet of the second gas-water separator is communicated with the electrolyte inlet of the electrolytic cell through a pipeline; a circulating pump is arranged on the pipeline of the second gas-water separator and the electrolytic cell; and the gas outlet of the second gas-water separator is communicated with the lower part of a tank flame arrester through the pipeline. The automatic oxyhydrogen machine strictly controls the liquid level of the electrolyte, the liquid level of water, the content of water in oxyhydrogen gas and air supply amount in the oxyhydrogen gas preparing process to avoid the detonation or water inlet phenomenon of the engine in a decarbonization process, and the process is automatically finished.

title: Preparation of ion exchanged polymer bound nitrogen adsorbent
abstract: A high capacity adsorbent may be used for enriching oxygen concentration in an air stream. Such a high capacity adsorbent may be from about 2 to about 3 times lighter relative to the currently available technology. Furthermore, the high capacity adsorbent is readily capable of regeneration after deactivation by water vapor. Unlike current available immobilization technology in which clay binder was used to bind 13X zeolite and additional 10% organic binder was used to immobilize beads, the adsorbents of the present invention may be made using just an organic binder, thereby reducing pore spoilage caused by the clay binder. Further unlike conventional adsorbents, which may use sodium as its cation, the adsorbent of the present invention uses a lithium cation, thereby resulting in enhanced nitrogen adsorption performance.

title: Getter
abstract: PURPOSE: A hydrogen-purifying getter in a form of metal hydride, MHx (M=Zr, V, Al, Co and Fe), is provided to produce super high purity hydrogen gas by using a reversible reaction between getter and hydrogen. CONSTITUTION: The hydrogen-purifying getter is prepared by the following steps: mixing powders of Zr, V-Al mixed with in a weigh ratio of 65 : 33, and Co in a weight ratio of 70 : 23 : 7; pressure-forming; reacting with hydrogen at 300-1500deg.C for metal hydride(MHx); and adding 5-40wt,%(based on MHx) of Al powder, being substituted by Ni, Cu, Fe or Co, as an additive for increasing strength. The resultant getter purifies hydrogen including impurities(O2, N2, CO, CH3, CO2, etc.) by reversible reaction between getter and hydrogen, which is represented by the formula, M + H2 ↔ MH2, and irreversible reaction between getter and impurities.

title: Heat driving type heat pump device
abstract:
PURPOSE: To permit the miniaturization of the passing part of operating gas, obtain a high output and a high efficiency if the passing part is provided with the same size and prevent the generation of explosion or the like due to the happening of the leakage of the operating gas by a method wherein the operating gas is constituted of mixed gas containing 25-55% of hydrogen gas and helium gas.  
     CONSTITUTION: Operating gas, containing 25-55% of hydrogen gas and helium gas, is sealed into a high temperature side cylinder and a medium temperature side cylinder. A pressure loss of the hydrogen gas, contained in the operating gas by 25% or more, is about 70% compared with the helium gas when it flows whereby the passing part of the operating gas can be miniaturized and, a high output as well as a high efficiency can be obtained when the passing part is provided with the same size. Further, the content of the hydrogen gas is 75% or less and, therefore, explosion or the like will never be generated due to the happening of the leakage of the operating gas.

title: Brazing method of aluminum alloy material and method of manufacturing brazed structure
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a brazing method of an aluminum alloy material, which does not use a flux and forms a junction fillet having sufficient junction strength.SOLUTION: In a brazing method, metallic powder is adhered to a surface of an aluminum alloy material, and then is heated to braze in an inert gas or hydrogen gas atmosphere without using a flux. The aluminum alloy material contains 0.40-1.5 mass% of Mn, and the balance Al with the inevitable impurities. The metallic powder contains at least one, two or more kinds of elements of Si, Mg, Zn, and Al. An adhesion amount of Si to the surface of the aluminum alloy material is 1.0-10.0 g/m, an adhesion amount of Mg is 0.1-3.0 g/m, and an adhesion amount of Zn is 1.0-5.0 g/m.

title: Catalytic dehydrogenation method for hydrazine hydrate through RhNiCo/CeO2@P@C3N4 nano-catalyst
abstract: The invention discloses a catalytic dehydrogenation method for hydrazine hydrate through an RhNiCo/CeO2@P@C3N4 nano-catalyst, and belongs to the technical field of chemistry and chemical engineering. The method comprises the steps that the prepared RhNiCo/CeO2@P@C3N4 nano-catalyst is placed into a reactor; the reactor is placed into an oil bath to be heated to a certain temperature; and then a mixed solution of the hydrazine hydrate and sodium hydroxide is added into the reactor for a reaction, and generated hydrogen is collected in a drainage mode. The RhNiCo/CeO2@P@C3N4 nano-catalyst is high in activity and selectivity. By adopting the nano-catalyst for the dehydrogenation reaction of the hydrazine hydrate, the dehydrogenation conversion rate and the selectivity are both at 100%, and the TOF value of the reaction is greater than 690h1.

title: Enhanced stability water-gas shift reaction catalysts
abstract: The invention provides processes for producing hydrogen that include contacting an input gas stream comprising steam and carbon monoxide with water-gas shift catalysts. The water-gas shift catalysts are copper-based catalysts containing low concentrations of platinum group metals. In some embodiments, the processes of the invention are conducted using water-gas shift catalysts having a an oxide support on which is dispersed copper or an oxide thereof, a platinum group metal and a reducible metal oxide. In other embodiments, the processes of the invention are conducted with a water-gas shift catalysts having a cerium oxide support on which is dispersed copper or an oxide thereof and a platinum group metal.

title: Recovering method for sulfur from high concentration so2 gas containing nh3 and so3
abstract:
PURPOSE: To recover high purity sulfur without clogging an apparatus by converting SO 3  and NH 3  mixed in vaporous sulfur into SO 2  and N 2 , respectively prior to the condensation separation of the vaporous sulfur.  
     CONSTITUTION: Exhaust gas contg. NOX and SOX is fed to activated carbon- packed absorption tower 2 together with NH 3  to decompose the NOX to N 2  as well as allow the SOX to be adsorbed. This carbon is supplied to regeneration tower 6 through line 5 and treated at 300W450°C in inert gas to take out high concn. SO 2  gas contg. SO 3  and NH 3 . This SO 2  gas is fed to the coal- or coke-packed the 1st reactor 8 through line 7 to convert the SO 2  into vaporous sulfur, and the mixed gas of  the sulfur, SO 3  and NH 3  is introduced into the 2nd reactor 11 packed with regenerated activated carbon supplied from tower 6. In reactor 11 the SO 3  and NH 3  are  converted into SO 2  and N 2 , respectively by contact with the carbon at ≥250°C, and this mixed gas is fed to condenser 14 through dust collector 13 to condense and separate the vaporous sulfur. The resulting sulfur is recovered from line 15.

title: Method for preparing 2, 3-butanedione
abstract: The invention discloses a method for preparing 2, 3-butanedione, which takes acetaldehyde as raw material, substituted 5-ethoxy thiazole salt as catalyst of acetaldehyde coupling condensation reaction and one or more in MnO2, Bi2O3, CuSO4 or Fe2(SO4)3 as catalyst for preparing the 2, 3-butanedione by acetylmethylcarbinol oxidative dehydrogenation; and the method adopts a 'one pot' method to realize the new technique for preparing the 2, 3-butanedione by carrying out coupling condensation reaction and oxidative dehydrogenation reaction on dimolecular acetaldehyde. The thiazole salt catalyst of the method is simple in preparation and low in cost, the dosage of the catalyst in the oxidative dehydrogenation reaction is less, and the technique process flow for preparing the 2, 3-butanedione is simplified by the 'one pot' method. The acetaldehyde has the conversion rate of 80-91%, and the 2, 3-butanedione has the selectively of 80-90%. Therefore, the process route provides a new method for the industrial production of the 2, 3-butanedione.

title: Device for the generation of oxyhydrogen.
abstract: Device for the generation of oxyhydrogen, which comprises an electrolytic cell (1) associated with an electrical power source, which causes the electrolysis of the water that forms the electrolyte contained in said electrolytic cell (1) to the passage of an electric current, the power being electrolytic cell (1) connected to a tank (2) by two conduits (11) allowing the recirculation of the electrolyte and the generated oxyhydrogen gas. This tank (2) has an outlet (21) connected to a bubbler (3), which has an outlet (32) of the filtered oxyhydrogen. The power supply comprises an electric pulse modulator (4) connected to the electric cell (1) to modify the amount of electric current supplied, this electric pulse modulator (4) being associated with control and monitoring means.

title: Water vapor transport power generator
abstract: An improved system for generating electrical power using a fuel cell. More particularly, a system for generating hydrogen gas by reacting water vapor with a substantially non-fluid substance and transporting the generated hydrogen gas to the fuel cell which generates electrical power. Reacting water vapor with the non-fluid hydrogen generating substance rather than liquid water prevents caking of the non-fluid substance and deposition of byproducts onto the non-fluid substance that interfere with continued generation of hydrogen gas.

title: Purification apparatus of synthesis gas for biomass gasification
abstract: The present invention is a biomass gasification synthesis gas purification apparatus, to purify the synthesis gas generated in the gasifier, the first cyclone connected to the rear end of the gasifier to remove most of the dust generated in the gasifier, and the 1 A condenser connected to a rear end of the secondary cyclone to recover the wood vinegar by cooling the syngas, a wet scrubber connected to a rear end of the condenser to remove tar, dust, and acid gas in the syngas first, and the wet scrubber A secondary cyclone connected to a rear end of the secondary gas to remove water and tar droplets in the syngas, an adsorption tower to remove tar and water mist not removed from the secondary cyclone, and a rear end of the adsorption tower connected to the It is suitable for operation of tar, dust, and water, which have a great influence on the operation of the gas engine by including a tertiary cyclone that removes dust. title: Hydrogen flow material for metal hydride reaction container
abstract: PURPOSE:To make a hydrogen flow material so as to have heat resistance and elasticity, be setting up a spring coil in the covered layer made up of knitting a long fiber bundle making alumina and silica the main component. CONSTITUTION:A hydrogen flow material 4 is provided with a spring coil 8 in the axial direction, and a porous, tubular covered layer 10 made up of knitting a long fiber bundle 9 making alumina and/or silica the main component is set up around the coil. As for this spring coil 8, for example, it consists of a stainless steel wire or a piano wire. The long fiber bundle consists of more than about 70$% of alumina and/or silica and an oxide and impurities of alkali metal in the rest. With this constitution, it is in no case broken if bending stress is added to the axial direction, and, what is more, it has elasticity even in the radial direction, so that volumetric expansion of the metal hydride can be fully absorbed and relieved.

title: Mercury adsorbent, process for production thereof and method of adsorptive removal of mercury
abstract: The invention provides a mercury adsorbent which makes it possible to remove mercury efficiently through adsorption from liquid hydrocarbon, exhaust combustion gas from a thermal power station, natural gas, off-gas from various processing plants, or the like which contains both mercury in various forms such as simple substance, ion, and organomercury and a coexistent substance bringing about hindrance to the adsorption of mercury; a process for production thereof; and a method of adsorptive removal of mercury. The mercury adsorbent carries 27 to 70% of potassium iodide based on the weight of the whole adsorbent and has a volume of pores having pore diameters of 1μm or above of 0.6mL/g or above.  The mercury adsorbent is filled into an adsorption column (10) or the like, and a hydrocarbon fluid is passed through the column (mercury removal step).  When the hydrocarbon fluid contains water of saturation, a dehydration unit (9) is set on the upstream side of the column (10) to conduct dehydration (dehydration step).

title: Pressure-resistant vessel
abstract: In a pressure-resistant container (1), a peripheral part (3a) of an opening (3d) of an inner shell (3) is attached to an end surface of a mouth ring (4) at an inside of the container (1) so as to cover thereof. Further, the pressure-resistant container (1) comprises a cylindrical pressing member (5) which is screwed into an inner peripheral surface (4b) of the mouth ring (4), an inner peripheral surface (b) (shown in  FIG. 3 ) of the mouth ring (4) which exposes between the inner shell (3) and the pressing member (5), a first seal (an O-ring 10 and a liquid sealant 11a) which peripherally contacts with the inner shell (3) and the pressing member (5), a valve (6) having a screw part (6b) which is screwed into the inner periphery (4c) of the mouth ring (4) at a position of the container outer than the pressing member (5), and an inner end part (6c) which is inserted in the hole part of the pressing member (5), and a second seal (an O-ring 12) to seal between an outer peripheral surface of the inner end part (6c) and the inner peripheral surface of the pressing member (5). Further, the pressure-resistant container (1) comprises a third seal (an O-ring 13).

title: Composition of a material with porous structure, method for preparing the same and use of a composition
abstract: The present application relates to adsorbent materials exhibiting suitable for use in removing contaminating compounds in dynamic regime porous structures.  Obtaining these adsorbents is performed by forming slurries prepared in aqueous medium with mixtures of coal dust and powder of natural clay with particle size itemized below 20 micrometers 90%, these materials, once formed and dried, they undergo a only heat treatment in the presence of water vapor and carbon dioxide to obtain adsorbents with broad pore size distribution.  The structure of these materials has micropores corresponding to the activated carbon obtained during the activation of the carbon and of the same mode contains mainly mesopores corresponding clays used and macropores formed from the interparticle spaces.

title: Thermally limited selective oxidation methods and apparatus
abstract: A method and apparatus (10) are provided for selectively oxidizing carbon monoxide in the presence of hydrogen gas while leaving the hydrogen substantially unoxidized. This method utilizes a catalytic material (28) which in a more oxidized state is readily reduced by carbon monoxide and which in a more reduced state is readily oxidized by air. A carbon monoxide/hydrogen gas mixture and air are alternately contacted with the catalytic material, such that the carbon monoxide is selectively oxidized and the catalytic material is regenerated.

title: Lubricating oil for inner surface of copper pipe and production of copper pipe using the same lubricating oil
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To obtain the subject lubricating oil capable of manifesting excellent lubricity and remarkably reducing residual oils after carrying out an annealing treatment by including a hydrocarbon-based polymer having an average molecular weight within a specific range and a specified structure. SOLUTION: This lubricating oil comprises an unhydrogenated hydrocarbon- based polymer which is a copolymer, represented by the formula, having 500-2,000 average molecular weight, consisting essentially of isobutylene and partially reacting n-butene therewith. A copper pipe with a reduced residual oil on the inner surface of the pipe can be obtained by coating the inner surface of the copper pipe with the lubricating oil, carrying out diameter reducing working, then replacing the atmosphere in the pipe of the copper pipe with hydrogen gas (preferably >=99.99% purity) or a mixed hydrogen gas (preferably a mixed gas containing the hydrogen gas of >=99.99% purity in a volume of 20% expressed in terms of volume ratio with a nonoxidizing gas) and performing an annealing treatment in a nonoxidizing atmosphere or a reducing atmosphere.  The resultant copper pipe is useful as a copper pipe for air conditioners and freezing or refrigerating equipment and a copper pipe for heat transfer.

title: Metallic alloy coating system and method
abstract: Tubulars are immersed in electroless nickel coating solution to coat the tubulars. Prior to the coating step the tubulars are blasted with a clean medium and washed and rinsed in alkaline solution. The tubulars are arranged in a bunk for washing, rinsing and coating. LLDPE stretch wrap applied to outer portions of the tubulars prevents coating of the outer portions. The tubulars are electrically separated from the bunk and the coating solution tank, and the tank is provided with anodic protection to prevent coating of the tank. The bunk is provided with a header assembly to provide solution flow through the tubulars via nozzles on the header assembly in addition to flow caused by the vortex effect created by velocity of fluid exiting the nozzles. The bunk is arranged in the solution tank so that the tubulars are at an angle to horizontal to efficiently remove hydrogen gas. Solution flow to the header assembly is filtered to remove particulates.

title: Gasification of plastics
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To efficiently decompose plastics into hydrogen gas. SOLUTION: This is a gasification process by allowing plastics to react with water at a temperature over 200 deg.C. A compound that liberates a halogen on the reaction is used as a reaction catalyst. As a halogen compound to be used as the catalyst in this process, a chlorine-containing plastic, hydrochloric acid, hydrogen bromide, sodium fluoride, sodium chloride and other halides, are cited.

title: Three-element solid-liquid propellant rock protection system for vehicles
abstract: The invention relates to a three-element solid-liquid propellant rock protection system for vehicles and provides a three-element solid-liquid propellant rocket protection system for vehicles, having a protection function. The system comprises an injector 1, a combustion chamber 2, a control system 3, a supply system 4, an injection pipe 5, a storage tank 6 and a parachute 7. The system is characterized in that the combustion chamber 2 is connected with the storage tank 6 through the injector 1, the combustion chamber 2 is filled with solid fuel, the control system 3 is connected with a vehicle cab control system and is connected with the supply system through a wire, and the storage tank 6 contains liquid antioxidant and liquid hydrogen. The system having the protection function has the advantages that front end and rear end collisions of vehicles can be avoided.
